# Thread: Yeast  food intolerances-any hope to maintain after hcg?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yeast-food-intolerances-any-hope-to-maintain-after-hcg.2968/>
**Subforum:** HCG Protocol
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kcaliaz — 2012-08-11T04:26:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yeast-food-intolerances-any-hope-to-maintain-after-hcg.2968/post-44254>

Sorry for the bazillion questions but I could use some advice, direction, etc.

So, I've done 2 previous rounds, lost 15-17 lbs each time but could not maintain it at all! Gained it all back plus some! Followed the P3 guidelines, but no go. I've since had labs done that have shown major yeast in my digestive tract along with an egg allergy (I never knew!! I was eating eggs daily!). My dr put me on nystatin for 3 weeks and I had significant die off symptoms, but I don't think it got rid of it in such a short period of time. Also have been taking 7keto DHEA to increase testosterone levels and a few cycles of Progesterone (oral) to see if we can jump start my body into raising my levels (it was 0.2 on day 18...yikes!). Not sure if I believe my dr on that one...can taking it a few cycles really jump start your body into making it on its own??

I have now started another hcg round and am losing slowly. I seem to have some hunger this time around which I didn't have the first 2 rounds. I'm on hucog injections...125 units...same as my previous rounds. Should I go lower, like 110, to see if it helps reduce the hunger?

And even more importantly, I want to plan out how to proceed in P3 this time around. I want to maintain!!! I'm thinking I should make sure absolutely no gluten, no eggs, no nuts. Should I give up dairy too? I don't normally have any except 2 tbps half n half in my coffee...should I give that up to, you think? Also thinking of taking garlic extract, oil of oregano and perhaps Candex to continue to fight off the yeast.

Any other things that may be a factor in not maintaining in P3?? Thanks for any suggestions!! I'm so worried I won't be able to maintain again...

### colleencoble — 2012-08-11T04:45:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yeast-food-intolerances-any-hope-to-maintain-after-hcg.2968/post-44255>

So sorry for your problems! I don't believe you can jumpstart your body into making progesterone. Your levels start plummeting in your thirties. Not sure how old you are though. If you have taken at least 4 shots, skip your shot tomorrow. If you feel better, then lower the dose. If no better, then raise it a bit. I bet it's too high though. The more rounds you do, the less you need.  I would do no grains, eggs, nuts and dairy. Try butter in your coffee. Many who gain on half and half can do the butter just fine. It tastes just like cream in your coffee. Throw it into the blender with MCT oil and it has a nice "head" on it too. So yummy! Good luck.

### Kcaliaz — 2012-08-11T18:28:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yeast-food-intolerances-any-hope-to-maintain-after-hcg.2968/post-44344>

Ok, skipped dose this morning...we'll see how I do hunger-wise.

As for eliminating foods, I'm willing to give it a try! So, no eggs, no gluten, no dairy, no nuts, no artificial sweeteners. I'll just use coconut milk in my coffee....although the butter idea sounds much better! I'll have to try that. I'm going to make up a list of meal options so I'm fully prepared for P3 and don't rely on old standby foods that I'm trying to avoid.

Worried about the progesterone issue. I am in my first cycle of no progesterone....have one more before I get another blood test. Just don't want me levels to plummet again and have that be a factor in not maintaining in P3. Maybe if I start to feel poorly again this cycle I can call my dr to get back on it. I'm in my 30's so I don't think I'll start magically producing normal levels of it again!

Colleen- do you recommend a certain calorie range once I jump into P3?

Thanks for the advice!! It's much appreciated!!

### IceBarbie — 2012-08-13T07:20:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yeast-food-intolerances-any-hope-to-maintain-after-hcg.2968/post-44646>

I just wanna toss in the idea of trying Colloidal Silver for your yeast. I've tried many things over the years at a huge cost to our family budget and CS is the best thing I have found for Candida. I take about 3 tablespoons in the morning and again before bed. Yeast is a difficult thing to get under control. I've read that it goes dormant in the body then it will start proliferating again once you eat sugars or starches. I absolutely believe in killing it--not starving it. Starving it by cutting out all fruits and sugars only makes it morph and cause leaky gut as it searches deeper in your body for sugars to feed it. I use 16 oz bottles of Silver Biotics that I get at iherb.com for $26 (about $15 less than what my health food store sells it for locally). I've read that Sovereign Silver is good too but it's pricier. I did use MesoSilver too but it didn't seem to have any effect on my Candida. Hope this helps!

### colleencoble — 2012-08-17T04:13:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yeast-food-intolerances-any-hope-to-maintain-after-hcg.2968/post-45291>

I don't believe in counting calories.  How did the skip day go with your hunger?

### Jack Kruse — 2012-08-17T04:28:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yeast-food-intolerances-any-hope-to-maintain-after-hcg.2968/post-45293>

this thread is going to like the next blog.......just saying

### Kcaliaz — 2012-08-17T05:04:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yeast-food-intolerances-any-hope-to-maintain-after-hcg.2968/post-45302>

Will be sure to read the next blog then!

Colleen-skip day went well...less hunger. So I've reduced the injection to 110. Doing better there but still some hunger. My only thought is maybe the killing yeast project at the same time is affecting my hunger levels?? I feel like I start tugging on a thread, which leads to even more threads. Thought I just had adrenal fatigue. Then discovered thyroid problems, then discovered hormone problems, then discovered a yeast overgrowth problem, then discovered food sensitivities. And through it all, weightloss has alluded me, except when im on hcg. I'm sure there's so much more going on that I just don't understand yet. And it seems my doc doesn't understand it either! I'll just keep reading, learning, asking questions. Thanks for following up with me!

### colleencoble — 2012-08-17T21:41:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yeast-food-intolerances-any-hope-to-maintain-after-hcg.2968/post-45413>

Try bumping it down just a bit more. All those hormonal things are so interconnected. Makes your head hurt.

Excited to see what you're coming up with, Doc!

### Fredricked — 2012-09-29T08:48:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yeast-food-intolerances-any-hope-to-maintain-after-hcg.2968/post-55171>

Try butter in your coffee. Many who gain on half and half can do the butter just fine. It tastes just like cream in your coffee. Throw it into the blender with MCT oil and it has a nice "head" on it too. So yummy! Good luck.

# Thread: Somehow I think the people on this forum will be ok :)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/somehow-i-think-the-people-on-this-forum-will-be-ok.2973/>
**Subforum:** The Cave

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### NWgirl — 2012-08-11T08:27:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/somehow-i-think-the-people-on-this-forum-will-be-ok.2973/post-44286>

[http://www.huffingtonpost.com/2012/08/10/us-corn-production\_n\_1763943.html](http://http://www.huffingtonpost.com/2012/08/10/us-corn-production_n_1763943.html)

### PrimalPam — 2012-08-11T18:15:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/somehow-i-think-the-people-on-this-forum-will-be-ok.2973/post-44339>

Ha! I saw this on national news last night. Beware...corn prices will rise due to drought!!! Your cereal, crackers, dog food, even meat! will be more expensive! This will cost the American consumer!

I thought, um, not me. Does.Not.Affect.Me.One.Bit.

Unless salmon have started eating corn.

### MartiD — 2012-08-11T18:24:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/somehow-i-think-the-people-on-this-forum-will-be-ok.2973/post-44343>

I'm thinking in a year or two the grass fed beef might not be so drastically higher. My husband thinks now is the time to invest in a half beef.

### Jonathin — 2012-08-11T18:55:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/somehow-i-think-the-people-on-this-forum-will-be-ok.2973/post-44353>

The price of all food will rise with the drought whether or not that food is directly impacted. Think how gasoline is manipulated. All sources of energy move in price to some degree. The idea of 'supply and demand' works as leverage for the commodity speculators to make profit.

### NWgirl — 2012-08-11T19:30:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/somehow-i-think-the-people-on-this-forum-will-be-ok.2973/post-44358>

> **Jonathin;44661 wrote:** The price of all food will rise with the drought whether or not that food is directly impacted. Think how gasoline is manipulated. All sources of energy move in price to some degree. The idea of 'supply and demand' works as leverage for the commodity speculators to make profit.

I think this is a good reason to make extra effort to support local farms and online sources of meats / fish that don't rely on corn/soy for feeding animals and where prices are more directly related to the cost of doing business. We buy 100% grass-fed beef but I am still a frequent Costco shopper for other items, especially the things that I don't eat but my family does. I've completely switched our oils to localish olive oil (small Cali farm) and wilderness family naturals coconut oil and any tallow, lard or bacon grease I can render myself.

Living in Oregon kind of sucks sometimes because of the lack of availability of raw grass-fed butter, yogurt. I buy kerrygold instead. Thank goodness we can always find the good farm fresh eggs.

The last couple years we joined a CSA so we have local veggies weekly with little effort. I hate being too busy to garden but this is a good alternative.

Progress! I have to remember 7 years ago I thought coconut oil was bad, I ate hamburger helper and fast food. A lot of good changes, and less reliability on the US food industry, yaah!

### Martin — 2012-08-13T14:02:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/somehow-i-think-the-people-on-this-forum-will-be-ok.2973/post-44659>

I saw it as very interesting news!

My brother-in-law is in Nebraska and the corn/soybean/wheat fields are parched. I try and see the opportunity in everything and this one is kinda obvious to us.

I wonder if it will inspire more diverse farming practices? More pastured meats? Awareness of what corp/gov is doing?

### kathylu — 2012-08-13T17:20:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/somehow-i-think-the-people-on-this-forum-will-be-ok.2973/post-44687>

> **Martin;44968 wrote:** I saw it as very interesting news!
>
>
>
> My brother-in-law is in Nebraska and the corn/soybean/wheat fields are parched. I try and see the opportunity in everything and this one is kinda obvious to us.
>
>
>
> I wonder if it will inspire more diverse farming practices? More pastured meats? Awareness of what corp/gov is doing?

Not a chance, unfortunately.

### shilohman — 2012-08-13T18:18:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/somehow-i-think-the-people-on-this-forum-will-be-ok.2973/post-44695>

> **PrimalPam;44647 wrote:** Ha! I saw this on national news last night. Beware...corn prices will rise due to drought!!! Your cereal, crackers, dog food, even meat! will be more expensive! This will cost the American consumer!
>
>
>
> I thought, um, not me. Does.Not.Affect.Me.One.Bit.
>
>
>
> Unless salmon have started eating corn.

Actually, farm raised salmon are fed corn/soy pellets. Sounds like the cost of farm raised will be going up. What a shame.

### MartiD — 2012-08-13T18:21:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/somehow-i-think-the-people-on-this-forum-will-be-ok.2973/post-44696>

I always avoid farm raised fish because I assumed it didn't give us the same effects of the ocean seafood. I do find it hard to find Wild Caught fish in Mid-Missouri unless it is frozen. I also read somewhere that Atlanta Salmon is always farm raised and the meat is dyed salmon colored for effect because they get that color from eating shrimp.

### PrimalPam — 2012-08-13T21:39:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/somehow-i-think-the-people-on-this-forum-will-be-ok.2973/post-44750>

> **shilohman;45004 wrote:** Actually, farm raised salmon are fed corn/soy pellets. Sounds like the cost of farm raised will be going up. What a shame.

LOL...just today, I accidentally picked up a package of salmon, threw it in my cart, before I read that it was "farm-raised". Put it back, and realized that 90% of the salmon at my local grocery is farm-raised! I did find the wild-caught Alaskan. More $, but toally worth it.

### kathylu — 2012-08-13T21:55:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/somehow-i-think-the-people-on-this-forum-will-be-ok.2973/post-44752>

> **PrimalPam;45059 wrote:** LOL...just today, I accidentally picked up a package of salmon, threw it in my cart, before I read that it was "farm-raised". Put it back, and realized that 90% of the salmon at my local grocery is farm-raised! I did find the wild-caught Alaskan. More $, but toally worth it.

Didn't Jack say that the worst fish is better than the best grass fed beef? So, I always look for wild fish, but will eat farmed (from the US only) if that's what they have.

### Raiken3712 — 2012-08-14T00:36:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/somehow-i-think-the-people-on-this-forum-will-be-ok.2973/post-44780>

I'd love to see some nutritional comparisons...of course it would vary based on freshness and some other factors...but seeing some numbers would be cool

Differences between fresh pastured/fresh feedlot, fresh wild caught/fresh farmed, month old pastured/month old feedlot etc... etc...

I like numbers too much . There are obviously other factors too...wild caught in Alaska might have different nutrient profiles than wild caught in Maine.

All I've seen are guesses about extra nutrition I haven't seen any actual analysis.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-08-14T02:37:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/somehow-i-think-the-people-on-this-forum-will-be-ok.2973/post-44797>

USDA has boatloads of them on their website. Massive amounts in fact.

### Raiken3712 — 2012-08-14T02:46:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/somehow-i-think-the-people-on-this-forum-will-be-ok.2973/post-44798>

The USDA as in the government has boatloads of nutritional comparison on their site? I thought they favored Vegan/Vegetarian with that recent Meatless Monday stunt...I guess that wasn't the USDA as a whole though....I'm gonna have to look that up. Guess I didn't try hard enough.

### PrimalPam — 2012-08-14T02:50:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/somehow-i-think-the-people-on-this-forum-will-be-ok.2973/post-44801>

> **kathylu;45061 wrote:** Didn't Jack say that the worst fish is better than the best grass fed beef? So, I always look for wild fish, but will eat farmed (from the US only) if that's what they have.

Yes, Dr. Kruse has said this many times. (Other than canned tuna, the spam of the sea).

So while I always look for and prefer wild-caught, I don't ask in restaurants where the fish were swimming the day they were caught. I just eat them instead of the plate of pasta I used to order.

### MartiD — 2012-08-14T17:56:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/somehow-i-think-the-people-on-this-forum-will-be-ok.2973/post-44905>

I bought two filets of salmon yesterday out of the fresh fish counter at the grocery store. It didn't say whether it was farm raised or fresh but I am assuming farm. It was good either way. At our local Sam's we were able to buy a large piece of fresh (not frozen)Salmon that stated it was wild caught. We were able to divide it into 4 servings and it was really good too. I do think there is a destinctive taste difference between the farm and wild as well as the frozen and fresh. Being in the middle of America I will take what I can get and afford!

# Thread: Anyone ever heard or done this form of 'mini CT'?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-ever-heard-or-done-this-form-of-mini-ct.2974/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### NWgirl — 2012-08-11T09:02:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-ever-heard-or-done-this-form-of-mini-ct.2974/post-44287>

My naturopath told me that on nights that I don't do a CT bath to try this method: wet a pair of cotton socks until soaking and wring them out, but just enough so they're not dripping. Put on the wet socks and then put another pair of dry socks over the wet socks, then go to bed.

I did this last night, not sure what it's doing but I like the idea of doing mini CT while sleeping.

### Raiken3712 — 2012-08-11T18:15:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-ever-heard-or-done-this-form-of-mini-ct.2974/post-44338>

Never heard of it but I can see how this might be helpful. If the socks are cold enough to elicit a response from your body than your body will try to warm your hands.

Since its just your hands it may not do much but it couldn't hurt. Or at least it seems fine to me...not sure how useful that is though.

### NWgirl — 2012-08-11T19:09:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-ever-heard-or-done-this-form-of-mini-ct.2974/post-44354>

The funny thing is the socks are completely dry by about 3am even though they're covered up. I didn't expect that!

# Thread: Hi from New York
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-new-york.2980/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bonniesherbals — 2012-08-11T17:39:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-new-york.2980/post-44332>

Hi everyone, just wanted to introduce myself. I'm Bonnie Rogers and I'm an herbalist and a wellness coach. I'm so excited to have found this forum as so much of what Dr Kruse mentions is what I recommend.

Our bodies were designed to be alkaline, however with the common diet people are eating and the stress that abounds in our world most of our bodies are acidic and filled with disease. My goal is to assist people in making small shifts that create powerful changes in their bodies. By just changing one small thing miracles can happen.

I want people to feel comfortable in their bodies, it doesn't matter if you are thin or overweight, small or large you should feel comfortable in who you are and learn how to release what doesn't serve you. Every Body is Beautiful ([http://wheredidmybodygo.com](http://http://wheredidmybodygo.com)) is the name of a workshop that I am presenting with a few other women to allow women to learn to release old messaging.

I'm so excited to learn from this forum ways that people are working to make a difference in their own health, the health of their families and the health of their communites.

### caroline — 2012-08-12T05:40:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-new-york.2980/post-44471>

Welcome to the forum - lots to learn!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-08-12T15:45:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/based-upon-twitter-feed-from-ahs-2012.2986/post-44513>

> **kathylu;44813 wrote:** Can't wait to see the vimeo recordings. It will be very interesting to see if the paleo celebs are starting to change their tune.

doubtful. dogma is rough to change. Did you see Kresser latest post......one of m,y doctor buds was at AHS12 and said Rosedale destroyed Jaminet and Kresser.........read his post and you'd think it was a walk in the park. I love difference of opinion but starches are not safe except when light cycles are longer......Rosedale believes they are never safe......I am not that extreme but I understand his point. if you get mitochondrial science well you can see why he believes starches are mitochondrial toxins.......due to ROS at cytochrome one. The paleo crowd does not want to look at that data because they are enormed with potato's and banana's to fuel their crossfit work outs.

### kathylu — 2012-08-12T17:06:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/based-upon-twitter-feed-from-ahs-2012.2986/post-44532>

> **Jack Kruse;44821 wrote:** doubtful. dogma is rough to change. Did you see Kresser latest post......one of m,y doctor buds was at AHS12 and said Rosedale destroyed Jaminet and Kresser.........read his post and you'd think it was a walk in the park. I love difference of opinion but starches are not safe except when light cycles are longer......Rosedale believes they are never safe......I am not that extreme but I understand his point. if you get mitochondrial science well you can see why he believes starches are mitochondrial toxins.......due to ROS at cytochrome one. The paleo crowd does not want to look at that data because they are enormed with potato's and banana's to fuel their crossfit work outs.

They just don't understand the biochemistry. Few do. If Guyenet, Jaminet and Kresser would spend some time at Peter's blog (hyperlipid) they would get a lot smarter a lot faster, but they all let their personalities get in the way. Rosedale is great, but I still don't see where he arrived at his opinions on the mTOR pathway.

By the way, would you consider doing a blog on metabolic flexibility at the mitochondrial level? I'd be really interested in your take on the subject. I've thought for a long time that obese people have wildly dysfunctional mitochondria, impaired ability to use fat as fuel, increased ROS (which kills mitochondria), just a vicious cycle.

I don't think anybody has hit on what went wrong and how in the first place - we are all still dealing with what is happening in the overfat body right now. Also, mostly in those who's balances went awry and started an negative cascade breaking more things slowly over time (thus slow/steady aging weight gain).

# Thread: Fermented sauerkraut  dill pickles...questions
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fermented-sauerkraut-dill-pickles-questions.2987/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 31
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PrimalPam — 2012-08-11T23:18:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fermented-sauerkraut-dill-pickles-questions.2987/post-44396>

Sauerkraut...good, I know. But is Claussen's from the grocery store ok? Or is there a brand/type to look for?

Dill pickles...same question.

### NWgirl — 2012-08-11T23:40:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fermented-sauerkraut-dill-pickles-questions.2987/post-44404>

The only brand of real sauerkraut (raw) with good bacteria that I know of is Bubbie's. It's pretty good but I prefer making my own as its inexpensive.

I have the recipe on one of my blogs: [http://thenourishingcook.com/sauerkraut-winters-miracle-vegetable-salad/](http://http://thenourishingcook.com/sauerkraut-winters-miracle-vegetable-salad/)

Let me know if you have questions, I have been fermenting veggies for awhile now.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-08-12T00:02:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fermented-sauerkraut-dill-pickles-questions.2987/post-44408>

Don't get anything that has vinegar in it for pickles. You want it fermented not vinegared! Bubbies pickles I;m pretty sure are made without vinegar.

### PrimalPam — 2012-08-12T00:06:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fermented-sauerkraut-dill-pickles-questions.2987/post-44410>

Oh thanks...Bubbies it is!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fermented-sauerkraut-dill-pickles-questions.2987/post-44431>

How can you tell if something still has beneficial bacteria intact? If you start to digest better? Any easier way to know?

> Let me know if you have questions, I have been fermenting veggies for awhile now.

I'm totally checking your blog out! I am just attempting to ferment salsa and pickles for the first time today!

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-12T05:08:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fermented-sauerkraut-dill-pickles-questions.2987/post-44462>

Round these parts, we are lucky - there are some local producers selling in stores like Whole Foods and Natural Grocers, the problem is they are like $10 a jar. I am hoping to read my new book on fermenting while on vacation!

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-12T07:59:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fermented-sauerkraut-dill-pickles-questions.2987/post-44489>

> **Patty Cakes;44716 wrote:** Don't get anything that has vinegar in it for pickles. You want it fermented not vinegared! Bubbies pickles I;m pretty sure are made without vinegar.
>
>
> Without vinegar but pasteurized or flash pasteurized. I wrote to them a while ago and they had to start flash pasteurizing b/c the jars were bubbling over and the stores complained about messes. Here's the answer:
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> Thank you very much for writing in to Bubbies, we are always pleased to hear from one of our customers and we would be delighted to tell you more about our food products in regards to whether they are raw, heated, or pasteurized.
>
>
>
> Our Pure Kosher Dills, Dill Relish, Pickled Green Tomatoes and Sauerkraut are all naturally fermented and cured in salt water brine using a lacto-fermentation process. These products contain live cultures and the enzymes that form from a natural fermentation.
>
>
>
> The Pure Kosher Dills, Dill Relish and Pickled Green Tomatoes are 100% raw; the Sauerkraut in the jars has been flash heated but not pasteurized. This means that the sauerkraut is neither pasteurized nor raw. We were forced to begin heating our jarred Sauerkraut to calm the cultures inside because they were causing the kraut to continue to ferment too much in turn causing a buildup of gas that then results in brine leaking all over our distributor's and retailer's equipment and shelving.
>
>
>
> When we heat our jarred Sauerkraut, it is quickly raised to about 135-140 degrees and then sealed in the jars. The goal here is not to eliminate all the beneficial cultures, but rather to stifle them so they won't cause the jars to leak. While the heating we do for our Sauerkraut is only intended to calm the gas producing nature of the product with the specific goal in mind not to eliminate the beneficial cultures. We do not claim that this product is raw for these reasons, but it still does have live bacteria. From our testing, it is above 140 degrees that you really begin to eliminate the cultures present in our products on a massive scale.
>
>
>
> It is important to note that our Sauerkraut is very crisp. This is because there are still vegetable fibers left intact in the cabbage which are the complex carbohydrates that break down into the simpler food that the lacto bacillus cultures feed on during the fermentation process.

### kathylu — 2012-08-12T14:33:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fermented-sauerkraut-dill-pickles-questions.2987/post-44500>

The salt in Bubbie's is way too high for me. I make my own kraut with 1 tbsp of celtic sea salt per 1/2 gallon, much less salty and tastier I think! I did start my original batch using 1/4 cup of whey (from home made yogurt), but since then I just start a new batch with 1/4 cup of juice from the previous batch. You can also experiment with adding other veggies to the mix, shredded carrot, sweet or hot peppers, garlic, green onion, a little pickling spice, cubed daikon, jicama, cauliflower, broccoli etc. It's all good.

I also pour a glass of kraut juice occasionally and just drink it! It's cloudy with yummy good bacteria. Just top off the kraut with some filtered water (and a little salt if you like, but I don't) and pop it back in the fridge.

### PrimalPam — 2012-08-12T16:10:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fermented-sauerkraut-dill-pickles-questions.2987/post-44519>

And thanks for the kraut recipes NW Girl and kathylu. One of these days, when I get adventurous...of course, this coming from someone who spent decades microwaving Lean Cuisines.

### [email redacted] — 2012-09-03T21:54:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fermented-sauerkraut-dill-pickles-questions.2987/post-49218>

Do I have to put homemade fermented veggies in the fridge after a few days and once the jar is open? Can I take some out and still leave them on the counter until they're gone?

### NWgirl — 2012-09-03T23:03:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fermented-sauerkraut-dill-pickles-questions.2987/post-49234>

> **Kath;49722 wrote:** Do I have to put homemade fermented veggies in the fridge after a few days and once the jar is open? Can I take some out and still leave them on the counter until they're gone?

After the initial fermentation they should be kept in the fridge. They will over ferment on the counter pretty quickly after that.

### [email redacted] — 2012-09-03T23:06:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fermented-sauerkraut-dill-pickles-questions.2987/post-49235>

Good to know-thanks.

### BJK77 — 2012-08-13T20:37:03+0300

I made salmon jerky before using Red Boat fish sauce - [http://redboatfishsauce.com/faq.html](http://http://redboatfishsauce.com/faq.html). It's the real deal. Good stuff!

### Michael — 2012-08-13T21:03:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-jerky.2989/post-44740>

> **Souldanzer;44726 wrote:** I've been making fish jerky

Dried fish? That's stockfish.

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stockfish](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stockfish)

I think it used to be quite common in Scandinavia.

Dried salt-cod was also much used in Southern Europe -- i.e. in Roman Catholic countries -- in places like Portugal, as "fasting" or Lenten food. I believe sometimes nunneries would put the fish in a running stream for hours at a time in order to wash the salt out before cooking it. You could probably find recipes for using something like that under the name bacalao or bacalhau or morue.

Soaked dried salted cod is also used in traditional West-indian dishes. Tried to find an example by googling and came up with this, which is stated here to be Jamaica's national dish:

[http://eatjamaican.com/recipes/ackeeandsaltfish-recipe.html](http://http://eatjamaican.com/recipes/ackeeandsaltfish-recipe.html)

I've no idea what ackee is.

### Qodesh — 2012-08-16T19:45:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-jerky.2989/post-45224>

Stockfish is unsalted fish, especially cod, dried by cold air and wind on wooden racks on the foreshore, called "hjell". The drying of food is the world's oldest known preservation method, and dried fish has a storage life of several years. The method is cheap and effective in suitable climates; the work can be done by the fisherman and family, and the resulting product is easily transported to market.

Stockfish is cured in a process called fermentation where cold adapted bacteria matures the fish, similar to the maturing process of cheese.

ah, thanks!

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-16T20:03:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-jerky.2989/post-45227>

Thx everybody. I don't use salt to preserve my fish though before drying it. It works well without it though but tastes better with some spicing.

I'm going to venture into low temp dried squid today.... Wish me luck lol. The stuff at the Asian store looks yummy but has lots of MSG added. I've also only dried salmon and smelts so far... Going to try some other fish with this batch as well. Maybe cod? Or tuna?

Dried fish, especially the salmon bc its fatty appears to make great ready food and food on the go if needed.

# Thread: Opinions on gradual increase schedule of hormones
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/opinions-on-gradual-increase-schedule-of-hormones.2990/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### tweety — 2012-08-12T01:50:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/opinions-on-gradual-increase-schedule-of-hormones.2990/post-44421>

So I am currently on 50mg pregnenolone, 50mg DHEA and 60mg thyroid. I have orders from my doc to increase my NDT by 30g every two weeks. I would also like to increase DHEA and pregnenolone. But do you have ideas or recommendations on how to space out the increments? I'm thinking in the weeks I am not increasing thyroid I could increase either the DHEA or pregnenolone. Is that too much too fast? Should I wait until I am finished increasing thyroid until I tinker w the other two? Also, which would you increase first- DHEA or pregnenolone? And why? I'm horribly low in all!

Thanks

### tweety — 2012-08-12T03:09:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/opinions-on-gradual-increase-schedule-of-hormones.2990/post-44435>

Well, my doc wanted me on 5mg DHEA. I told her I was already on 50mg. She did recommend 50mg pregnenolone, but I have a long way to go.

I know what levels I want to increase too, just curious about how slowly to increase. I want to give each time to work, and not get myself in too deep too fast.

# Thread: Hi from Queensland, Oz
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-queensland-oz.2991/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TribalSpice — 2012-08-12T02:14:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-queensland-oz.2991/post-44425>

Hi,

Obviously I am a newie here. I am really interested in the information about leptin and cold thermogenesis.

I have a few questions.

First off, I am female 169cm tall and 58kg, I have maintain this weight since I was a teenager (barring my 3 pregnancies of course where I went up to the mid to high 60's) and am in pretty good health. The last time I was actually sick was back in 2003. I believe in healthiness as a journey and will continue to strive towards it. I realise I have some level of leptin resistance as I do sometimes crave carbs (see below).

So now to the questions.

About 5 yrs ago I started getting headaches, not too bad, but often. I had looked at all possible reasons, dehydration, upper cervical subluxation etc. About 12 months ago I read about delaying breakfast so that the over night fast is at least 15-18hrs and getting outside first thing in the morning to stimulate production of DHEA. Within a week the headaches disappeared. The way it was explained to me about why delay breakfast was that the likelihood of our ancestors eating breakfast was unlikely because stored food from the hunt the night before would have attracted predators and put the tribe at risk of attack. This would mean having to hunt first, eat later. The other part of it is emptying the tank before you refill it. For me, I realised I was never actually hungry in the mornings anyway. I ate breakfast because it was the "healthiest" thing to do. Once I started listening to my body signals for hunger I found that I was only ever actually hungry at around 11am and 6pm. I also notice that if I eat breakfast, which was always a low carb, higher protein/good fats meal, I would crave food all day long, mostly sweet things. Dropping breakfast completely stopped this. Obviously this sits very differently to the leptin Rx, but I am guessing the longer fast has also improved leptin sensitivity. Am I the exception because my body composition is already different?

I have other questions, but I think I will just start with that.

Thanks

Spicey

### Jack Kruse — 2012-08-12T02:29:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-queensland-oz.2991/post-44428>

all depends upon your physiology and labs. Welcome.

### caroline — 2012-08-12T05:38:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-queensland-oz.2991/post-44469>

Welcome from N.S.W. enjoy the forum - so much to read and learn!

### TribalSpice — 2012-08-12T22:44:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-queensland-oz.2991/post-44570>

Thanks for the Welcomes  Yay, another Aussie.

I am really excited to be here, the information I have been reading has completely tickled my inner geek. I guess I should ask my questions out on the other parts of the forums.

### shilohman — 2012-08-13T00:03:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-queensland-oz.2991/post-44582>

Welcome, you probably should start reading Dr. K's blogs, there a lot, but many of your questions will be answered there. I would start with reading the THE LEPTIN RX...FAQ's. Have fun!

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-29T10:04:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-queensland-oz.2991/post-48034>

Hi there, your situation has a lot of validity. If I follow when I am hungry it is also around 11am then 6pm. I will be interested to know the answer to this. I'm finding it difficult eating enough protein within half an hour of rising. I must say am feeling much more energetic though!

### nonchalant — 2012-08-29T17:32:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-queensland-oz.2991/post-48087>

> **TribalSpice;44733 wrote:** Hi,
>
> I also notice that if I eat breakfast, which was always a low carb, higher protein/good fats meal, I would crave food all day long, mostly sweet things. Dropping breakfast completely stopped this.
>
> Thanks
>
> Spicey

Hi Spicey. I think you should eat a 0 carb breakfast so that it doesn't trigger cravings. But if even that amount you still get cravings, you may have to just wait it out. The LR helped me fight my cravings, and then they diminished greatly with CT. The thing that finally stopped all lingering cravings was eating fish every day. My body seemed to get completely satisfied with that, and cravings evaporated.

# Thread: Bone broth websites links wanted

**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Raiken3712 — 2012-08-12T02:44:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth-websites-links-wanted.2992/post-44433>

So there are tons of websites out there with bone broth recipes...anyone have any recommendations on good ones? Also I plan to get more but only have one pound of bone at the moment...is this enough to make some broth?

I've heard vegetables and other things to put in the broth...would it not be too bad if I just used ACV and Real Salt? That's all you need right? All the veggies and such are optional?

Thanks

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-12T04:02:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth-websites-links-wanted.2992/post-44443>

> **Raiken3712;44741 wrote:** So there are tons of websites out there with bone broth recipes...anyone have any recommendations on good ones? Also I plan to get more but only have one pound of bone at the moment...is this enough to make some broth?
>
>
>
> I've heard vegetables and other things to put in the broth...would it not be too bad if I just used ACV and Real Salt? That's all you need right? All the veggies and such are optional?
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> Thanks

Yup! I'm not sure the veggies add any nutrients. . I'm not even sure their flavor stays after boiling for days.

I've made some with just ACV and bones. Don't sting on the legnth of boil. With just bones go for 72 hrs minimum.

In the olden days, they used to keep a pot of bones boiling constantly, just adding new water and bones now and then.

Stick it all in the crockpot, add water as needed, and keep it going!

### Raiken3712 — 2012-08-12T05:12:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth-websites-links-wanted.2992/post-44464>

It doesn't boil for days technically from what I remember on seeing directions before...not that it matters. After some set amount of time you lower it to a simmer for the rest of the time. Boiling for days would leave nothing at all .

I'm pretty sure you didn't actually mean boil for days just messin around.

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-12T05:16:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth-websites-links-wanted.2992/post-44466>

Correct! low simmer for days just doesn't have the same joi de vevre!

# Thread: how much food ?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-food.2994/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### somewhatchubby — 2012-08-12T10:04:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-food.2994/post-44491>

morning all, after surprsingly getting my husband on board on the leptin thingy. I am amazed at how much he can eat at the crack of dawn. This morning ,humungous pork chop , three large rashers back bacon and three eggs. I would have popped a gasket after that much ,is he taking it too far or is it ok for a big guy to consume so much.? I havnt tracked it yet but I would guess easily 70 gms protein.

This morning I only managed to eat 39 gms , often I get over 50 gms but I feel sliglthy nauseated afterwards ,although this passes after an hour or so. Any other ladies struggling with this amount of food in the am ?

Also ,any ideas apart from eggs and bacon, canned fish patties. Sometimes I really need a change. Is full fat cottage cheese ok ? I find I can eat any amout of that.

thanks

### ealachan — 2012-08-12T12:33:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-food.2994/post-44494>

> **somewhatchubby;44799 wrote:** morning all, after surprsingly getting my husband on board on the leptin thingy. I am amazed at how much he can eat at the crack of dawn. This morning ,humungous pork chop , three large rashers back bacon and three eggs. I would have popped a gasket after that much ,is he taking it too far or is it ok for a big guy to consume so much.? I havnt tracked it yet but I would guess easily 70 gms protein.
>
> This morning I only managed to eat 39 gms , often I get over 50 gms but I feel sliglthy nauseated afterwards ,although this passes after an hour or so. Any other ladies struggling with this amount of food in the am ?
>
> Also ,any ideas apart from eggs and bacon, canned fish patties. Sometimes I really need a change. Is full fat cottage cheese ok ? I find I can eat any amout of that.
>
> thanks

The Rx guidelines say to eat at least 50g (and up to 70g if you're big / hungry) of protein every morning. If you add it all up, I bet your husband is falling within that range. One of the things I found surprising when I started the Rx was the sheer amount of food I needed to make up 50g of protein. Bacon is lovely, but it doesn't have a whole lot of protein per slice (although UK bacon has more than US bacon because yours isn't "streaky" like ours is. I miss UK bacon!). Eggs are 6-7 grams protein each. The pork chop is hard to tell without weighing it.

I've never had a problem feeling nauseated after a big protein breakfast, but I used to get wicked heartburn and/or just feel totally stuffed and sluggish. That partially wore off with time, and it also helped me to start taking an enzyme and an ox bile supplement. I don't have a gallbladder anymore, so things weren't working correctly in my guts to begin with, and the enzyme and bile supps really helped a lot. Might be worth giving them a try?

In terms of what to eat when you need a change, I like to do steak sometimes, chicken or pork sausage (make sure it's not full of sugar and/or MSG), canned salmon mashed up and scrambled with eggs, or anything I might have left over from dinner the night before (shrimp curry is fabulous after spending the night in the fridge). I would be careful with eating large amounts of cottage cheese, as it's a lot of dairy all at once and may possibly do bad things to your blood sugar.

Hope that helps!

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-12T16:30:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-food.2994/post-44521>

It's very diffeent from a person to another. I started the reset to stop the cravings and the binging, and I had to have 70 to 80g of protein! If I had "only" 50g, I would be hungry at night and you want to avoid that. You might have to try different amount of protein and see how you feel later that day.

I, too, was feeling a little nauseous when I started, but it quickly went away. Make sure you have plenty of fat with your BAB. To me, fat is more filling than protein. Make sure you have good quality bacon, CO, butter or coconut milk.

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-13T00:04:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-food.2994/post-44583>

Once I finally let go of my fear of food in the morning I went beyond 80grams lots of days..... I was hungry so I ate til done. My tummy sometimes wasn't so happy about it.

### bigknitwit — 2012-08-13T01:14:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-food.2994/post-44595>

I still don't ever feel like not eating anymore (but do feel like my stomach is full), often after 100+ grams of protein and fat (1500+ calories, tracked it once because I was curious). Often I finish my gigantic meal (1/2 pound fish, 1/4 burger too some mornings, plus the 1/4 cup or more coconut oil), and feel completely disappointed that I need to be done eating... Obviously my hormone satiety signalling is completely broken...

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-13T01:46:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-food.2994/post-44604>

> **bigknitwit;44904 wrote:** I still don't ever feel like not eating anymore (but do feel like my stomach is full), often after 100+ grams of protein and fat (1500+ calories, tracked it once because I was curious). Often I finish my gigantic meal (1/2 pound fish, 1/4 burger too some mornings, plus the 1/4 cup or more coconut oil), and feel completely disappointed that I need to be done eating... Obviously my hormone satiety signalling is completely broken...

I had exactly that for the longest time during re-feeding. Still have it sometimes. It's improving for me I think.

### bigknitwit — 2012-08-13T01:58:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-food.2994/post-44605>

I still shudder to think of the amount of food I packed in last Thanksgiving (before finding JK info), while I was intermittent fasting. I had fasted for 1.5 days before the big meal (made by me, all my favorite Thanksgiving foods). I was sooo stuffed, and yet everything still looked so appetizing. I am looking forward to making progress such that I can eat and food gradually loses its appeal as the meal progresses and finishes.

> **Souldanzer;44913 wrote:** I had exactly that for the longest time during re-feeding. Still have it sometimes. It's improving for me I think.

### primalminx — 2012-08-13T21:08:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-food.2994/post-44741>

I have found that if I eat in the morning, I am basically starved all day and can eat all day, this is not a good thing at all. Is doing a big protein shake ok?

### somewhatchubby — 2012-08-16T21:19:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-food.2994/post-45237>

Hi Ealachan, yes, that helps very much, thanks. I generally get over 5ogms but sometimes I feel less hungry. I ate at 6am this morning and didnt eat again until nearly 3 pm ,my stomach was growling a bit by then but I only ate a little.Tonight I could barely finish my dinner, which was shepherds pie, cauiflower mash and some broccoli. I ate the meat first !

I dont seem to have any carb cravings at all. I didnt eat a lot of them beforer I adopted this WOE.

Its true we're all different.

I was frustrated yesterday at the weekly weigh in that for two consecuative weeks I have put on 1 lb and now am back up to 11 1/2 stone where I started. I'm pressing on as I have felt much better, more energy and clearer headed.I'd really like to lose the 14lbs I feel is superfluous though.

### somewhatchubby — 2012-08-16T21:23:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-food.2994/post-45239>

AbetterME ,somtimes its the look of the food ,after I start to eat I am usually ok until I'm nearly finished. Sometimes I feel nauseaous for the best part of an hour and then it fades.

I have a lifetime of eating breakfast around 10.30 after the cows were milked. its a hard habit to break.

### somewhatchubby — 2012-08-16T21:25:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-food.2994/post-45241>

bigknitwig ,the less I eat the smaller my appetite gets. I suppose thats good ?

I'm generally around 1500 cals and I think someone is going to tell me its not enough ?

Good? Bad? Indifferent?

Talking about the no sugar added varieties.

You see it everywhere now, but every can I look at says it has lots of carbs but zero fat. So the good fat in the coconut isn't expressed? Looks to me like just a carb-y drink.

For a while, I was buying the pulp version, which is delicious, but realized it has too many carbs and not enough other good stuff for me probably. Opinions?

### Jack Kruse — 2012-08-13T01:26:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-water.3001/post-44599>

good.....high in potassium, low in sodium and high in sugar.......and can be used to replace an IV solution in trauma. Has its place in the world.

### PrimalPam — 2012-08-13T01:40:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-water.3001/post-44602>

### ealachan — 2012-08-13T20:24:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-water.3001/post-44726>

### Endless — 2012-08-20T06:46:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-water.3001/post-45819>

### Shijin13 — 2012-08-20T14:42:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-water.3001/post-45831>

OMG coconut water is awesome I can't keep that stuff around its so damn good. Must be from all that time spent in the tropics.

Did you know that coconut water can be substituted as plasma for a blood transfusion. In WW2 they used it in the pacific theater when the medical corps ran out of plasma during extended battles on the pacific islands. Learned about that at the War in the Pacific museum in Guam !!!

That's also where I began my love affair w/the drink!!!

# Thread: Thyroid and BP
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-and-bp.3003/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 25
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Destiny — 2012-08-13T04:00:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-and-bp.3003/post-44618>

I have a question in regards to Cytomel and BP. I started Cytomel a week ago. My evening BP goes to 104/64 in last couple of days. I do feel a bit dizzy. I expected my BP to go up not down. Is this normal? Any ideas?

### bigknitwit — 2012-08-13T16:59:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-and-bp.3003/post-44683>

Ughh - I've just been through literally a few months of this, and I can tell you it was really really difficult. Although my thyroid labs looked reasonable, my blood pressure just crashed and my heartrate went through the roof, and yes, there was lots of dizziness. Eventually I became almost non-functional - feeling like there wasn't enough blood getting into my brain. My worst symptoms were in the mornings, when BP would drop to 90ish/58ish, and heartrate would be above 100 resting. I felt very unwell. CT helped dramatically, but I found myself living in the cold to maintain some level of functionality (raised BP, dropped the heartrate). I had been adjusting my Armour dose upwards and wasn't tolerating the T3 very well, probably due to adrenal issues, or inadequate ferritin (at least that's what I'm hypothesizing). Finally, last week, I gave up, and returned to T4 only therapy. It took about 4 days for things start improving, but now, 6 days in, I am stabilizing. I will try again with T3 once I feel I've fully stabilized.

When do you take the T3? How much? Do you take T4 as well? Have you taken T3 before? How is your adrenal function, and iron levels? You may need to drop the dose lower and ease in slowly. It sounds like your adrenals are not tolerating the increased metabolic rate brought on by the T3 boost, but I'll be very interested to hear what others have to say, having just gone through this at the extreme.

### chocolate — 2012-08-13T20:58:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-and-bp.3003/post-44738>

[http://www.healthboards.com/boards/thyroid-disorders/596343-critically-low-potassium-blood-pressure-drop.html](http://http://www.healthboards.com/boards/thyroid-disorders/596343-critically-low-potassium-blood-pressure-drop.html)

My daughter and I are hyper t and get those symptoms when we are low in potassium. Some doctors think you are high in sodium, when really you are low in potassium.

### Destiny — 2012-08-14T02:17:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-and-bp.3003/post-44790>

> **bigknitwit;44992 wrote:** Ughh - I've just been through literally a few months of this, and I can tell you it was really really difficult. Although my thyroid labs looked reasonable, my blood pressure just crashed and my heartrate went through the roof, and yes, there was lots of dizziness. Eventually I became almost non-functional - feeling like there wasn't enough blood getting into my brain. My worst symptoms were in the mornings, when BP would drop to 90ish/58ish, and heartrate would be above 100 resting. I felt very unwell. CT helped dramatically, but I found myself living in the cold to maintain some level of functionality (raised BP, dropped the heartrate). I had been adjusting my Armour dose upwards and wasn't tolerating the T3 very well, probably due to adrenal issues, or inadequate ferritin (at least that's what I'm hypothesizing). Finally, last week, I gave up, and returned to T4 only therapy. It took about 4 days for things start improving, but now, 6 days in, I am stabilizing. I will try again with T3 once I feel I've fully stabilized.
>
>
>
> When do you take the T3? How much? Do you take T4 as well? Have you taken T3 before? How is your adrenal function, and iron levels? You may need to drop the dose lower and ease in slowly. It sounds like your adrenals are not tolerating the increased metabolic rate brought on by the T3 boost, but I'll be very interested to hear what others have to say, having just gone through this at the extreme.

You are right, I skipped the third dosage today that I added 3 days ago and I feel normal and more energetic again. I think I need to wait for a couple of weeks before going back on 3 doses.

I just take 6.25 mg in the morning and afternoon. I've never took any T4. My adrenals are not great but OK. Iron and ferritin is lowish, working on it!

### Patty Cakes — 2012-08-14T03:00:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-and-bp.3003/post-44805>

I learned when I started on T3 only meds that I need to take my BP and HR every hour to monitor what my body is doing. In the beginning my experience was that it increased my HR and BP about an hour after taking it and then it slowly lowered. I recently changed brands and it had a slightly different effect with lowered BP and normal HR. Fillers maybe? I heard people do have different reactions with different brands or generic versions.That is probably why it was recommended to me that I take vitals often. The BP will start to go up slightly when I need a dose. I have been on T3 only for 4-1/2 months now and still learning my optimal dose based on symptoms and what my body is doing. I raised the dose about every 7-14 days. I only have low adrenals and it sounds like you have a couple of things working against you. You will have to raise slowwwwwly. It's a process and takes time but you will make it through it.

> **Patty Cakes;45117 wrote:** I learned when I started on T3 only meds that I need to take my BP and HR every hour to monitor what my body is doing. I raised the dose about every 7-14 days. I only have low adrenals and it sounds like you have a couple of things working against you. You will have to raise slowwwwwly. It's a process and takes time but you will make it through it.

Patty, I have a bunch of questions if you don't mind. You have been on T3 for a while. Is there any limit for staying on it? What is the average time for you between doses? How many doses a day do you take? What was your starting daily dosage. What is your dosage now?

I read on thyromadness that Reverse T3 might get cleared up in 8 weeks. Then if the T3/RT3 ratio is good, is it a good idea to switch to natural des. thyroid?

### Patty Cakes — 2012-08-14T05:27:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-and-bp.3003/post-44850>

Is there any limit for staying on it?

My understanding is that some people are on it for life. Some people cannot tolerate T4.

What is the average time for you between doses?

I take a dose at 3:30am (a slightly bigger dose), then one at 10, one at 3pm and I try to get another one in the evening or I just die at night! Some people, due to reasons I cannot remember, take a dose every three hours but I think that is rare.

What was your starting daily dosage? I was on 150 mg of Natural Thyroid when I switched to T3 so my starting dose will be different from yours. I started with 12.5 in the morning and two other doses of 6.25 each.

What is your dosage now? Now I take 72 mcg.

I believe it can take 8-12 weeks for rT3 to clear. Mine is clear now and my ratio is 44. Woo-hoo!!! So excited! I think the choice of T3 vs NDT is an individual one. I actually feel better on T3 in some ways and in other ways better on NDT. I'm still not sure what to do.

### bigknitwit — 2012-08-14T15:15:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-and-bp.3003/post-44878>

May I jump in and ask a question? What is the reason to move to T3 only meds? To clear out elevated reverse T3? How do you go about figuring out why your got the elevated RT3?

I read somewhere that the hyper-like symptoms I was experiencing are more likely due to unsufficient cortisol levels. My hypothesis is that the body shunts everything it's got into making cortisol, and then falls short in trying to make other hormones, like aldosterone (which regulates electrolytes and blood pressure). Also, I read that supplementing sea salt helps because the body has a hard time holding onto it. I know I can't seem to eat enough salt.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-08-14T18:01:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-and-bp.3003/post-44907>

> **bigknitwit;45200 wrote:** May I jump in and ask a question? What is the reason to move to T3 only meds? To clear out elevated reverse T3? How do you go about figuring out why your got the elevated RT3?
>
>
>
> I read somewhere that the hyper-like symptoms I was experiencing are more likely due to unsufficient cortisol levels. My hypothesis is that the body shunts everything it's got into making cortisol, and then falls short in trying to make other hormones, like aldosterone (which regulates electrolytes and blood pressure). Also, I read that supplementing sea salt helps because the body has a hard time holding onto it. I know I can't seem to eat enough salt.

T3 is the med of choice for clearing out the rT3. That is not the only reason to take it though. Some people cannot tolerate T4. Or some docs like to script T3 + T4, all synthetic. I'm sure there are other reasons for it too!

Testing is the best way to know if your aldosterone and renin need help. I believe that sea salt water does help with the aldosterone but I'm not positive. This is where the ASI test comes in. If you are on thyroid meds you can get hyper or hypo symptoms from either thyroid or from the cortisol. Always test to know for sure. I've seen people get the shakes with such low cortisol that the body couldn't handle the thyroid meds even though they were on a very low dose. Adrenals should always be fixed first....I learned this too late!

### Lyndra — 2012-08-14T23:49:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-and-bp.3003/post-44951>

> **bigknitwit;45200 wrote:**
>
>
>
> I read somewhere that the hyper-like symptoms I was experiencing are more likely due to unsufficient cortisol levels. My hypothesis is that the body shunts everything it's got into making cortisol, and then falls short in trying to make other hormones, like aldosterone (which regulates electrolytes and blood pressure). Also, I read that supplementing sea salt helps because the body has a hard time holding onto it. I know I can't seem to eat enough salt.

I can say that after about a month of drinking salt water in the morning (1/2 tsp sea salt in 8 ounces water followed by another 12-16 oz of water), my crazy, non-stop thirst has abated. Used to be I would drink a full glass of water and feel just as thirsty as before I drank it. Also, my lightheadedness when I stand up is getting better. I haven't had my aldosterone retested but I suspect it has improved greatly. I've also made some other changes (started on low-dose Armour and increased iodine) so I can't way for sure the salt has done it, but I suspect it helped.

### Destiny — 2012-08-15T13:59:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-and-bp.3003/post-45002>

I am back and doing really well on the Cytomel dosage of 6.25mcg twice a day. Is there a good way to tell WHEN I need to increase to 3 times a day?

### Patty Cakes — 2012-08-15T18:13:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-and-bp.3003/post-45031>

I think you may feel hypo symptoms again. I took my vitals like a crazy person and found out for me that when my BP would start to go up I needed a dose. An hour after the dose my BP would go back down. I don't think that is normal but it is how my body reacted. Also my temp initially went up but then if I needed to up a dose, temp would get a little low. I started with three doses initially and have pretty much stuck with that but sometimes I add a smidge at night.

### Destiny — 2012-08-18T02:23:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-and-bp.3003/post-45470>

> **Patty Cakes;45361 wrote:** I think you may feel hypo symptoms again. I took my vitals like a crazy person and found out for me that when my BP would start to go up I needed a dose. An hour after the dose my BP would go back down. I don't think that is normal but it is how my body reacted. Also my temp initially went up but then if I needed to up a dose, temp would get a little low. I started with three doses initially and have pretty much stuck with that but sometimes I add a smidge at night.

I think I am getting a hang of it! I take one dose at 6 AM and another between 2-3 PM. Today I felt a bit hypo since this morning. I decide to take a dose at noon. I also expected that I'll need another one in the afternoon but I didn't.

When I got home from work, I cleaned up the whole house! That is unheard of for Friday!! I used to just collapse to bed on Fridays.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-08-22T07:08:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-and-bp.3003/post-46244>

So how are you doing now Destiny? Can you play with your dosing or is your doc micromanaging your T3?

### Destiny — 2012-08-22T13:30:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-and-bp.3003/post-46257>

> **Patty Cakes;46629 wrote:** So how are you doing now Destiny? Can you play with your dosing or is your doc micromanaging your T3?

I certainly could play! My doctor gave me one dose of 25 mcg per day. The instruction on the bottle was to take it once a day which I did not. I knew better! So I still can go up another 12.5 mcg but still don't feel that I need it.

I just started my 3rd week on it, isn't it strange that I am still at 2 doses of 6.25mcg per day?

### Patty Cakes — 2012-08-22T19:31:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-and-bp.3003/post-46364>

That is a very low dose for sure. But you need all kinds of internal support structures to be right before you can tolerate some of the other higher doses, like your iron and adrenals. Also right now you are using up any T4 that is there and may be converting to FT3. Once you clear the rT3 (you did have high rT3 didn't you?) many people find they need to lower the dose because their cells are no longer clogged with rT3 any more and can take up the T3 they couldn't before. I never experienced that. But I was on a T3 in the beginning that I realized after three months was weaker, had a filler that didn't agree with me or whatever, it just didn't work as good as the next batch (different brand). New batch has helped lower my HR which is great!

Good luck and remember use others advice as guideline not hard and fast rules. I try to take the lowest dose possible without sacrificing my total body and thyroid health.

# Thread: I hope some of you go to this blog and post about this......
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-hope-some-of-you-go-to-this-blog-and-post-about-this.3004/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 36
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2012-08-13T05:04:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-hope-some-of-you-go-to-this-blog-and-post-about-this.3004/post-44624>

It appears some of Terry Wahl's comments might have been sullied about by 'some' Paleo MD's (DEANS not Garcia because she was there and he was not. He just read the tweets she heard the context). I really like Dr. Garcia message and his blog, it is quite good. I am however not in agreement with his conclusion nor the conclusions of some other paleo MD's linked in this blog. I dropped my 2 cents because I think Terry should be the face that this community (not who is) needs and I think some are worried about that.

What say you all?

[http://paleolithicmd.com/2012/08/11/a-physicians-manifesto-in-defense-of-my-profession-in-our-world/](http://http://paleolithicmd.com/2012/08/11/a-physicians-manifesto-in-defense-of-my-profession-in-our-world/)

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-13T05:26:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-hope-some-of-you-go-to-this-blog-and-post-about-this.3004/post-44627>

I think most of us can discern the difference between trauma care and health care. Treating acute or chronic symptoms vs mitigating causation (which typically also reduces symptoms). They are both noble and practical.

Saying one is inferior/superior depends on the case at hand. It's entirely relative. Shoes can make great soup bowls in a pinch, but most aren't very useful as a flotation device.

I can see both sides, and I feel her point is misunderstood.

I do agree that we need more prevention, not more damage control.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-08-13T05:33:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-hope-some-of-you-go-to-this-blog-and-post-about-this.3004/post-44630>

I think we have some of the paleo facade creators stirring the pot behind the scenes in this one (DEANS). I just cant fathom someone not getting Terry's perspective but maybe it is because they have not been sick and a MD and a patient. I have and I get that perspective. Maybe that is why I understand where she is coming from. I also think someone in an Ivory tower who does not practice what they preach clinically has to be looked at carefully. Living paleo personally and not professionally because your in an Ivory tower fishbowl is not enough in my view. Maya Angelou said this, “I do not trust people who don't love themselves and yet tell me, 'I love you.' There is an African saying which is: Be careful when a naked person offers you a shirt.” I thin sliced that comment from this mess.

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-13T05:47:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-hope-some-of-you-go-to-this-blog-and-post-about-this.3004/post-44632>

Agreed.

Homeostasis at work.

Some relevant FM Alexander quotes come to mind...

"When people are wrong, the thing that is right is bound to be wrong to them."

"You can't do something you don't know, if you keep on doing what you know."

"Everyone wants to be right, but no one stops to consider if their idea of right is right."

### Jack Kruse — 2012-08-13T06:08:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-hope-some-of-you-go-to-this-blog-and-post-about-this.3004/post-44636>

Terry just dropped her two cents in so I was glad to see her defend herself from the controversy

### LinD — 2012-08-13T06:13:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-hope-some-of-you-go-to-this-blog-and-post-about-this.3004/post-44638>

> **Tom;44936 wrote:**
>
>
>
> I do agree that we need more prevention, not more damage control.

Unfortunately, some of us are beyond prevention and MUST get into damage control. Or did I misunderstand your statement?

### KiwiLauren — 2012-08-13T07:33:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-hope-some-of-you-go-to-this-blog-and-post-about-this.3004/post-44647>

I don't think Dr Wahls or anyone else is advocating that the medical professionals stop helping sick people who are 'beyond prevention.' Many of us on this forum are 'beyond prevention' status (myself included) and Dr K takes up our cause like no one else. From what I see in Dr Wahl's post, she is talking about her vision for optimal medicine and her intention to help guide the field in that direction. Without a new vision, they'll just get the old system reinventing itself. Personally, I want a doctor who can treat the broken AND keep the non-broken healthy. But to do that, all doctors must let go of their curent training paradigm, which is what Dr Wahls said in her rebuttal. If anyone seriously argues that the system is not built (whether or not consciously) to make money off of our illnesses, then they have their head in the proverbial sand.

### Inger — 2012-08-13T08:29:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-hope-some-of-you-go-to-this-blog-and-post-about-this.3004/post-44651>

I read the blog too.. and I cannot believe one can not see what Dr Wahls ment.

It was crystal clear to me she talked about DESEASE. That means NOT a broken leg, a car accident... but it looks like people see what they want to see / read.. that is very intresting. Tells a lot about where people have their mind.

A heart attack is a cronic desease too. It just does not show well on the outside befor the attack occur.

Hat off to the Doctors who have power to fight aganst this major ignorance (that is making truth into lies) that seem so common today.

It makes me think of the Doc that invented handwashing after childbirth assistance to prevent infections in the mother. No one believed him, even if it was crystal clear. He had to whitness how the mothers continued to die in front of his eyes. He ended up in a mental institution and died there.

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-13T13:55:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-hope-some-of-you-go-to-this-blog-and-post-about-this.3004/post-44658>

> **LinD;44947 wrote:** Unfortunately, some of us are beyond prevention and MUST get into damage control. Or did I misunderstand your statement?

Agreed. Less of something does not mean none of something.

Assuming that you would rather not have been broken in the first place, what does our system need to focus on changing?

Getting more doctors and healthcare providers focused on health optimization/maintenance not repair is a better paradigm than what we have now.

If I forget to change the oil in my car and damage the engine, I would still try to fix what's broken. The better approach would have been maintaining it.

### Inger — 2012-08-13T14:31:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-hope-some-of-you-go-to-this-blog-and-post-about-this.3004/post-44660>

Tom good point.

Personally.. if I had followed what my doctors told me to do through the years.. I would be bad off today. Sad but true.

I would have no tonsils anymore.. I would be on the p pill.. I would eat statins... OMG I just do not want to think about it.

I am so happy I took my health in my own hands long ago. Makes it so much easier to fix what still is not optimal!

My intuition told me back then, I need to be careful if I love myself.. there is a better way than what my doctor suggest to me. I saw what is to come if I follow their prescriptions.. (easy, we all have such people in our surroundings, family friends.. but it is almost tabu to speak loud about it - a year ago a dear friend of family died from borreliose, 40 yo male with many children. So sad. And it was the chemical cocktail that he got in the hospital that killed him I am sure) and it scared me as a teen! Yes, sometimes fear can be helpful. ;-)

I am sorry if this sounds harsh but it is my own N1.

And no, I am not afraid to go to doctors, I go every year to check my blood and do labs. But I do it privat, pay all by myself, and get what I want.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-08-13T14:45:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-hope-some-of-you-go-to-this-blog-and-post-about-this.3004/post-44662>

> **KiwiLauren;44956 wrote:** I don't think Dr Wahls or anyone else is advocating that the medical professionals stop helping sick people who are 'beyond prevention.' Many of us on this forum are 'beyond prevention' status (myself included) and Dr K takes up our cause like no one else. From what I see in Dr Wahl's post, she is talking about her vision for optimal medicine and her intention to help guide the field in that direction. Without a new vision, they'll just get the old system reinventing itself. Personally, I want a doctor who can treat the broken AND keep the non-broken healthy. But to do that, all doctors must let go of their curent training paradigm, which is what Dr Wahls said in her rebuttal. If anyone seriously argues that the system is not built (whether or not consciously) to make money off of our illnesses, then they have their head in the proverbial sand.

I agree. I think Dr, Garcia is fighting the good battle in his practice. I am not so sure about the doctors who practice in an Ivory Tower and Rx paleo for themselves but refuse to practice what they preach in the clinic to the best of their ability. I understand that it is a dangerous business but to get change we must do things differently than we have before. I heard that she said on the dais at her own institution to patients with IBD and UC stop being a JERK and eat good food. I am sorry but that is not my perspective in how to incorporate paleo into an academic practice for a patients benefit. I had several doctor friends up their at AHS call me after she made the comment. Not one of the "paleo leaders' will say a word because they like to control the meme of what paleo is. I think patients need to control the meme of what it will become and their doctors will adapt to it. I guess because Terry and I were sick MD's we have a perspective unlike others. I respect different opinions but I do not buy the current meme about this at all. I just wanted to hear what you guys all thought because here most of our community are the sick and broken. Our perspective here is not a 25 yr old crossfitting crowd who is fighting for their right to eat safe starches all year long.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-08-13T14:48:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-hope-some-of-you-go-to-this-blog-and-post-about-this.3004/post-44664>

### Shijin13 — 2012-08-13T15:33:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-hope-some-of-you-go-to-this-blog-and-post-about-this.3004/post-44669>

I posted 1 comment on your FB page, that was respectful of both sides. But after doing that I began to get HOT about the whole thing. Then posted a rant (sorry about that ) but it needed to be said. When I get to Huntsville and have an Internet connection I plan on leaving not comments on the blog as a combined post. Im still pretty hot about

Edit: Here is Gretchen post from FB about Dr. Garcia's blog: Gretchen Bronson I can't see my 1st response to this (FB mobile sucks right now). But this continues to get me hot! Deans pov is the same method of practice that mismanaged my care for the past 10 yrs. while I'm glad there are docs to fill the void of catastrophic care, and those who will step outside their comfort zone and work with a patient to address the underlying causes. It's the mediocrity of care that's unacceptable. The motto of do no harm isnt being followed when a doc won't even consider your symptoms if your labs are in the "normal" range. When docs don't try to solve the underlying issue we get sicker as a species. When did the minimum become the standard of care - we haven't even see the implementation of Obama care. I bet there are lots of people who would jump at the chance to fix the problem but what they're being told only makes it worse not better. So when they don't see results they give up and just accept the pill or the dx that theyre obese and there's nothing they can do about it. They've been beaten by the system they thought was here to help them. But that system was set up to fail both patients and doctors who truly care

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-13T15:43:06+0300

>

(easy, we all have such people in our surroundings, family friends.. but it is almost tabu to speak loud about it - a year ago a dear friend of family died from borreliose, 40 yo male with many children. So sad. And it was the chemical cocktail that he got in the hospital that killed him I am sure) and it scared me as a teen! Yes, sometimes fear can be helpful. ;-)
>

>
>
>
> And no, I am not afraid to go to doctors, I go every year to check my blood and do labs. But I do it privat, pay all by myself, and get what I want.

Not harsh at all, and modern healthcare's failings are what brought me to Eaton and Cordain's work on food and FM Alexander's work on self use and function 9 years ago. I grew tired of the backward thinking in the healthcare of both those I loved and myself.

I knew that my problem wasn't caused by a lack of pain medication.

I'm lucky to have found what made sense. I just keep letting go of it and finding a simpler, more practical 'it' as my understanding becomes refined.
