mm, I ate about an oz of pasture butter yesterday before dinner! so good.

### endless — 2013-03-16T21:43:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-80804>

> Did you try Jack's remedy for cravings? Somebody posted it somewhere around here ... drink an ounce of olive oil.

Nope. I think that would cure me of cravings for sure though, since I'd be barfing. I drank olive oil as part of a gallbladder flush once and it was bad enough mixed with grapefruit juice!

Eating butter, however, I am totally on board with.

I expect the craving thing to drop off quickly anyway...I've been out of most of my supps for over a week and finally back on them all, plus cutting out the kombucha and zevia. The other day as a 'treat' I had 4oz of full fat coconut milk with 4 drops of stevia and a few drops of vanilla, and that was perfect. I have organic cherry jello I can make with stevia if I feel the need. I sometimes crave sweet and fruity, like gummy berries or dried fruit, and that's when the dark chocolate or manna doesn't help.

Inventory is done, and the day has turned drizzly and cold. I'm going to eat my lunch of leftovers and then decide whether to get outside and dig a new flowerbed or stay in and ct....

### endless — 2013-03-17T19:30:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-80875>

Beautiful sunny day today! It's cool and windy but I was able to sit out with my feet on the ground anyway. I'd stay there but I have an appointment. Hopefully still sunny when I get back. Spring cleaning has begun...I washed all the 'cat blankets' that protect my chairs and did some hair removal with a sticky roller. Took my swiffer vac apart and fixed it. Love that thing, it's so great for sucking up hair fluffs off the hard floors.

Eggs, avocado and bp chai for breakfast. I made a grass fed roast in the crock pot last night. It was a blade roast. Extremely fatty...I took off a lot of the fat that didn't render out, but the rest I just blended with the juice and the onion and it made really good gravy. had it last night with just sauteed mushrooms, but tonight I'll make some cauliflower mash.

### janagram — 2013-03-17T19:37:07+0200

and good on ya for spring cleaning....still haven't recovered from a massive one I did 2 yrs ago. Traumatized! (I obsessed ...went around on hands and knees with a dust rag...)

speakin of CTing....which I haven't done in many moons....I was wondering if living in a warm climate would help. Like you're always warm, so CT sounds appealing. Since just being in cold air doesn't seem to count as Ct. Forgive me using your journal to ponder.....

Nope.....not for me anyway! I am sitting in a tub right now with ice on top of me - bot not happy!

### endless — 2013-03-18T01:01:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-80911>

I have a hard time CTing if I am already cold, so for me I think summer would be easier. Today I would have seriously considered getting in the river but it was really windy. The water is probably 38 but would feel colder because of the current I would think.

Speaking of CT, I think I have noticed some strength gains from it. I have taken the 'no exercise' LR pretty seriously and other than walking have done almost nothing for the last year (April I think was when I started...) Today I had a fitness assessment with a friend who is a personal trainer, because like an idiot I have signed up for the Tough Mudder in Whistler in June and I need to start training. She was actually very amazed at how strong I was for how little I have done. I had to keep going up in weight so she could get me to failure on bicep curls. I managed to plank for 1.5 minutes. Now if only my body would begin to LOOK like it's strong and fit. I am really hoping this bloated middle of mine starts to deflate soon, it's so embarrassing to not be able to suck in my gut.

### diane — 2013-03-18T03:13:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-80929>

Way to go Krista! Those strength gains are amazing!

### endless — 2013-03-18T05:01:24+0200

Thanks Diane! I'm pretty impressed, and I figure it must be the CT that's kept me from becoming a total weakling....good motivation to keep it up.

I started digging a flower bed beside the porch today. I can tell I will be outside lifting heavy things very soon, whenever it's not raining!

### caroline — 2013-03-18T06:48:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-80936>

That is awesome krista.....before you know it you will be back to your swelte self! That is what JK has been saying all along .....you will get much fitter and stronger without the exercise

I see so many people jogging at the beach on a hot day and I just see a heart attack waiting to happen.. Lots of older and really out of shape people too. There was a young girl yesterday who was very obese and her trainer kept hollering at her to pick up the pace. I was hoping she had her cell on her to call an Ambo.

### endless — 2013-03-18T17:00:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-80977>

I'm glad I don't fall for that anymore. Sometimes I watch The Biggest Loser on tv and their workouts are alarming! I can't even imagine what their cortisol levels must be at. Yet, how do they lose so much weight? It's a mystery to me.

Things seem to be ok on my HAB regime....I'm making sure to have a BP matcha every morning for the fat, and today it was 3 eggs and an avocado again. Lunch yesterday was a can of tuna with celery and green onion and curry powder and mayo, eaten outside in the sun with bare feet!! Love that spring is here

Friday the oyster merchant is in town, I will buy my usual 2 dozen and have a feast. Maybe some mussels this time, too. I\`m not buying them from the grocery store lately, I\`m trying to cut down on my spending as the supps were pretty expensive this last order. I\`ll eat the GF beef and elk in the freezer and the canned fish, and save my splurge for the oysters.

### caroline — 2013-03-18T22:07:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81017>

sounds like a good plan .....I am spending so much money too.....I am gonna need to get a job!

### endless — 2013-03-19T03:13:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81048>

Well, had a can of sardines for lunch while sitting in a parking lot in my car....not ideal but what the hell. I need to have something more, though, I was really hungry by 4pm. I forgot to put the mac nuts in the car, a few of those would have been enough I think.

Strange weather...snow one minute, sun the next. Bought myself a wheelbarrow today for gardening purposes, but when I went to assemble it I discovered they'd given me the wrong handles Sooo, I could either drill new holes, or wait till tomorrow and exchange them. I'm thinking I won't be building the flower bed tonight so I might just wait.

I started my 'couch to 5k style' running training today....1 minute running, 2 minutes walking, repeat 10 times. After this week it's increasing the running by 1 minute a day till you are running for 30 minutes. Running is not really my thing but I will have to train in it a bit for the event in June. It's a 20km course so if I don't run some of it we'll be out there for hours! The CT is good training too...here's a pic of one of the course obstacles from last year.... { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. ### endless — 2013-03-19T17:07:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81094>

My sleep seems to be getting better. I don't wake up thinking 'wow, what an awesome sleep', but I often don't get up to pee until about 5am, and that's usually after about 7 hours. Ideally I'd like to sleep 8, but I've never been a long sleeper so 7 might be my limit. I try to stay up but 10 is my usual bedtime.

Pan fried sole fillets for breakfast, with my BP matcha. No ripe avocados this morning so I grabbed a couple slices of bacon to top off the fat. No carbs other than the coconut flour breading I used. I peed on a stick this morning out of curiosity and I am definitely in ketosis now, despite having a single glass of wine with dinner and a few squares of 80%. I think cutting the dairy out (except butter) and laying off the stevia soda and kombucha has made a huge difference in how I feel.

I've made it to day 23 so far this cycle with the progesterone started on day 12. Was feeling a bit twingy last night, so I'm not sure if I will make it the whole 28 days this time or not, but at least I'm heading back in the right direction! Hopefully this bloating will start to decrease as well. I was thinking of stopping the D-Ribose for a week to see if that might be contributing, after reading that thread with Mamagrok's experience. Maybe next cycle. Right now I'm just visualizing getting my period at the right time, and peeing out all the extra fluid my body seems to be storing!

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-19T17:09:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81096>

### endless — 2013-03-19T19:52:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81113>

Wow, I'm getting off lucky today....no work intruding on my life yet today  Did another 30 minutes of intervals followed by 15 minutes of lower body CT. I was tempted to try the river but didn't really plan for it when I left the house....maybe tomorrow if it's appropriate.

Sun shining again today too...now that my legs are freezing I think I'll head outside for some sun and see if I can warm them back up.

### endless — 2013-03-20T03:21:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81152>

Well, the sun may not be high enough to give me vitamin D, but it is high enough to burn....I have a sunburn on my face and chest after being out for about 2 hours this morning. Makes me wonder how high my D has to be to protect me. Higher than it is now, obviously! I've put aloe on and vitamin C serum, but it's still very red and inflamed.

Anyone have any good remedies?

### vkiernan — 2013-03-20T13:31:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81168>

I use to put vinegar on a cloth and keep lightly pressing it on the skin for a bit. Then the heat will come out (you will feel it) and you will feel much better. It can be difficult to put vinegar by the nose though, LOL! Good luck.

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-20T17:07:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81182>

cold wet tea bags also should help ... the tannin in the tea is supposed to soothe sunburn. And this is why I started the tanning booth in February. I wanted to be adjusted (i.e.tanned) before I got out in the real thing. Some of the worst sunburns I had as a kid I got in early Spring in mid-morning. Only for me it would only take about 20 minutes, not two hours. So maybe you've made progress?

And just fyi, when I was in college I went skiing 2 days in a row and blistered my face (this was in the dark ages before sunscreen). I peeled 3 layers of skin and looked like a raccoon for months. There was some nasty concoction called Foille ??? the infirmary gave me to put on the burn. THAT one didn't hurt because it was so bad. It was just ugly.

### endless — 2013-03-20T17:47:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81190>

So I am confused about the vitamin D thing. How can the sun be strong enough to burn, but not strong enough to stimulate Vit. D? That doesn't make sense to me.

thanks for the suggestions, I will do both I think. I'm a little bummed about the burn (although it felt SO GOOD sitting out there yesterday!) because today it is snowing and gray again and it would have been a perfect day for the tanning salon. I think I'll be laying off for several days to heal my skin instead. I used an ice pack on it last night, and will do some face dunks this morning (maybe cold tea instead? Hmmm...)

Kind of ok with the snow other than that....I am SORE today, and not upset about missing my running/walking. I did that in the morning yesterday, followed by finally putting together my wheelbarrow and finishing my new flowerbed, which involved lifting some very heavy things (rocks). Then I raked the yard and various other tasks, followed by fire practice. I did stretch last night before bed but my muscles are sore today. I think the CT helped, I did it right after the run, and only the lower body.

I took an extra dose of mag last night, and I am feeling it this morning in the nether regions. Funny, the other three types (malate, orotate, threonate) combined are a fairly substantial amount, but I've never gotten loose stools from them. The citrate, I get it often. This time could have been just pushing me over the edge of tolerance, but I've noticed it with smaller doses as well. I wonder if that means I SHOULD take citrate, or shouldn't?

I went nuts in Crappy Tire yesterday and bought a whole bunch of bulbs! Freesias, lilies, tulips, hostas, crazy Peruvian daffodils, astilbe and begonias. I haven't even made the shade bed yet, so getting a bit ahead of myself here. I also bought deer netting, and I am going to need it. Tulips are like deer crack.

### vkiernan — 2013-03-20T19:10:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81197>

Nothing like planting all those great things and reaping the loveliness from it! Keep writing, you might motivate me to do more. I have an open bed that has been sitting for years and really needs some attention.

### caroline — 2013-03-20T23:20:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81226>

I have no affinity for plants at all - wish I did......sounds like you are quite an all rounder! Seems like you are a very handy gal!

### endless — 2013-03-21T18:05:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81322>

I try, Caroline! I have been single for a good while too, so usually if I want something done I have to learn to do it myself!

I used to have a huge vegetable garden, and I grew most of our food. I started getting burnt out because I was the only one doing most of the work (I was living with a hedonist who always found something more enjoyable to do than weed) so I quit. I moved around a lot for the last several years, and last year was my first garden again in awhile. I am back to loving it...even though I still have beautiful winter squash under my bed that I have not been eating! Guess I should give the rest away. I can easily grow all my onions and garlic for the year, and usually I grow lots of beets and carrots as well. I suppose I will shift my focus a bit to reflect my changing diet. No need for winter squash or root veggies in such abundance, and I will grow more sturdy greens. I will still grow lots of tomatos and make fresh salsa, because I love growing tomatos.

Less than stellar dinner last night...I worked fairly steadily all day and then stopped in at a gym on my way home to try out the new program my trainer friend made for me. I did only half of it though, it was getting last and I was so hungry. I picked up a cooked rotisserie chicken and some greek salad and vinaigrette slaw at the grocery store because I couldn't wait. So....not even good quality chicken! Oh well. At least I didn't use it as an excuse to go get gluten free pizza.

Breakfast was the same, leftovers from dinner plus my BP matcha. I'm very impressed with my body for handling the caffeine so well this past week! I still get a bit of 'bumpiness' in ym heartbeat, but the palpitations seem to be much less than they used to be. I have been avoiding this matcha powder because when I tried it before it made my heart crazy. I'm taking D-Ribose in the morning too, wonder if that helps?

I am going to try to hit the gym again today. My body is really loving the activity after so long avoiding it. I am staying mindful of my cortisol and making sure I don't get too intense. Making an effort to spend my evening 'tv' time on the floor stretching instead of just sitting.

### janagram — 2013-03-22T02:04:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81382>

yeah, I am doing that latter thing too! I love it! I get into some deep stretches, downward dog (that makes it hard to watch the tv), some plank poses, just plain squatting...its fun.

I have given up trying to have any kind of flowers. The damn deer even eat my marigolds. I tried putting them in window boxes and the little bastards come right up to the house and just nibble away. Actually, half the time they yank the plants right outta the dirt.

### endless — 2013-03-22T04:37:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81406>

I gave up at my old place too Darleen. This time I am going to make some beds and cover them with that fine black deer mesh. You can hardly see it but they HATE it. I also clean my comb out after I wash my hair and toss the strands on the beds...they don't like that either.

So I just spent some time on [http://rhythmicliving.org/](http://rhythmicliving.org/) and I am slightly alarmed.....I am seriously wondering about high dose BHRT now. How could SO MANY women all complain of weight gain and other negatives from it without there being something to it? It's bizarre.

### SeaHorse — 2013-03-22T14:11:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81425>

I remember asking JK a question after the Q and A on hormones....he is not a big supporter of women having periods after they go through menopause from taking BHRT. It's a matter of degree I think. There are certainly a lot of opinions on both sides of the fence.

### endless — 2013-03-22T17:18:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81451>

That there is....I am still cycling naturally, just trying to boost my very low levels, but I am going to proceed with caution. I don't think throwing large doses around is necessarily right for everyone.

That being said, I am on cycle day 26, and this will be my last day using the P cream this cycle! I am so glad I made it without premature bleeding! I figured out that I have been using 50mg per day, once a day. If I increase, I think I will just start using it twice a day for 100mg total. The compounding pharmacist I spoke to said that I am probably getting very good absorption from using it vaginally, better than dermally or orally, so I will stick with it. Not going to change anything else until my next set of labs, and see where to go from there.

### janagram — 2013-03-22T17:57:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81456>

>
> That being said, I am on cycle day 26, and this will be my last day using the P cream this cycle! I am so glad I made it without premature bleeding! I figured out that I have been using 50mg per day, once a day. If I increase, I think I will just start using it twice a day for 100mg total. The compounding pharmacist I spoke to said that I am probably getting very good absorption from using it vaginally, better than dermally or orally, so I will stick with it. Not going to
>
>
>
> change anything else until my next set of labs, and see where to go from there.

Krista .... what made your Pharmacist think you were absorbing better - vaginally? I just don't understand how you decide which method is best. With the cost of all this stuff it would be great to know which method is the most effective and then stick with it ..... or I wonder if it changes over time - I guess that would make sense too.

### endless — 2013-03-23T01:53:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81501>

I'm not sure, Caroline....maybe it's just that mucous membrane absorbs better than skin? I know it seems to be common knowledge that you only absorb about 30% when you take it orally.

Has anyone watched or listened to the webinar yet? The size of the download was quite a lot smaller than the file size listed on the link. I'm about 15 minutes in and there has been a few 'skips' or audio cutting out.

### caroline — 2013-03-23T02:01:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81502>

### diane — 2013-03-23T02:48:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81509>

I've got the oral progesterone - start it tonight. I asked the doctor which one she recommended -she said oral, so I figure I have to start somewhere. Meanwhile, I can tell that the Vitex has been doing something to progesterone, as a lot of things have started to come back. Kinda cool, actually.

### endless — 2013-03-23T03:29:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81512>

Good to know Diane! Dr. K told me that oral is better for sleep. I have some, but I haven't tried it yet. I also have some vitex tincture that I have had for a long time...wonder if it'd still be effective. I might try adding that in next cycle.

### endless — 2013-03-23T20:34:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81557>

Yesterday I went to the gym and did both my running intervals and my weight program...plus some core work and lots of stretching when I got home. My body is obviously craving the movement after so long! I'm moving slowly because I had metatarsalgia on my left foot after a night of dancing in some inappropriate footwear months ago, and I'm just starting in minimalist runners so I don't want to push my feet into a chronic injury. So far so good! I'm stretching and exercising my feet a lot too, trying to rehab them after years of using high-tech super supportive running shoes. Poor feet, they have been made so weak from not being allowed to be feet and move naturally!

Today is a rest day, exercise-wise, because tonight I will be dancing again (this time in proper shoes!) and will get a good workout that way It's a beautiful sunny day here....COLD in the breeze but it's tempting to go and bare it all anyway. I'm not, because of my recent experience with burning myself. I'll do some limited exposure.

Breakfast was salmon ceviche and avocado with BP herbal chai. I haven't had raw salmon in awhile, it was a nice treat. Didn't get oysters this weekend so that was my consolation.

### caroline — 2013-03-24T00:37:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81585>

It sounds like everything is coming together nicely for you Krista.....have a great nite of dancing - I am very jealous!
You must get to meet tons of guys! Have you got your groove on?

### diane — 2013-03-24T02:43:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81590>

Yes, the right dancing shoes are very important! What kind of music do you dance to Krista? Hope you have an awesome night!

### endless — 2013-03-24T19:38:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81635>

Caroline, I need to take some lessons from you for that! I am a real introvert in person...it's hard for me to meet people. I was thinking about that on my drive home last night. I might have been able to meet some men if I wasn't so awkward. Not to mention still gun shy from my last disasterous relationship. I have never actually 'dated' anyone I did not already know as a friend, co-worker, whatever. I can't even imagine internet dating. I am of the opinion that if it is meant to happen it will....although the more I can help by putting myself out there the better, I guess.

It was a fun night...I met up with a girl I know who was buying my extra ticket, but eventually was just on my own. It was very crowded so dancing anywhere was fine and I was able to stay away from the packed dance floor. I left shortly before 2am. He was still going, but my feet were a bit sore and I was ready to leave. The 'drunk lunks' were getting harder to avoid and I am short and apparently not easily seen through beer goggles! I got jostled around a bit too much there at the end.

Diane, I like a lot of stuff, but this was a DJ that plays here in the summer at the Shambhala festival and is very well known and popular here. I heard it originally through my brother's girlfriend who is into that kind of music and discovered that I like a lot of it, especially for dancing. Now I know why there are so many people using drugs at 'rave' type events though...it would be almost impossible to dance as long as they do without some kind of help. It's an odd experience going to a bar sober....you realize there are not many other people there in the same state of mind!

Well I was hoping for a nice relaxing day off, but I just got called to work. Hopefully a short and sweet one and I can come home and putter. The sun is shining and with any luck it will kill off the last of the snow in the yard.

I stopped the progesterone yesterday, and today I got my period. In retrospect I maybe should have taken it one more day as it is day 28 today. I'm ok with that, just happy it's more normal! Now I have to decide whether to start as early this time. I started day 12, and wonder if I should push it back to day 14. Last time I tried to do that I bled before I could start the progesterone. Have to think about it some more.

### caroline — 2013-03-24T22:32:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81646>

Krista - I am very shy too but trying to do better. For me - what has made a huge difference is feeling a lot better about myself now that I have my weight under control. It just gives you so much more self confidence ...... but also - the last few years I really gravitate toward positive, motivated, kind people. That does a world of good to be around this kind of energy .....I don't have time for anything else these days.

### endless — 2013-03-25T17:13:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81695>

Caroline, I think that is what is making it harder for me right now is my weight gain/bloating from this BHRT....I feel very self conscious about my midsection and can't (won't) wear anything that shows it. I think that once that goes away I will be less reticent. I am still a few pounds heavier than my Ideal, but it's the puffy tummy that is making me reclusive.

So....I had great intentions for a 'me' day yesterday, but ended up going to work for a bit. While I was out I hit up the fish bus for some sockeye lox, so the outing was worth it. I came home and hit the trail for my run....but it was not without it's interruptions. I was stopped by an older gentleman who politely asked me if I could come into the hall where the AGM of our local co-op store was going on and sign something because they were short one member for a quorum or something. Too funny...I love rural living. So my dog and I went into the hall and signed so they could get on with the business at hand.

THEN my dog decided to head off by herself across the field to the river for a drink. I kept on going, confidant that she would catch up like she usually does. This time she was looking for me in the wrong direction, then got distracted by another person and dog and panicked because she couldn't find me. Since she's deaf, I couldn't call her, so all I could do was watch her scurrying around and then start heading home. So, I aborted my run and went to rescue her.

We stopped at the bridge for a drink (dog, not me) and I decided to test out the water. I can't believe I was thinking of getting in there, it is REALLY cold! I went in up to my knees, and staying in for 30 seconds took everything I had. My feet started aching immediately. I waited a bit and did it again, but that was all I could manage. Maybe I will try it later in my cycle...I'm thinking of what Gretchen said about CT being harder when progesterone is low.

I had a 'planned' cheat dinner last night. I've been off cheese for the most part for awhile now, so I picked up a gluten free pizza crust and made a pizza for dinner with the tomato sauce I made from my garden, some organic mozza, and veggies. Also had 2 glasses of red wine. I enjoyed it immensely and now I think I am good to go without anything cheesy-tomatoey for a good while. Was probably partly because of my period, I tend to crave that 'comfort food' then.

Today I will hit the gym if schedule allows, get my tax stuff to the accountant, and hopefully get some sun/grounding. Another sunny day!

### endless — 2013-03-26T03:15:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81800>

Well I discovered a new 'type' at the gym today, but kind of an odd one.....there was a lovely young woman there who I noticed because she was casually gliding around the weight room, stopping occasionally to pose...er..I mean stretch....in front of various mirrors. She was so obviously admiring herself that it was funny to watch. Didn't pick up a weight the entire time. It was pretty funny. Also funny was that while her shape was nice, it was obviously mostly due to genetics and diet, not fitness. She was very slim waisted (no excess cortisol) but had a booty on her like J-Lo (that she had proudly clad in tight sweats with 'vegan' blazoned across the top of her ass). I'm thinking lots of estrogen from all the soy in her diet. Top half was thin and pale, like most vegans I know. I swear, people watching at the gym is becoming the most entertaining part of my day!

Of course, I probably provide a lot of entertainment value myself, hauling up my yoga capris that have slid below my belly flab every time I do a running interval....

### endless — 2013-03-26T19:49:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81875>

Another sunny day I'm appreciating each one, because I know that the rainy spring days are on the way. If it's warm enough I will think about doing some more yard work.

Had a slightly disturbing thing happen....I have been living in this rental for 1 1/2 years now, and for the first time since 2008 I have been feeling secure with my living space. I moved 7 times between April 2008 and July 2011. This place has been a dream come true, and I've made no bones about it to my landlords that as long as they are happy with me I am considering this a long term situation. Now I've been informed that my neighbors next door want to buy the piece of land that my house is on, and I feel pretty confidant that if they do, my time here will be over. I don't know what is going to happen for sure, but I am feeling a little less secure than I was. It is SO HARD to find an affordable rental for one person in my area, especially with my critters. I'm really hoping this sale does not happen, for my sake. It's frustrating to be in this situation, and being totally dependant on others for my living situation. Sucks to not be able to afford to buy a place to live.

I'm trying not to worry about it, but it's tough.

### diane — 2013-03-26T20:35:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81881>

Krista I hope it works out for you! Maybe the neighbours will buy it and still let you live there?

### caroline — 2013-03-26T23:05:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81893>

I understand your situation completely. It is so much harder when you are on your own. I am sending you positive thoughts and some quantum energy....

### endless — 2013-03-27T01:39:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81910>

Thanks, Caroline....quantum energy gladly accepted.

Diane, that would be great, but I just instinctively feel that they would rather have me out. We live very rurally, and if they buy this piece and boot me out, they have complete privacy, whereas now they are as close to me as if we lived in a suburb or something. I don't really blame them for wanting that, it just sucks for me. BUT, on the other hand, maybe they would appreciate the extra rental income...I have to stay positive.

Finally got my tax stuff to the accountant today....after getting everything ready it became obvious to me that I have to ask for a raise in my mileage. I need about $2000 more per year to cover all my fuel, insurance and repairs. That is only a 5 cent per km increase, but I am still nervous about asking.

NO exercise today....I took the usual doggie walk and then spent a lot of time outside grounding in the sun. Might have burnt my face again, doh! I will do my stretchnig routine tonight but will save the strenuous stuff for another day.

### endless — 2013-03-27T02:07:28+0200

### endless — 2013-03-27T17:58:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81965>

I thought of that, Darleen....I will ask them about that if I get the chance, but I have an idea that they may already have given the neighbors first dibs( like before I even moved in...). It's an obvious choice....the piece I am on actually WAS originally part of theirs, but the previous owner sold it to my landlord 15 years ago. It kind of 'belongs' with their property. I'm actually wondering if I should approach the neighbors themselves and ask them what their intentions are and if they are planning to boot me out. I can confidantly say that they wont find a better tenant...my concern is that they don't want a tenant at all.

F@H, yes, getting the tax stuff squared away is a relief. I really have to get a better system going through the year instead of having piles of receipts to deal with at the end of it! I used to do the bookkeeping for my ex-fiance's business, so it's not like I don't know how. I just don't want to spend the $$ on the Simply Accounting software.

I didn't work out yesterday.I am hoping to get to the gym today, schedule permitting. I think the day off in between is good, if anything I should have CTd yesterday but just could NOT bring myself to hang out in my dark little bathroom when it was beautiful and sunny outside. I need a stock tank!

Had a good night at practice last night. I asserted myself within the engine 'crew' and told them I wanted to run the truck...usually I defer and let the guys do it. Every once in awhile I need to remind them that I've been doing it longer than any of them and that a penis is NOT a requirement to do this job. Still bothers me that they always seem surprised when I manage to operate everything properly and not screw up. I swear, sometimes the size of the male ego just leaves me speechless.

### endless — 2013-03-27T21:02:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81979>

Jeez....now I have to decide whether to pay the $149 for the EMF series. I will have to mull it over.

Went for my run this morning in the sun, followed by standing in the river up to my thighs. I lasted longer today, probably in only five minutes total but it felt great! My feet hurt a few times, which prompted me to get out but I went back in. Hopefully it'll help soothe my shocked lower leg muscles that are wondering what the hell they are being subjected to after so much time languishing.

I'm noticing a difference in my period so far this cycle....far less crampy, steady flow that is not clotty, if anything it's slightly watery. It seems to be lasting slightly longer than before, when it was short, heavy and painful. All in all I'd have to say I prefer this!

### endless — 2013-04-13T20:36:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-84001>

I took the TriField for another week so I could do some more testing, and was shocked when I brought it home and found the levels in my house MUCH higher than I originally found. Last time the background was 0.2-0.4 (milligauss), this time it was 1.25. Scary....so I've been taking measurements in the same spot whenever I think of it, and recording them. It seems to vary from 0.2- 1.25, with no pattern I can discern. Yesterday in the morning it was very low, from 0.6 at 6am to 0.1 at 8am....but this morning it is around 1.0! Either this meter is shot, or something bizarre is going on in my area.

I was not impressed with the reading at the tanning salon either...I have been using the 'weakest' bed, and when I took a reading inside it was over 50. So much for electronic ballasts...obviously there is more to it than that. Not sure what to do now...I still have a lot of credits there to use. Maybe I will try the stronger beds, just to see if the readings are worse or better. I really wish I had another meter to test with!

Im planning to get the laptop ground set up today, I have everything I need except one adaptor so I can use the small grounding rod I got from earthing.com. I'm not going to be using it for earthing myself anymore in light of the fluctuations in the strength of the magnetic field...

Tried to find a multimeter yesterday, but could not find one with the right range....I guess I will have to order it online. I am wanting to have the body voltage meter to help figure out some more about how to fix my environment the best I can.

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-04-13T21:07:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-84002>

What is the Trifield reading outdoors away from your house and any electrical devices? This will be the "natural" reading. Thus, it is doubtful any indoor reading would be lower.

As Michael Neuert has said, the Trifield readings are usually high. My N=1 example ... using the Trifield at my computer desk, I get a reading of 1.2 but using the Alpha UHS meter I rented from him, the reading in the same location was .43. ... a big difference.

Time of day and how many electrical devices (i.e. A/C, etc.) can cause gauss meter readings to vary in the same location.

### endless — 2013-04-13T22:40:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-84005>

I am pretty sure it is coming from power lines, not from anything inside my house. It does not change inside even when I turn off the main breaker. Outside it is the same. I have some large power lines about 400 meters away, and there must be a large fluctuation in their output....I can't figure out what else would cause it. When I originally tested them a few months ago, I only did it once and my background reading in and out of my house was 0.2. At that time I had to go halfway to the powerlines to get a reading of 1.0

I'm hoping the readings ARE high, although what I took from him is that the reason they might be high is interference from dirty electricity....not sure if that would apply here.

Loved that quote Christa. And Peter, you are so right.

That is why I do not read newspaper anymore. They are like they try to scare us all the time, without any suggestion to solving the problems. Just bad news. Like the other day my dad as I went downstairs and they were eating breakfast, he reading the newspaper.
He said;
Inger, look here what's going on! A catastrophe! I said, what is it dad? He read loud; **In Madagascar huge amount of big grasshoppers invading the land are causing famine.**
I said, dad, can you see the mismatch there? He said, no? I told him Johannes in the bible was living off from grasshoppers and honey (my dad is very religious so it always works to tell him stories from the bible..lol). Only. How could grasshoppers cause famine? They are great foods! So the hole thing was a plain lie and upside down. People just have to go and catch those grasshoppers and they will have more than enough food!

I said to dad that is why I do not read newspapers. They are so full of lies and stuff that want to scare us it is not worth it at all.

### endless — 2013-07-06T07:08:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-89583>

I have not seen the webinar or q&a, but I would have loved to see the q&a...movnat sounds right up my alley. I just finished 2 weeks of rest after the mudder and am ready to get back to some strength exercise.

I do have a fairly good garden, although it got a late start due to bad spring weather. Things are just starting to take off now....I'm eating arugula and spinach, kohlrabi and kale, and the peas are almost ready. The horses reached over the fence last night and yanked my three largest sunflowers out...grrr. So today I strung a line of electric fencing along the top in case they decide to try any more thievery!

Still doing a bit of CT in the river, although it's really not that cold anymore. Better than nothing I guess. I am most certainly NOT gardening in a bikini....still suffering from a big puffy midsection since starting the Pg....and now it seems worse since starting iodine this week Not feeling very bikini worthy lately!

Also, I'm worried about my blood sugar.....consistantly high, even through the leptin reset. I just took it randomly tonight (right after eating raw kohlrabi, probably should have waited longer) and it was 138! FBG has been consistantly about 110 or higher. I wonder if the elevated cortisol from the training I was doing made it worse, but it didn't seem to improve at all even at my strictest epi paleo. I'm thinking of asking the doc for metformin.

### endless — 2013-11-06T22:44:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-100016>

Hi Caroline,
No horses were electrocuted in the making of this garden.... Actually they got the hint pretty quickly. Garden is all put to bed now, garlic all cozily planted under a big straw cover and waiting for snow.

I can't say I'm resurrecting this journal exactly, but I want to record some stuff in here to keep track of it. I am SO BEHIND in the blogs....working a lot and not able to spend much time online. I did manage to finally listen to the hormone Q&A with Tim Jackson last night. It was great, and pinpointed some things I want to work on next.

I don't see getting a magnetico or Earthpulse anytime soon...still sleeping with breakers off and grounding strap on and other than recently, since the time change, sleeping pretty well.

I finally figured out how to calculate my P/E ratio and want to record the changes.

April 2012 14(!)
Sept 2012 92
Jan 2013 172
May 2013 110
Nov 2013 585 (!)

I don't know if something finally shifted or that was a lab error, I'll find out next time. My estrogen is still low, bottom of the range but my P has finally shot up. I think I should back down on the supplementation.

DHEA was low in Nov of last year (2.1 with a range of 1.4-14.7), better in May (8, range 2-10), but now is back down again this Nov (3.8, range 1.4-14.7)

The only thing I can think of is that I started alternating one month regular DHEA with one month 7 Keto. Dosage didn't change.

My T3 has risen a wee bit...from 4.5 last year to 5.1 this time (range 3.5-6.5) T4 done for the first time, it's low (12.3, range 11.5-22.7) I'm still trying to figure out what that means. Still haven't found RT3 available anywhere.

My biggest focus over the past 4 months has been my stubbornly high FBG...I've tried so many things. PGX fiber, Nopal, ACV, green coffee bean extract, cinnamon, chromium, and on and on! It's frustrating, and doesn't seem to respond in any coherent way to any diet changes I've tried. More carbs, less carbs, keto....

Strangely, this morning I had a reading of 5.0 (90) for the first time in months! Usually I am around 115-120. Now I have to try and figure out which thing was responsible....I ate at least 4.5 hours before bed, so decided to take a tbsp of coconut manna and an oz of raw cheese before bed. I also drank a large glass of ACV water before bed. I ALSO took 1 tbsp of unmodified potato starch in water when I ate my dinner.....been reading a lot about people's experience with resistant starch and decided to give it a try. I'm not going on the potato diet, but if this starch with my meals can help lower my BG I'm willing to give it a try.

After the hormone Q&A I think I need to look carefully at my phase II detox pathways in the liver....which most likely means finally getting a consult about my SNPS.

Ahh, the never ending quest for health!

Hope everyone is doing well, maybe once the snow covers up my yard I'll be spending more time in here again.

### endless — 2013-11-07T05:39:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-100049>

Thanks guys

Gretchen, I really don't know....I upped my dose on my own at one point, now I take 240 mg orally at night plus 0.5g of a 10% cream vaginally in the morning during the luteal phase of my cycle. Thinking I might drop the cream this month and see what happens.

Since I got the ratio recovered I also started testosterone cream, for about two months now. Unfortunately it is still low and I'm not noticing any improvements in libido yet. Funny, Dr. Tim said on the Q&A that most women with insulin resistance have HIGH testosterone....trust me to be the outlier.

I took another tbsp of potato starch for dinner with my chicken livers and coconut flour/squash fritters. I'm curious to see if the FBG reading is good again tomorrow.

### endless — 2013-11-11T05:04:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-100377>

Oh. My. God.

I just made the best seafood soup. Homemade salmon stock from my sockeye that I bought this fall, with onions, garlic, mushrooms and bok choy sauteed in pasture butter, some Thai spices, and some wild sockeye and sole. Shiritaki noodles for some resistant starch, I have to do soup more often, it's such a great way to get the bone broths in! This stock was positively jiggly when I dumped it in the pot, even close to room temp.

So, FBG hacking continues.....the 5.0 was followed by 6.0, 6.3, 6.3, 5.6 Not as impressive, but still well below the previous week of 6.7-6.9. The potato starch is working, and depending on when I take it it affects my FBG differently. I have to say that I have not exactly been stellar this week either....I have eaten concentrated sugars every night and still had lower numbers. (I had a small bag of dried mango that I needed to make gone, and I bought TWO POUNDS of dark Callebaut chocolate to make some medicinal chocolates for some friends and unfortunately have had to sample it several times....

Anyway, if I take it after dinner, it IMMEDIATELY squashes any desire to snack for the rest of the night. Amazing. AND it results in a lower FBG in the morning. I tried having a TBSP of manna the other night, wondering if that was what had caused the low reading, but no, I got 6.3 in the morning. It was only last night when I took 2 TBSP starch in water shortly after I ate dinner that I got the lower reading again.

Oh, forgot to mention....I'm also doing the iodine protocol, and am up to 100mg of Lugol's most days (not every day....I use my pendulum to decide) and so far feeling good!

### endless — 2013-11-12T20:18:51+0200

Well, it's my birthday today.... 11/12/13  Maybe I should buy a lottery ticket.

So I am pretty impressed with the resistant starch results. If I take 1-2 tbsp in water after dinner not only do I have no 'snack attacks' or cravings, my FBG in the morning has been amazing! I've gone from 6.4-6.7 average to 5.0-5.5

I suppose there is probably a reason why it's not a great idea, but for now I am ok with using it. The high FBG was stressing me out, so if I can remove that as an issue I can focus more on other things for awhile  I've been pretty slack on the diet and I want to get back to keto. I'm going to pick up the lamb I ordered today, yay! I asked them for extra hearts and livers so I'm excited to get those, haven't had any heart for some time now. I'm thinking about trying the high meat with some of it. I saved a lot of my kale and swiss chard from the garden, I vaccuum sealed it and froze it, so I'm planning to use that through the winter.

Gotta go to work....

### endless — 2013-12-07T06:43:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-102677>

Hi Inger!

I guess I was too optimistic thinking I would be on here more often.... I have been experimenting with the potato starch and also with tapioca starch, and I've noticed a drop in my FBG from the 6.4-6.9 range to 5.4-5.9. That's with random usage. When I consistantly used it at least twice a day it dropped into the 4.7-4.9 range! Only problem is I think I'm reacting to the potatos...I'm getting joint pain, which I have always gotten from potatos. I have ordered some green plantain flour and will also try that. In the meantime I am going to lay off the potato starch and try only tapioca for a week or so to see what effect that has.

It's cold here lately....-18C today. So I decided it was the perfect time to defrost my freezer! I just stuck everything outside for the day while I went to work and left the freezer off and open. Came home and stuck everything back in. I love passive housework.... I took stock of the contents while I repacked. Still have 2 roasts from last years order of grass fed beef, one pork roast and one ham. I have about 10 pounds of sockeye salmon fillets, some scallops, prawns and cod fillets from Costco, my 1/2 of lamb, and 10 whole local chickens. The door is full of canning jars full of bone broth and bags of raw nuts. I have a box of vaccuum sealed kale and chard from my garden, and 6 pounds of pasture butter. I have venison coming too, my brother shot me a buck last week, yay! Heart and liver from the lamb and deer, btu I noticed my last bag of chicken livers is freezer burned, so I guess the cats will be getting it. I also have one bag of local wild huckleberries left. I think I'll be ok if the zombie apocalypse happens...provided my power doesn't go out that is!

I'm feeling like my gut is unhappy lately....a bit of travelling put my routine off and now I'm having lots of bloating and gas. I need to double up on the ferments and stuff for a bit and see if it helps!

I am not going to buy me a BG meter after all... so glad I waited. I just could not believe we need some not in season, refined foods to stay healthy. It would be just against Nature's laws somehow? ### endless — 2013-12-17T21:50:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-103683>

Hmm, my lamb stock didn't turn out as well as I'd hoped....think I used too much water and simmered it too high since it didn't gel when it cooled. Still smells amazing though! I have the second half of the bones to try again. I'm also ordering some Great Lakes gelatin to start using as well.

My seafood consumption has tanked....I still have the salmon I bought in the fall in the freezer but I'm trying to use the stuff I already have so not buying much. What I have is venison, lamb and chicken so using more of that lately. I guess I can't really call myself epi-paleo anymore! I can't bring myself to buy most prawns anymore now that I know how destructive that industry is...,.seems to be the case with so many things. I do have several lamb and venison organs in the freezer and my plan is to cut up one of the livers into small chunks and freeze it in 1-2 oz servings that I can just down raw or throw into a smoothie.

Feeling like such a sloth today! I walked the dog this morning but I'm having very heavy menstrual cramps and just can't get motivated to move around much more.....

In other news....the potato starch is not happening much anymore, I just react too strongly to it. Funny, it's the only nightshade I notice that with. Instead I will try eating some raw almonds. I've also ordered some Fungal Defense and Primal Defense from Garden of Life and am going to take those for awhile to see if I notice anything. I still have an awful lot of abdominal bloating and obviously there must be something less than optimal going on in my gut!

### sooperb — 2014-03-02T00:34:36+0200

### endless — 2014-03-02T23:15:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-113099>

> I like it, you look quite mysterious

Thanks! I think I look annoyed, but it's blurry so hard to see I guess.

Hi Audrey! So I have to disappoint you, I am still raunch-less  I have been single now for 4 years and celibate for 3. That's scary! I might have to lower my standards soon....

You asked me about my RS experimenting...I stopped with the potato starch because it bothers my joints, but I am still using some food sources...most shockingly for this forum is I've added back some legumes. I'm soaking them for 24 hours, cooking them in bone broth, and freezing them to maximize resistant starch content. I'm eating about a 1/2 cup with my eggs and avocado a few times a week. Also part of the occasional baked and cooled potato. I had also ordered some green plantain flour, another good source of RS, and used it by making cookie dough with it. I froze balls of this, thinking I could eat a couple at a time for a dose of starch. Apparently I won't be doing that again as I was unable to police myself and ate them all pretty damn fast.  I had some impressive digestive reorganizing going on for awhile, major fartage and bloating (good thing I AM single...hee hee) but that has settled down and I am still having the good Bristol Chart results! FBG is not AS low as it was while using the starch directly, but I'm down to an average of mid-90s from 115-ish.

My biggest issue at the moment is nerve pain in my arms....I wake up with numb fingers. I've been working with my chiro on it but so far it's not improving, so I might have to try something else. I've had good results with IMS in the past, just can't afford to go very often.

Hi Krista ...... celibate is not good! but personally - I am raising my standards!

Did you see Jack's webinar on RS? his biohack...

I like what you are doing - you are working on what is right for you .... Jack talked a lot about that and getting RS in food.

If our redox is good our body can handle stuff ... it will do its own housekeeping. That makes so much sense to me

Do you have a new phone or anything else? what has changed? re - arm pain?

we have a new member Alex ..... please read her story.....

I can't believe you have no raunch???? what is Martin going to say .....

Please tell me more when you have time .....

How is your pet snake???

### endless — 2014-03-05T08:04:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-113518>

Well, I did strict leptin reset for a long time, now I am not following a strict protocol of anything really.....I eat less seafood than I did on the reset, I eat some of my meat and seafood raw, but I do eat more vegetables than is suggested for my climate at this time of year now. Last winter I was much more epi-paleo. I still eat almost no fruit, but like I said I have incorperated some resistant starch foods.

I don't think it's carpal tunnel, but I'm not sure. I've actually got one bum wrist that is starting to piss me off a lot when I try to do push ups, but it's been like this for over 30 years. I'm pretty sure the numbness is coming from my neck. Off to the chiropractor for an adjustment and decompression therapy on Friday, hopefully that will help!

Audrey, I did listen to Jack's RS biohack....my experience was different than his, for sure. I already use nuts so not much is different in my current approach really. I did notice a big effect on my morning blood glucose with the potato starch, too bad it's irritating to me or I'd continue it!
My snake is doing fine, he must be doing a spring cleanse or some secret snakey protocol, as he's been off his food for a month or so. Probably just the time of year. I always find it a little disturbing when he goes so long without eating but it doesnt seem to bother him, It's just a really long intermittant fast, I guess....

Signed us up last night for Tough Mudder in Whistler again, so I will have to get my ass in gear one again. 110 days of training left!

My adrenals are a little tired tonight. I attended a suicide today, but the call came in as a gunshot wound and to wait for police attendance, so we were not sure what we were walking into. Nerve wracking, followed by feeling kind of helpless since there was nothing to be done to help. Going to take some magnesium and go to bed. If I was drinking I would have some wine first,but I have not had a drink since the end of December! Not even planned, I just stopped. I have no intention of abstaining forever, but I am happy to have removed the 'habit' portion of drinking alcohol from my life....now I think it will be more of a special occasion thing. Downside is, I can't self medicate at times like these....or I guess that's actually an upside.

### endless — 2014-03-19T21:34:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-116500>

I think this is la large part of my problem...excess mucin. Everything I'm reading about it fits me exactly.

> Myxedema, which is the retention of mucin, can also occur when the tissues do not properly process and utilize thyroid hormone. (Myx is the Greek word for "mucin," and edema means "swelling.") Mucin is a compound comprised of sugars bound to a protein and in modest amounts is a constituent of connective tissue. (Connective tissue lines blood vessels, comprises nerve sheaths, is part of the fascial envelope surrounding muscles, and is in organs and glands, in the gastrointestinal and urinary tracts, and in the mucous membrane lining of the respiratory tract, including the sinuses.) By nature, jelly-like mucin absorbs water. When present in normal amounts, mucin is not a problem. But in excess, the hydrophilic (water-loving) mucin can cause serious problems wherever it accumulates in the connective tissue. Over half of the hypothyroid population (55%-60%) has abnormally high amounts of mucin, which accumulate more with age. In fact, the medical term for "hypothyroidism" used to be myxedema.

> "Medical textbooks about hypothyroidism state that myxedema is thyroprival (pertaining to or characterized by hypothyroidism) and pathognomonic (specifically distinctive and diagnostic). Translation: if the thickened skin of myxedema is present, you have hypothyroidism. Normal skin is relatively thin, and you may easily lift it with your thumb and index finger. If you look, you’ll find a number of people whose skin is almost impossible to lift. This is due to the marked swelling and glue-like infiltration of mucin in the skin and underlying tissues that result from hypothyroidism. Women’s skin usually has slightly more subcutaneous fat than men. Hence, their skin tends to be thicker. There are many different degrees of myxedema.
>
> Unfortunately, even if your skin is of normal thickness, you may still have hypothyroidism. It is only one of many signs of this condition. This diagnosticclinical finding has been forgotten, usurped by the almighty thyroid blood tests. Mucin is a normal constituent of our tissues, and its accumulation is often increased with hypothyroidism. However, the accumulation of mucin may only affect the internal organs and tissues and spare the skin. You may have hypothyroidism despite having normal skin."
>
> Excerpt from Forumotion - "Myxedema"

### sooperb — 2014-03-27T18:58:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-118330>

Squatting might help with the quads and the hamstrings. I've been trying to do it as often as I can (i.e. when no one is looking lol) and I'm amazed at the difference. When I started I couldn't lift my leg up at right angles using a towel, now I find that I can, until I wobble. In fact I was so surprised I wobbled with the shock. There's a facebook page (fancy that!) where they are doing the 30 minutes a day squat. You don't do it all in one go but work up to squatting for 30 minutes once a day for 30 days.

I like simple stuff and I was chuffed to find that I could do it, not for the full time yet, without having to prop myself up. Might be worth a try, I find it quite relaxing.

### nonchalant — 2014-03-27T22:49:05+0200

Squats do wonders for my lower back.

### endless — 2014-03-29T02:33:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-118599>

I'm a pretty good squatter....I sit in a really low squat sometimes, until my feet start to cramp up! What bothers me is sitting in a chair with both feet on the floor. I always haul one foot up onto the seat or tuck one leg under. Obviously I don't wear skirts much

Unfortunately the great way I felt after the massage was wiped out yesterday by my workday. I had an 8 hour install to do and was pretty sore and tired afterwards, my lower back aching. Wish I could have squatted in the car for the hour's drive home! Not only that, I stopped and picked up gluten free pizza for dinner and ate too much of it, LATE, because of poor planning. I knew I'd be working late and I should have brought something. On the other hand, I had two kombucha bottles of vitamin C water with me so didn't have to drink random water from the mall. Enjoyed the HELL out of my pizza (I've almost completely stopped dairy so the cheese was a rare treat) but felt like crap afterwards. Today I'm making up for it by eating light. I have a bunch of green bean prosciutto bundles and some raw elk in the fridge, so that's dinner for sure.

### caroline — 2014-03-29T06:21:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-118621>

Krista - do you eat the hearts and liver raw?

### endless — 2014-03-30T16:47:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-118818>

Yes, I love raw heart. The liver, not so much, but I partially thaw it and dice it up into pill-sized cubes, and freeze in a ziploc bag. I take 1-2 oz out and just swallow it with lemon water when it thaws. I have lots of liver, I got 6 livers when I bought lamb meat last fall, and I'm still working on the last venison heart. I think I've used up all the heart, though....

I bought a new pair of compression pants yesterday....if I can't do much with my upper body at least I can start running a bit. Have to do it sometime!

### caroline — 2014-03-31T07:27:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-118952>

How do you eat the heart krista? Just as is ? Or with evoo and balsamic?

### sooperb — 2014-03-31T12:00:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-119020>

I tried a bit of raw heart yesterday, I was cutting it up for my daughters dog! They were all aghast as I sliced off a bit and chewed it. It wasn't at all how I thought it would be, not in the least unpleasant so another to add to my list to try more of.

### endless — 2014-03-31T23:39:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-119122>

Good job! I first tried it after Inger wrote in her journal about it way back when....I like to slice it thin (easier when partially frozen and/or with a very sharp knife) and drizzle a bit of nice olive oil or nut oil and some lemon juice and fresh ground pepper. I've used a bit of fresh raw onion too. Once I even bought a whole beef heart from the grocery store and rendered all the hard fat around the top in the crockpot....it made the most yummy, beefy-smelling lard!

I finished the last of my raw marinated elk today....thought it might have gone off a wee bit but hard to tell because I used fish sauce in the marinade. Ate it anyway. Hopefully my gut is strong enough to handle it if need be!

Pretty good day so far, I got my blood drawn for the thyroid panel and the RT3 which I am ridiculously excited about since I've been looking for it for so long. Told the doc I was uncomfortable with the CT scan so he referred me to a neurologist for (hopefully, sometime this lifetime) an MRI. Squeezed in for an adjustment at the chiro and made another appt. for next week for laser therapy with him. I also bought a head traction device at the drugstore that I think will be helpful and cheaper than decompression therapy. Hopefully I can get this under control sooner than later!

### endless — 2014-04-03T04:25:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-119545>

So the doc that they scheduled me for the 'meet and greet' with was NOT what I was hoping for......so frustrating when the guy I saw at the walk in was the one I want to get in with! I was totally honest with him and he, to his credit, told me that he wasn't into BHRT or anything resembling functional medicine....so I think I may give him a pass as my new 'family doctor'.

Oh well, I guess I'll just stick with the walk in clinic and hope I can keep getting lucky and hitting the good doc's shift.

In other news, I seem to have misplaced 3 pounds all of a sudden....wish I knew how so I could repeat it 7 or 8 more times!

### endless — 2014-04-06T07:20:07+0300

I've been wanting to get a companion for my dog because she's all alone when I go to work, but I was thinking of an older dog. I really don't relish the thought of training a puppy! I couldn't resist, so I guess we'll muddle through somehow. Hopefully my dog isn't upset about it. He's coming in 3 weeks.

### caroline — 2014-04-06T09:57:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-120018>

### sooperb — 2014-04-06T16:20:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-120052>

I wish you luck! After spending a week with my daughter and her Parson's terrier who is 9 month's old, I would prefer an older dog any time! He has a learning curve of a straight line. He loves to try and hump other dogs, either sex he's not fussy, is deaf to all voices telling him to stop, come back, sit down etc. He's just been castrated but it hasn't made much difference. Yes he's adorable at times, not enough to make me like him though.

I think my dog would love a companion. He's around 8 and we got him when he was 6.5. He's a border collie, very well trained but so gloomy. When my other daughter turns up with her blue merle collie it's like he is switched on, ears up, eyes bright and they have such fun together. Hubby says no more pets though, with two cats and 3 hens he thinks that's enough to have to get minded when we want to go away.

### endless — 2014-04-07T05:34:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-120166>

Audrey, I've been looking at rescue dogs for months....nothing suitable has come up. Too bad, since I know in other areas there are so many. I have close neighbors with horses and chickens, and small kids. Every time I found one that seemed like they might be a good fit, there was always something...doesn't get along with cats, or needs a fenced yard. I finally decided on a puppy that I can train and will grow up around all those things. He's a pug boston terrier cross.

Sooperb, my dog is a blue heeler and she is the same....lights up when there is someone to play with. I've been feeling bad for awhile now about her solitude when I am not home, so hopefully this is the answer.

### caroline — 2014-04-08T06:20:39+0300

### sooperb — 2014-04-08T13:38:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-120363>

Just been watching a 9 week old heeler puppy on youtube, wow, I'm impressed with how much it can do even at that age. Dogs are like children, they need constant training and supervision. Alas, most people train their dogs just like they train their children, hardly at all. There seems to be some sort of belief that kids will just teach themselves how to behave, what's dangerous and what isn't, what's socially acceptable and what isn't, they are expected to just pick it up as they grow. Most don't, hence problem children and problem dogs.

### endless — 2014-04-09T19:31:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-120641>

I'm very lucky with my heeler. I was living with my ex when we got her together, and when we split up she was only 4 months old. I had been the primary trainer up until that point, but I sadly left her with him because she is deaf, and I thought she would be better off with the other, healing heeler he already had. I didn't get her back until she was a year old. She's remarkably good considering I had no idea how to train her and went on instinct. I think this new one will require a lot more diligence.

In other news, my weight has finally started to drop (yay!), but I think I have an ovarian cyst. Im only on day of my cycle and have had consistant 'ovulation type' throbbing in my left one for the past 2 days. I've never had ovarian pain outside of ovulation before. I know they are common in perimenopause, also wondering if my iodine protocol could be partly to blame since it apparently downregulates estrogen. I'm trying to get my ratio right, but I could still be low estrogen/ estrogen dominant. Will have to wait for the next set of labs to see where I'm at. In the meantime, castor oil packs.

### caroline — 2014-04-11T01:16:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-120881>

The whole iodine supplementation thing worries me .......I know Dr. K. isn't a fan .....it seems to me this is something we would need help with? My TSH went thru the roof ......my last labs everything is normal and my thyroid looks better than it has in a while ....I stopped the iodine
just my opinion of course!

### endless — 2014-04-12T07:28:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-121086>

Yes, I've heard that can happen. My TSH didn't rise, but apparently if it does it's nothing to worry about and it goes back down. I should be getting the RT3 results soon along with the rest of my thyroid tests so curious to see how they are. My temp is still a little low.

Guess I will ask for an ultrasound just to see what's happening in there. The ovary pain subsided and for the last 2 days I've just had the odd twinge, like I usually do around ovulation, except it's early. No other strange symptoms!

I have to make a quick trip to Vancouver to check in on my grandmother....going to drive down on Sunday and back on Thursday. I'll have to bring food and water, I refuse to drink chlorinated water. She lives in an assisted living facility and eats in the dining room and the food is horrendous (well, to people like us, anyway!).

### caroline — 2014-04-12T09:52:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-121089>

### endless — 2014-04-18T20:39:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-122413>

Hi Audrey....  I am about an 8 hour drive from Vancouver. Glad I drove through the pass fairly early yesterday, the snow level is low this morning! Hard to believe it's nearing the end of April.

The food is pretty institutional....overall my diet was drastically different. I couldn't really bring my own food like I had planned. My Oma would have been mightily offended if I didn't eat with her. I took her out for breakfast the first morning, so I could at least get eggs and bacon. Had to eat lunch there, my choice of 6 different sandwiches , or cottage cheese and fruit. I definitely have eaten more fruit over the last few days than usual! I managed to get away for one good meal...went to Earl's for a good salad with grilled chicken. Other than that it was pretty bad. I have to go shopping for veggies today, my fridge is bare. Think I'll pull out some salmon for dinner.

Got a lot done today....I was hoping not to have to go to work but did. I cooked myself a duck breast for dinner and I've concluded that I don't really like duck. Skyping with my ex who is in Thailand....he's telling me about how hot it is there and how cheap the massages are. Nice work if you can get it....

### endless — 2014-04-20T20:01:37+0300

I get a free dinner that someone else is cooking tonight, for a change! My landlord is hosting an easter dinner with turkey and all that jazz. I don't usually do anything for easter but I won't turn down the meal.... I'll most likely even eat some potatos and stuffing, since they're making gluten free.

Other than that, going to haul out the rototiller and give the 'working side' of the garden a quick fluff and get to work making some raised rows. I could plant peas and onions now, anyway.

### endless — 2014-04-26T06:04:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-123487>

Ugh. My car is broken down again  I just can't seem to get ahead these days. Good thing I have good food in the freezer and the garden ready to start at least! I'm going to have to lay off the supplements until I get caught up on bills and all that crap.

I'm going to bed early tonight and cuddle with my furry buddies.

### endless — 2014-04-30T19:19:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-124093>

Finally got my 'new' labs....taken in January so now out of date but my part time doctor was away all winter.

Good news is my vitamin D is finally where I want it....76! (191 in Canadian values). Other than that, not so great. My DHEA will NOT budge, it's still 3.6 (umol/L) out of a ref. range of 1.4-14.7, despite supplementation. My progesterone is still low, although it's been slowly drifting upwards over the last 2 years, it was a lot higher in November and then took a drop for the test in January. I stopped taking the oral progesterone, and went with just the cream, around that time. Maybe I should start back up on the capsules.

Ferritin is 21. I thought that seemed low, but my doc seems to think that if high equals bad (inflammation) then low equals good. I'm not sure, I need to look into it. I still think its low.

My free T3 seems to hold steady at about 4.5 (3.5-6.5) and free T4 at 12.5 (11.5-22.7) Still waiting for the reverse T3 I got drawn in March so I'm hoping for good news.

Testosterone is 1.7 (<2.6nmol/L) and testosterone bioavailable calculated is 0.5 (<1.0) Seems ok, but the fact that I still have zero sex drive suggests that it might not be.

I'm going to go lay outside naked in the grass. Screw the labwork. I'm just feeling 'off' today.

I'm not able to get in the river as much these days because the runoff has begun and the river is coming up quickly. It's pretty fast and much colder! Weather is still kind of cold and there's been a lot of rain the last few days. I can't wait for more sun, I want to get out in the garden and get my feet dirty!

So I was looking back on my labs since April/12, and my P/E2 ratio has gone from 14 in April 2012, to 110 in April 2013, to 585 in November 2013, and back down to 165 in January 2014. I think going off the oral pg might have been a mistake. I think I'll go back on it for awhile and test again....

Lazy day....visited with my sister who as here for a quick visit from Denver, went for a walk with the dogs and had a quick dip in the river, and some reading. I had more grandiose intentions but my body seemed to want a rest day.

### endless — 2014-05-09T21:46:08+0300
