Cycle day 12 yesterday and I could feel my left ovary kicking up a fuss.....started my prog. cream for now until I get in to the pharmacy.

I upped my training time yesterday to 50 minutes (interval running). My legs were VERY tired last night. I used the foam roller a lot and had a short, hot epsom salt and baking soda bath before bed. Not bad today, although I wouldn't want to do it again. Maybe some upper body.

It's cool and rainy and damp, my least favorite weather for getting things done outside.

### caroline — 2014-05-09T22:59:41+0300

### endless — 2014-05-10T19:07:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-126203>

Yes, I am....my brother is going to do it with us this year, I'm excited about that! He's never been very physical, this will be a great way for him to feel proud of fitness instead of avoiding it.

I NEED the sun to come back!!! It's very unsettled and cool. Yesterday I drove through snow over one of the passes. I'm just craving the warmth. Much easier to get into the cold river when it's sunny out, too.

My puppy is 9 weeks old today! I'm glad he will be getting more independant, this is challenging for me, someone who has never had kids. Yesterday I had to leave him for 4 hours for work, and he didn't handle it well. Came home to a big mess, and had to wash everything including the dog. Don't want to push him too much though, the last thing I want is to create separation anxiety! I'll take him with me today if I have to.

STILL no rt3 results....I'm patiently waiting for a phone call. In the meantime I will keep on keeping on and hope things are improving!

### caroline — 2014-05-10T23:21:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-126220>

I thought the best thing for puppies is a crate? we used to have a ton of friends who were vets....

### Marie — 2014-05-10T23:31:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-126223>

Endless,

4 hours is WAY too long for a pup of that age. The general rule is 1 hour per month of age up to a max of 8 hours. If you can't get there to take him out, pay someone to stop by while you are gone to get him out. It doesn't take much to create SA and takes a lot of work to fix it.

Never set your dog up for failure.

Marie

### endless — 2014-05-12T19:06:20+0300

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-127108>

I use a small pen, which contains an open crate and his bed, plus toys, water, pee pads and hot water bottle. I would never leave him in a crate if there was the possibility of me being delayed....at least this way he can pee and move around. I'm not crate training, but he has one in his environment that he plays in an is comfortable with in case he ever needs to be confined in it.

I was doing very well there, I cut out cheese entirely in December when I quit drinking alcohol. I still use goat milk kefir in smoothies and pasture butter, but lately some cheese has crept back in and I think I need to cut it out again. I feel better without.

Yesterday someone on FaceBook posted a bunch of free elk meat, cleaning out a freezer, so I went and got it. Too old for me to eat (2006) but it'll be great dog food. I got about 40 pounds for free. I love the free network on FB, so much stuff getting passed around that would otherwise be tossed out.

I have 6 pounds left to drop to be at my 'mini goal'. Not actually trying that hard, but I've decided to not drink any booze until either I hit 145lbs or the post-mudder beer, whichever comes first. Either way it'll be a six month break. I've saved a ton of money on wine!

Krista... I have become very aware of the risks with man made supplements, I can tell I have suffered ENOUGH from them now

I am done with it! I will leave the biohacks to Dr Jack.... I just do not want weird pimples, weird moods.... weird dry skin.... anything of it anymore

I just do some magnesium here and there when I remember.. and some PQQ when I am way too lazy... and a coQ10 once in a while.... but all of them not regularly. I am just so disappointed on supps to be honest. I think they easy just messes you up. I so believe in it when Jack says - if man made it, do not take it!
My last try with chlorella was no fun in the end.... now, I rather just eat wild greens and herbs and seaweeds. Way better

I have MSM too that I got me long ago but I just too it a time or two and lost interest... I eat so much raw onions it must be enuf.. I now use the MSM on my skin when I burn myself on nettles - what happens about daily.....

### endless — 2014-05-28T00:28:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-128616>

Thanks for the feedback! I didn't notice anything the first time I tried it either......just reading a lot about it on the magnesium FB group and thought I'd give it a try. I use DMSO topically for muscle pain and it doesn't give me any trouble, so not sure if the palpitations are related. I have actually overindulged in dark chocolate lately a few times, maybe that's it.

Inger, my diet is good, but nowhere near as good as yours, so I am still using supplements for now.  As I find good whole food substitutes that I feel comfortable with I am slowly getting off them.

Speaking of diet, I made a truly awful smoothie this morning....ran out of parsley so used cilantro. Bad idea, as it does NOT go well with raw cacao, almond butter and cinnamon. I threw nettles in there too, which is also pretty strong flavored. Think I'll keep the strong greens out of that combo, and save them for the ones with berries and acerola powder.....might be a better combination.

### endless — 2014-05-29T07:12:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-128781>

Thanks Audrey, will take a look at that.

So I went to the neurologist today and had nerve conduction testing, because of the numbness and weakness in my arms and hands. She said I have impaired Ulnar nerve function, and gave me a handout on Ulnar Neuropathy. I'm trying to become more aware of positioning so I'm not putting pressure on that area of my elbow. Have to start putting my arms in when I CT in the river...often I leave them out.

Still eating from last year's garden, while waiting for this one to get going. Tonight I had spaghetti squash and meat sauce with local venison, my own onions, garlic, swiss chard, peppers and tomato sauce. That was the last squash and jar of tomatos. I still have garlic and chard in the freezer but everything else is gone...now gotta wait for the new crop to grow. I bought a pound of beautiful organic strawberries on the way home on a whim...I rarely eat strawberries but I ate the entire thing! Enjoyed it thoroughly but now I am too full, and craving mineral water.

### caroline — 2014-05-29T07:48:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-128788>

I loved strawberry season .....

### endless — 2014-05-29T19:26:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-128855>

I've never been a big strawberry fan.....fruit in general, really. My favorites have always been peaches and blueberries, but I really don't eat fruit much at all. Yesterday I must have needed something in those strawberries, though!

I'm realizing how much I position myself with my ulnar nerve exposed to pressure, whether sitting or lying down. No wonder it's compromised. Tried last night in bed to NOT rest my hands on my stomach like usual, but keep my arms extended at my sides. Not easy to change habits! I had a restless sleep because of it. Didn't help getting an emergency call at 5am and leaping out of bed and out the door.....I get an instant adrenaline rush when the fire radio goes off...at least it was close to my usual wake time and not the middle of the night.

FINALLY, today I have an appointment to get my thyroid results from 2 months ago.... for all the good it'll do me.

So the doc who did the RT3 ordered me a bunch of other tests to see where I'm at, mostly the hormones that I've done fairly regularly, but he also wants me to do a saliva panel at the same time....only problem is, it will cost me $300 and I am really strapped this month. ### endless — 2014-07-07T08:40:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-133353>

I have been SO HUNGRY lately, it's disturbing....eating non stop. I made a big meal of lambs liver with onions and mushrooms and bacon with baked sweet potato and ate that for 2 meals in a row, then some elk burgers and now I have lamb curry in the slow cooker. I need to make time for a seafood meal! i have some squid in the freezer, but not sure what to do with it....

Yes, I am enjoying it Inger! I have amazing parsley plants, so every morning I am making a blender drink of about 1 cup of parsley, 2 cups spinach and some beet kvass and lemon juice...it's so good!

### endless — 2014-09-16T06:09:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-141135>

Hi Hope,
I don't really have a recipe....I usually just use lamb stew meat, onions, cauliflower and/or green beans, sometimes some kale, and throw in coconut milk and spices (red curry paste, garam masala, etc.) I actually don't have Jack's cookbook, I should look into getting that I guess. I love curry and lots of my meals tend towards that type of spice.

Just got a line on some grass fed beef liver, on Facebook. It's amazing what I've been able to source on social media! I'm going to pick up about 10 pounds tomorrow.

I'm making progress in the garden, got all the dead tomato and squash plants cleared away today, nothing left in there but the carrots, beets and kale, and the flowers. The 'fallow' side is tilled and ready for next year, I just have to decide where I want the garlic and get that bed prepped for planting next month. The weather has been nice, so I've been trying to get down to the river for a swim most days. The water is getting colder, it finally is feeling almost like CT again.

### endless — 2014-09-19T04:45:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-141371>

I joined a bunch of local groups...the usual buy and sell, free groups, and also farm/homestead type groups. That's where I sourced my lamb last year too. I've managed to get a bunch of old free meat that people are throwing out when they clean out their freezers too, and make cat food out of it.  I think my last 3 purchases are the beef liver, an area rug and 6 beef tails. Chopped the tails up with an axe and made dog bones. I'd have made bone broth but I couldn't verify the source of the cows.

Just finished eating some of the liver...it's so good! I make it with diced bacon, chopped onion and mushrooms, and add gluten free worchestershire sauce and balsamic vinegar to the pan to make a bit of sauce.

### endless — 2014-09-21T03:31:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-141487>

Yes, it's amazing the practical uses for social media!

It was a beautiful hot and sunny day, so I celebrated the last official day of summer by spending a couple of hours at the river. I lay down in the river on a big flat rock so I was all underwater, but the sun could still penetrate. Lovely! Grounding and CT in the sun. The water is still not TOO cold, probably about 65. But I realized that the reason CT is so hard for me to maintain in the winter is because I'm doing it in my somewhat dark and gloomy bathroom instead of outside in the sun. I'm going to be using my CFB/CGB combo a lot, I think, unless I finally get my shit together and get a stock tank and heater for it to put outside.

### Inger — 2014-09-21T09:05:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-141501>

I cannot be in water any other way than naked anymore it seems..... or it feels way weird!  like swimming with clothes on

Krista, you could lit candles... listen to music.... sing... or anything else that makes you tub time in winter magic
and maybe do shorter baths, maybe more often, and keep it cooler at home instead? Like change it up a bit and see what is perfect for you
or instead of baths just roll in the snow every day for a minute
It is great there are so many possibilities to do CT  Your river CT sounded amazing!
The CFB/CGB is a great way to CT too I am sure....

### endless — 2014-09-24T00:24:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-141770>

Not naked that day, as it was the weekend and there were a few others around. Some days I go in naked there.

I've tried that, Inger....I do nice WARM baths at night with candles, but I don't CT at night, only in the day. I just hate being in there in the daytime. It might be different somewhere else, this room is just very gloomy in the day. I'd much rather be outside. I do roll in the snow when the snow is soft, too.

I would love to go to the river today too, but I'm staying home with my pup. He got bumped hard by a car coming down the driveway last week, and has still been in a lot of pain. I took him to a dog chiropractor today and he had a lot of work done, now he needs to be quiet and calm for the rest of the day, so that means I do as well. I will head out to the garden and do some more clean up instead.

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2014-10-03T11:22:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-142671>

> [endless said:](/goto/post?id=76535)
>
> I didn't get my appointment this morning...there was a mixup. I did get my latest labs to review and will try to get in later today. The Vitamin D is not there, hopefully she can call for the result before my consult on Sunday.
>
> Not much exciting....my DHEA has risen from 2.1 to 3.5 in 3 months....still really low!
>
> TSH is down slightly again...2.93 in April, 1.25 in September, 2.28 in November and 2.08 now. Seasonal, maybe?
>
> IGF-1 is 199 (range 116-298). Never tested that before so I have no idea if it's good bad or otherwise.
>
> Sex hormones all lower than last time, despite supplementation...even T, which I have not been taking. Could be because I tested on day 21 but my cycles were going off the rails and I bled a few days later....
>
> Cholesterol the same, but HDL is higher and LDL is lower. Triglycerides slightly higher.
>
> I hope to get on the right track again after my consult with Dr. K! I probably won't have another for quite awhile so I have a lot to ask.
>
> Off to work.

if you find the router IP you can type it in the browser and disable wifi while you are plugged in
it is usuallys something like 192.168.1.1
go there in your browser and look in the menus for wifi, you can turn it off that way

### endless — 2014-10-06T07:36:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-142937>

Time sure gets away from me between posts here....
The weather has been pretty nice lately, so I am trying my best to take advantage of the sun. I find my body craving the light and heat, even though it's probably too late in the year for much vitamin D synthesis. Today I only had an hour or so in the yard....worked on my garlic bed as I will be planting it soon.

Had to attend a firewood accident this morning...it was quite a distance and luckily the person had an ex paramedic living next door or he'd have been dead when we arrived. Thanks to a well placed tourniquet and a helicopter he will likely keep his leg. A good reminder that felling trees is ALWAYS a two person job.

My pup seems to be healing....I have been on the fence about getting xrays of his hip (almost $400), since it seems that even if it is fractured it is minor and likely there would be no treatment anyway. His next chiro treatment is tomorrow and I will re-evaluate then. Since his last appt, I've given him 2 doses of Ruta graveolens 30C, followed by one dose of Symphytum officianalis 30C, magnesium 100mg twice a day, and a natural dog remedy for inflammation and he's better than he was last week.

I have TWO ex-partners staying
with me right now! I'm not sure what it is about me that attracts that but I have one sleeping on the porch and one on the living room floor...
 And I've still been celibate for 5 years.....

### Inger — 2014-10-06T08:44:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-142949>

well... maybe one could sleep with you in your bed.. so you would get some electrons?  My ex always does that  and we hold each other tight and it feels very good

Hi all....I suck. Been busy, totally falling off every wagon I can find, and feeling it. I'm about to FINALLY have my house back to myself, FINALLY have someone hired to train so I can have some work relief, and FINALLY get serious about keeping to my protocol.... I've been craving epi-paleo and CT but haven't had the time or the space to do it. Next weekend is the last 'fling' before I get firmly back in the saddle for winter. I just got a new computer too, and getting it set up has taken some of my usual lurking time. Hope everyone is well and I will be back for real soon!

### fitness@home — 2014-11-15T14:53:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-147443>

### Lahelada — 2014-11-16T01:45:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-147468>

### endless — 2015-02-15T02:27:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-155659>

\*\*sneaks back in......\*\*

I think my brain has finally started functioning again... I have a part time relief worker trained and am finally getting a few days off here and there. I climbed back on the wagon and have been strictly alcohol and sugar free, eating mostly keto and feeling more energy and less bloating. Missing the occasional glass of red wine but I'm saving it as a reward for when I reach a weight goal I've set for myself.

Spring is here early! My driveway is a mud bog and the snow is disappearing from the yard fast. Today was probably 10C midday...went with my sister in law for a long walk with the 4 pups and stopped for a river CT on the way back. THAT'S still really cold. I was lobster red in 30 seconds. It's relief to be able to CT outside again though and not have to run in immediately.

Starting into The Fourth Phase of Water this weekend...it's been in my 'to read' stack for awhile but now that I'll be going to his and Jack's talk in Vancouver next month I'd better get up to speed! I am also criminally behind in the blogs. I'm hoping to get back to silver membership sometime this year, I've really missed having webinar access but finances dictated I cut back. I'm excited that I was able to swing the ticket to this event at least.

Decided that Tough Mudder will not be happening this year....too expensive although I will miss hanging in Whistler for a few days. My sister in law's mother is wanting us to sign up for a little 5k mud event with her in Kelowna which is much cheaper and will be fun so I'll probably do that instead. Far less training required too which is a bonus.  I was JUST getting into xc skiing on the trail that runs all along the river near me and the big melt hit! Short ski season for me this year....and now we're in that interim phase of slop and muck that isn't conducive to outdoor recreation at all. Hopefully it'll dry up fairly quickly ao I can get on my bike.

### caroline — 2015-02-15T02:41:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-155662>

So glad you are back Krista! Great that you are going to that event ...and of course you already know that we will need lots and lots of pics!!!

How are you doing/feeling??????

### endless — 2015-02-15T19:09:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-155688>

Doing pretty good Audrey! I was feeling terribly run down and fat and achy, but since I've cleaned up my act and got some help I'm getting back to normal.  Still single though....just celebrated (?) my 6th V-Day in a row that way..... Had a good time though, my SIL and I went out to a burlesque show at a local club. It was fun, and we are both not drinking so I was able to relate to someone else who wasn't tipsy! I stayed clean on my diet all day too, so not feeling crappy this morning.

### endless — 2015-02-16T01:05:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-155724>

What a beautiful day! Took another long sunny walk with the dogs on the river trail, and stopped for a dip. It's so nice when you can get out and warm up naked in the sun! The snow is melting very fast so there was lots of sand and dry grass to stand on. Kind of surprised the two guys in the canoe though...

### caroline — 2015-02-16T01:53:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-155725>

The snow is melting very fast so there was lots of sand and dry grass to stand on. Kind of surprised the two guys in the canoe though...

you gave those guys a super duper Valentine treat! YAY Krista!

I love your new pic! How many rodeos have you been too????

### endless — 2015-02-16T02:14:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-155726>

Rodeos? Not a one...that is actually a vintage Canadian sealskin hat that used to belong to my grandfather. I was trying to get up the nerve to wear it in public.

Yes, I finally found someone to train as a relief worker so I can have some days off! Took awhile as it is part time and not an easy position to fill. I was getting very run down working every day.

I am eating ketogenic these days. Not strictly epi-paleo, although I try for a seafood meal once a day. That is what works best for me, and always has, but I indulged too much this summer after Tough Mudder was over and I was working too much and it led to a full on meltdown. I was eating all sorts of crap (well, for me...) I even ate doughnuts one day. Gluten, sugar, booze, french fries.....I had myself a good old time for awhile there! Then I discovered I had gained 25 pounds and felt like shit so I put myself into ketosis and stayed there. I'm 10 pounds back down so far and the CT and diet are doing the trick. I have set a weight loss goal and when I reach it I will allow myself to add some red wine back in, and fruit in the summer, but I have to stay away from grain, period. It's like an addiction if I give in.

### caroline — 2015-02-16T02:44:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-155735>

that is an awesome hat! we were just up the coast in Queensland and now I am the proud recipient /owner of a cowboy hat! \[straw\]

Have you been adding lots more fat to your diet? Fruit is killing me this time of year ...mangoes cherries, plums, peaches .....

### endless — 2015-02-16T02:50:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-155737>

Not really....I'm not restricting fat by any mens but I'm not deliberately adding a lot of fat. I drink a cup of Chaga turmeric tea with coconut milk every day, and I'm cooking with fat I rendered from a beef heart, or ghee. My protein and fat macros are roughly equal, and trying to stay below 25g of carbs. I have broken the sugar habit once again, but I did have a short craving for an apple today when I smelled them in the grocery store. Looking forward to some fruit in the summer!

I'm going to bed early tonight. I was full asleep at 12:30 last night when we got a structure fire call so I was awake and in bright light for about 45 minutes (I didn't have to go to the fire so I came home). I didn't feel very rested this morning.

### endless — 2015-02-17T18:52:28+0200

Another brilliant sunny day I will try my best to get in the river. I must take advantage of these opportunities now before the runoff starts and the water is too high.....right now we are still at winter levels and it is unusual for the air temp to be this warm in Feb. so it's bonus outside CT time.

I don't think I'm quite awake this morning...sprinkled vanilla powder on my bacon instead of in my chaga tea.

### endless — 2015-02-20T16:34:38+0200

As soon as they are able I'm going to do it again...obviously I have a goal to work towards.

I took my dogs to work with me today because I had to take a ferry and knew I'd have some wait time on the lakeside. We went for 2 nice beach walks, one on either side, and I got to sit in the sun and eat my can of sardines for lunch.  No CT, sadly, this time.

It was another beautiful sunny day outside but still cold....the ground is mostly bare but still frozen. Good for cleaning up dog poop but not much else in the way of yard work yet.

### endless — 2015-03-03T17:33:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-157303>

Yes, I sure can! Might take me a few days but I'll post it here.

I'm running around like an idiot today getting ready for my trip to the coast....and of course my vehicle decided to add a layer of challenge to that plan by needing an expensive fix before I can go. I'm grateful that my mechanic is squeezing me in today and that my co-worker is letting me use his van so I can still leave tomorrow. I just have to absorb the financial hit...at least I've already paid for my ticket to the symposium on Saturday....

### endless — 2015-03-11T18:33:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-158225>

Well, the trip was a mixed blessing....a difficult few days hanging out with my grandmother. Her dementia makes it difficult to be around her as she is one of those very negative people to begin with.  I found it stressful and was relieved to leave Saturday morning to head downtown for the day of quantum fun! Had a great time at the symposium, all three speakers were great and I met some of the other forum folks. Got to enjoy some social time with Jack and the rest, with oysters and red wine of course! Then had a nice relaxing Sunday spending time outside in gorgeous weather before heading home.

I came down with a wicked head cold on the way home.....first one in about a year. Wondering if it's partly due to extra stress, and I suspect it has something to do with the whey protein I took to use for breakfast. I've been considering giving it up completely since it seems to irritate my sinuses. I took enough with me to use for breakfasts, as I was staying at the facility where my grandmother lives and had to eat some things I normally wouldn't. I did it to keep the peace, mostly...she was already irritated with me for having my protein shake for breakfast, I think breaking out a can of sardines at the table for lunch would have been too much. So I bit the bullet and ate what they served....luckily had a really nice lunch of seared raw tuna and roasted brussels sprouts at the conference, and then oysters later with Jack.

I dragged my ass outside yesterday morning and layed on the dead grass in the garden, trying to ground and soak up some sun. Pretty low energy though. I'm snacking on sockeye roe this morning, trying to give myself a boost.

Interestingly, my diet shift has had immediate positive digestive effects. Before I went I was eating keto, but not super heavy on the seafood, maybe one meal a day. I was eating a fair amount of eggs and a bit of raw dairy. Feeling pretty good but definitely not getting a good 'Bristol score' most of the time. Since I've been home, 4 of my 5 meals have been scallops, shrimp and mussels in coconut milk with turmeric, with a bit of roe and avocado. The only other thing I've eaten is some almonds and one apple. Back to a solid 4, how about that?

I posted these on FB but here's a couple from the day....shoot, it says the file is too large. I don't know how to change that on this Mac, will have to wait.

### caroline — 2015-03-12T04:21:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-158324>

So glad you went to the conference and had an awesome time Krista!

I am loving the turmeric paste ....seems to be making my body work better!

### endless — 2015-03-13T18:47:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-158457>

Still sick....this must be making up for all the colds I haven't had over the past year or so. Not getting any sleep which is the worst part. This morning I finally forced myself into the cold tub. Not as cold as usual, I could only do 55 and up to the boobs. Still didn't feel up to doing my upper torso. Felt good, though. I'm waiting for it to warm up a wee bit before I go outside for my sun.

Reading the new blog post made me realize that although I've been fairly good about the blue light thing at night, I still use my computer and phone a lot during the day. I guess I didn't think of that as being as big an issue. I changed the settings on my flux to keep it at sunset levels during the day, and I'm going to hunt down one of those orange cling films that Ruben had on his phone in Van....I look at my phone a LOT for work all day. I'm also going to start sourcing a more fashionable set of lenses for nights out...uvex is great for home but I liked the clip ons Jack has.

Still haven't forgotten the fermented salsa, just waiting to speak to the person who made it.

### caroline — 2015-03-14T06:24:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-158494>

Hope you are feeling better Krista.....

### endless — 2015-03-14T18:12:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-158525>

I'm NOT! I hate this....I literally have not been sick for at least a year, possibly longer. I forgot how shitty it is. I've lost my voice and can't taste or smell anything. Might as well eat MHS for the next while since I can't taste anything anyway. I've been doing all I can (I think) to support my body in fighting it off. I will try another cold tub this morning. I'm never quite sure whether CT is appropriate during illness. If I was feeling chilled I would likely say no but today I feel mildly feverish so maybe it'll do me some good.

I'm going to have some scallops in coconut milk curry for dinner and try to hit the sack early. Maybe I'll be back to normal tomorrow!

### endless — 2015-03-15T20:04:17+0200

I didn't get a chance to pick up any of the fish I usually use for MHS (I use whole 'sardines' that are about 6 inches long, not just heads), so I think I will do a nice seafood soup instead tonight with some of my sockeye bone broth.

### endless — 2015-03-16T00:43:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-158602>

Thanks, Nicole...I hadn't looked at it that way. I hope I have the same result.

I skipped the CT today....had to get that damn dog out of here. Instead I loaded up my dogs when I took her home and spent an hour walking around outside in the rain with them, throwing the ball and stuff. Didn't make it into the river, shoes stayed on, but being outside was nice despite the cold damp day. Now my craving is for getting warm and possibly taking a nap.

### endless — 2015-03-17T05:40:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-158737>

I think I'm finally over the worst of this....feeling better today but energy still pretty low. This morning I felt like walking fast with the dogs but by 5pm I was wiped out.

Time to brew up a new batch of VIGR Chaga tea for the week....think I need to lay off the coconut milk for awhile though. I've been using it in the tea and one meal a day (usually my curried scallops) and wondering if it's too much. Starting to get cravings for more 'springtime' foods and less heavy stuff.

### endless — 2015-03-23T04:10:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-159253>

I guess it's a good thing, but I'm frustrated that I am not able to keep up to all the interesting conversations online.....here and on the Quantum FB group especially. I just don't have the time and then when I do it's time to get off the computer.

Anyhoo....worked today but I got lucky and got finished in time to make an event I wanted to attend. A neighbour hosted an EMF meeting/talk by a remediation specialist, similar to Michael Neuert, but local! We are in an unusual position here because there is still no cell coverage OR smart meters in the valley where I live. And a lot of people are willing to fight to keep it that way. In fact, the person who was presenting studies with the author of the book 'Dirty Electricity', Dr. Samuel Milham, who has agreed to come here in May to 'map' out the area pre-smart meter installation so we have a baseline and some proof later if needed of the RF pollution they will undoubtedly cause. I put my place down as a volunteer test site, I'm excited to get a reading of my area including ground current.
Today he went over basically the types of EMF and the main ways we are exposed, some remediation steps and demonstration of dirty electricity and readings after adding Stetzer filters to the circuit. I bought a couple of the filters to plug in to the worst spots in my home, where the electronics are plugged in. I'm interested in installing on-demand switches into the bedroom circuit, so that no power is present until you flick on a lamp or something, and then it energizes until you turn it off again. I don't think it would work with my current location but if I was building or renovating I'd sure consider it.

### endless — 2015-03-26T17:21:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-159477>

Tightening up on the light stuff and the electricity management has helped....I got lazy and was leaving the one bedroom circuit energized at night because I like to read before bed, but that line runs through the wall at the head of the bed. I can notice the difference when I shut it off. Using the red headlamp to read again and sleeping better. I need to invest in a new set of UVEX, mine are old and getting pretty scratched up. Also thinking about getting some of the Gunnars for daytime use. It will go on the 'wish list' for now.

### Inger — 2015-03-28T21:08:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-159678>

I use a candle if I want to read in bed - and the candle light even seems way too bright, uh
Red head lamp sounds great

Good you have tightened up on your fake light consumption...
I hate fake light  After 3 years of limiting it I just seem to hate it more and more.
I can feel how it stresses me.... how on earth can it be good.. it is kinda like poison

It should be illegal?

### endless — 2015-03-29T22:32:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-159769>

I was thinking the candle light seemed to bright too, Inger! Thought I was losing my mind. I think the red light is easier to read by because it doesn't flicker.

Got lots of fake light last night, I was helping a friend at a local wine tasting festival, pouring wine all night. It was way too bright in there, but I was not prepared for that, and wouldn't have felt comfortable wearing my clunky old UVEX anyway...I need something more socially acceptable for nights out!

I'm starting to get the garden cleaned up....garlic is all up about 2 inches, and I have chives and some volunteer spinach too. I have to build some raspberry supports now that I've cleaned out all the dead canes. Kind of cold and windy outside today so maybe I'll change the oil on the snowblower and put it to bed for the season instead. Time to drag out the rototiller!

### endless — 2015-04-04T05:04:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-160270>

I've picked out a spot for my outside CT tank....it gets sun in the morning till about noon (in the summer). I could put it in a place that gets longer exposure but my plan is that this will encourage me to get out in the morning for the sun. Not to mention that in the summer the water will be coldest first thing in the morning. In the late fall/winter I can empty it and move it onto my porch, which gets NO sun unfortunately but has an outlet so I can plug in a heater to keep it from freezing. And hopefully I will be spared CTing in my gloomy bathroom year round. Just have to get to the farmer's supply to get the tank ohm a day I'm not working so I have room in my car.

### caroline — 2015-04-04T06:04:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-160274>

### endless — 2015-04-09T05:02:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-160694>

Still haven't managed to pick up my tub....but I'm hoping to do it on the weekend. Tomorrow is supposed to be warm and sunny and rather than driving (unless I have to for work) I'm going to build a bed for my onions and get them planted. Maybe peas, too...

Some poor tortured soul committed suicide on the bench that overlooks the spot that I CT in the river last night. Sat down and shot himself in the head.  I walked down there today and burned some sage and left some beautiful fragrant cottonwood branches on the bench, and scattered some sand over the blood that they missed when they cleaned up. Took the dogs down to the beach and played like we normally would afterwards...wishing the person well on their way and focusing on the beauty and purity of the river.

I FINALLY found all my downloads of the old webinars! ### endless — 2015-04-14T12:48:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-161162>

My best buddy is missing.... Elvis showed up in my yard 5 days after we put down our cherished senior kitty who was dying of kidney failure. I had no intention of keeping him but he weaselled his way into my heart back then (10 years ago) and became my familiar, my best soul friend, and my constant companion. He has been there through the worst years of my life and more recently through some good ones. We live in the forest and predators are a fact of life....my animal companions are always taking a risk when they are outside, but I don't believe in 'keeping them safe' by restricting them to indoors only, because that is not their nature and they would be unhappy. I think my boy finally got unlucky in his venturing out last night...he's been missing for 24 hours now. I'm heartbroken but trying to remember that we are all just temporarily incorporated into these bodies....I hope to see him again someday in whatever way that happens......until then I will miss him like crazy.

### fitness@home — 2015-04-14T14:26:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-161169>

Awww, I'm sorry Krista. I hope he is okay and just took an extra long ramble outside.

We have an 18 year old Siamese that will leave an empty hole in my heart when he passes (hopefully has a few more years). Can't leave pets loose down here in my neighborhood. Our dogs have become backyard escape artists but I usually have to look no further than the first home they find kids outside at.

Let us know if he shows up...

### notsoperfick — 2015-04-14T15:28:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-161175>

Endless that's so thoughtful of you to respect someone's last place on earth and to mark it joyfully. I despair at the scenes of fatal accidents when people put up flowers and mementoes year after year, their loved one never lived there, they should celebrate and remember in a place they lived, seems a much more relevant thing to do to me.

I hope your cat is just momentarily entangled somewhere. I have a cat who took to leaving home every time there was something she didn't like, such as my daughter's dog coming to stay. Longest I didn't see her was nearly 3 days and then she came strolling up, hungry but otherwise fine. My Bengal on the other hand is a real homebody, he doesn't like the dog much either but gets as far as the garden across the road and then calls when he hears either of us outside, which immediately alerts the dog as to his whereabouts. I'm hoping he is back with you soon, Elvis didn't die he just went home

### endless — 2015-04-17T06:13:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-161486>

Thank you both. I find myself getting anxious each time I drive in to the driveway, hoping he will be back but so far he is still gone.  Realistically I doubt he will be back, he was not a young adventurous guy, and never missed breakfast if he could help it. I miss him so much...

Got some results for my last saliva hormone test today but I can't get the damn photo to upload because I still don't know how to use this stupid Mac! I don't know how to change the size of the file. Grrr.

### caroline — 2015-04-18T03:27:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-161581>

So sorry Ktista that your friend is missing .......maybe Elvis just went walk about? xoxo

### endless — 2015-04-22T03:55:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-162006>

No, I don't think so Audrey, although if he shows up I will of course be overjoyed....he and I were pretty tight and not only is is out of character for him, I don't 'feel' him anymore, if you know what I mean.
In spite of that his presence is everywhere here and I take comfort in that at least.

I need some help....I suspect that after a lifetime (so far) of great digestion I have developed GERD or silent reflux (maybe they are the same...). I've had several episodes of strange symptoms that feel like I am having an asthma or even a heart attack, very disconcerting. Oddly, they have ALL happened when driving over mountain passes over 5,000ft, not that I think that could have anything to do with it really....but on longer drives. I'm wondering if my body position could have anything to do with it. I start to feel like I have pressure on my throat and chest and breathing is difficult, and then my heart starts to pound and I feel shaky. That part could be anxiety triggered by the first part though., since it's always happened in the middle of nowhere where there is no cell service or help if I should need it.

Also coupled with that is a more or less constant uncomfortable feeling in my upper abdomen/sternum area. It's frustrating! I'm going to start with the assumption that for some reason I have low stomach acid and try taking HCL with meals to see if it helps at all. I do know that grain and sugar give me heartburn, but it's happening with everything lately.

### caroline — 2015-04-22T04:25:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-162013>

gall stones?

Please take care....xo

### endless — 2015-04-23T03:36:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-162151>

So obviously I need to fashion a better cover for my new CT tank....went to get in and there was a dead mouse in there... I had to empty and refill instead. I'll have to get a piece of 3/4 inch plywood that I can cut to shape. It'll keep the mosquitos from breeding in there too.

Spent all day in the garden yesterday hoeing up raised beds...I've got blisters on my hands and a sunburn....I am not at the place where I don't burn yet. I crave the sun so much that I couldn't bear to get out of it, even as I felt myself burning. It's from the winter of deprivation...next winter I will go back to tanning and won't be so desperate for it in the spring!

### caroline — 2015-04-23T06:37:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-162167>

Hi Krista ....How are you feeling?

You were hoeing naked - right??? xo

You are such a capable young woman ....when are the yurts going up?????

### endless — 2015-04-25T03:37:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-162360>

No, not naked this time because my mother and stepfather were stopping by and because my skin needs a gradual intro to the sun....

I am still wanting to build a little guest room outside, or maybe find an older travel trailer to use. I have a good spot to put it and it would be much easier to have guests if they didn't have to sleep on the living room floor! Mind you, soon I'll be setting up my sleeping porch outside again and guests can have my bed.

Still having the strange symptoms...but I'm trying some things to see if anything helps, including sipping a/c vinegar in water with meals.

### endless — 2015-05-16T06:34:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-164408>

Man, I just can't seem to get over here very often....

Starting where I left off though, I got my CT in this morning outside in the sun in my lovely new CT corner (which I STILL can't figure out how to post a pic of here from this Mac, since I don't know how to resize photos ) but I posted it on FB in the quantum group if anyone is interested. Followed up with sitting naked in the sun eating my breakfast of salmon row and avocado and followed by an hour of naked gardening! My neighbours are in Hawaii so I took advantage and let it all hang out. Usually I at least have a sarong on but today I went for the full monty. Covered up after that to avoid too much sun, I still burn if I overdo it.

Productive day otherwise, too. Changed the oil in my snowblower (finally) and my rototiller, filled my gas cans, fixed my lawnmower, planted some of the greenhouse and wired on the raspberry supports. Various other chores filling in the gaps. I still have at least half of the garden to plant, too, but got flowers planted last week so the porch looks nice at least. Bought myself a beautiful Mandarin Orange Azalea bush, i memory of my big orange kitty Elvis who is no longer physically with me. It's over by my CT spot so I can see it while I'm in the tub.

I went for a stress test at the hospital yesterday to check out my heart. No problems, at least as far as that test would show. The doc thinks that I am just one of those people who is hyper aware and can feel the extra beats that my heart throws off more than some other people do. I never thought about it, but apparently there's quite a variation of how aware of their body different people are. Anyway, I still think my problem is digestive in nature because it seems a lot better since I've been taking HCL with meals. I'm still not sure WHY it's happening but working on that.

I'm going to be in bed early tonight....I've had super early mornings all week despite wanting to 'sleep in' till 7 or so. Was up at 3am yesterday for a chimney fire, and by the time I got home it was 5:30 so no point in going back to sleep. My first thought was 'who the hell is burning their wood stove in the middle of May'? It's not even cold at night anymore.....

### caroline — 2015-05-16T11:20:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-164415>

Hi Krista .... You really had a big day! Have to check out your C/T pic.

Elvis has left the building?
xoxo

### endless — 2015-05-17T04:59:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-164494>

Hi Caroline,
Yes, sadly Elvis has left the building. I really miss him and yet I distinctly feel his presence around here still so it's bittersweet.

Got cabbages, kale, brussels sprouts and kohlrabi planted today, plus the peppers are in the greenhouse ready to go in. Tomorrow, schedule permitting, I will plant some bush beans and greens, and prep my tomato bed. I have a section I was going to grow a cover crop of buckwheat on, but the prediction is for a hot, dry summer and so I am thinking I might just cover it instead to save water. I bought some planting bags to plant my squash plants in and I can just set them on top of the covered area. And then of course I will still have all the mulching to do......good thing it's a healthy hobby. Grounded and in the sun. My back suffers a bit though.

I FINALLY saw a magnetico on Craigslist, but it's on the coast. If they reply I will see if they are willing to package it for shipping because it's a really good deal. Can't really afford it but it might be my only chance to get one. I'll leave it up to the universe...if I'm meant to have it I will.

My 11 year old nephew is spending the night tonight. Currently hanging out with my snake and playing on his computer. Severe technology addiction! I'm going to try and get him to play a board game or something after dinner, but I don't think I'll be awake long. I've been out on emergency medical calls every morning this week and my sleep has suffered.

### caroline — 2015-05-17T08:50:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-164501>

Hi Krista - so sorry about Elvis.

You are quite the farmer! I am very ,very impressed. Have you always enjoyed planting? Do you trade your stuff for stuff you don't have? Are you quite self sufficient? pickles etc.?

Now if you put up some yurts we can come and help you garden

Actually no, when I was a kid I hated helping my mother in the garden. Of course I was mostly picking rocks, so that didn't exactly get me fired up. I didn't get interested in it till I had my own garden.

Hi Inger!
Yes, it is a great way to get out in the sun and be barefoot. I grow for my brother's family too since I eat less veggies these days!
I sure hope I reach the stage where I don't burn at some point! I got quite red on my chest and arms yesterday because I was in the sun for 30 minutes in the cold tub, then another 3 hours or so in the garden. Today I used an umbrella for shade to let my skin recover.

### WereBear — 2015-05-20T03:50:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-164754>

endless, do you take supplemental Vitamin D3? It has helped me tremendously, and is supposed to increase one's sun exposure without burning.

Then again, I'm over forty.. when our natural ability to produce it with sun starts dropping off, I understand?

### endless — 2015-05-24T18:45:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-165209>

Hi WereBear,
I don't anymore....I did for while and my labs didn't budge upwards at all, even at 16,000 IU a day at one point! I tried a tanning bed instead and up went my blood levels....so I decided to stop the supplemental D3. I am 46, so I guess if that's true I should be seeing a natural decline. Then again, the most nut-brown, leathery people I see are usually over 60.

I also think it's partly about honouring my northern european heritage (all VERY white people!) and taking it slow. I don't use sunscreen anymore so I'm having to become more conscious of when my skin has had enough sun. This is the first spring that I'm really trying to stick to a routine (work permitting) of getting outside at sunrise and getting my CT and breakfast in in the early morning sun, and hopefully some naked gardening before the sun gets too intense. I need one of those hats with an umbrella sticking out of the top.

### WereBear — 2015-05-24T18:58:38+0300

I am so happy for you that you can do naked gardening! I have a similar very Northern European genetics; British Isles, Scandinavia, and some rumored Dutch for seasoning.

### WereBear — 2015-05-25T01:55:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-165249>

### caroline — 2015-05-25T02:20:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-165250>

I also used to always burn and not tan \[European heritage too\].... now haven't in four years.
People always tell me now that I am so lucky to have olive skin!

I think the biggest part of it is C/T .....and epi paleo of course!

### endless — 2015-05-25T04:56:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-165258>

I am definitely darker than I have been in years past by this time.....albeit with a slight reddish tone. I do pink up, especially on my chest and face, and then it fades into tan. I rarely to never peel. The funny thing is, year round, people are always asking me if I have been on vacation because they think I have a tan! I must glow....

I'm on the fence about skin damage too Nicole, my mom lives in Mexico 9 months out of the year and her skin is getting that thick, leathery, 'elephant skin' look. Mind you, she is not doing CT or eating epipaleo, but she lives at sea level and has other things going for her.

Naked gardening has been awesome, but my neighbours will be back from their holiday any day now and I will have to put something on. I will still CT and sit out afterwards in the buff because I can hide behind my house, but I'll have to throw on a wrap in the garden.

### nicld — 2015-05-25T14:44:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-165284>

My skin does the same thing, pinks up, especially on the chest. When I am warm it gets really pink. I even had to buy a darker shade of foundation and that has NEVER happened. Even when I went to Costa rica and got a little too much sun I really did not peel from the light burn but the skin did slough off. Now the hubby peeled from his burn and it was so gross.

I have one area that I can lay out nude too but when doing anything else I need to be covered up. Next place, walking around nude is going to be one of the biggest factors in picking the house, besides no towers near by and meter not on the house.

### endless — 2015-05-29T20:13:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-165852>

Neighbors are home....bummer. It's coinciding with some cooler rainy weather though so I'm not having the desire to get out there naked in the garden anyway.

Having a really hard time getting my CT tank cold now....I bought some blocks of ice but I would need to add several to get the temp below 60. Yesterday it was 70, and I put in one block and also 6 two litre pop bottles filled with ice, and that kept it at 60 for the 30 minutes I was in. I'd probably need a couple more blocks to get to 50. Too much money....my CT will have to be at 60 for the hot season!

I'm having trouble keeping all the balls in the air at once lately. Between work and life in general being busy, I never seem to get a full 'optimal' day in. I can have a great morning with CT, sun, seafood, etc and my day will go sideways and I end up coming home late from work and being too hungry to fast till morning so I am eating too late and getting light at the wrong time.I haven't managed to eat earlier than 8pm for at least a week. It's annoying! I need to order another pair of UVEX (or two) for starters...mine are old and trashed. Also really need to get something similar for when I am not at home. And order some more blue blocking film for my car instrument display. No wonder I keep putting off renewing my silver membership....every month I have too many other related expenses! Can't even buy a lottery ticket because I work in the industry....

Speaking of expenses, I have COMMITTED to buying the 20 gauss magnetic I found on Craigslist! Luckily the person selling is willing to wait for me to be able to arrange a couple days off to go get it, it's a days drive away. I was on the fence because it is a size smaller than my mattress, but after calling the company and speaking to Dr. Bonlie's wife about it I am going ahead. If and when I end up sharing the bed with someone else I will need to get the proper size, but for just me it'll be fine and it's 1/3 the price so I can't pass it up. That's an unexpected expense I don't mind!

### fitness@home — 2015-05-29T23:30:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-165861>

### endless — 2015-06-02T20:51:32+0300

If there was a lottery of some kind on the site with silver or gold memberships given away I would be playing every day!

### endless — 2015-06-04T07:09:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-166492>

My neck is really buggered.  Sunday I rototilled a 1000sq/ft section of my garden (after digging up a bunch of raspberry shoots that were in it), shovelled and hauled 10 loads of horse shit from my neighbour's place to my garden, spread it and covered the section with 6ml black plastic. I woke up the next day in excruciating pain. Not sure what happened. I went to the chiro yesterday afternoon but it's still really inflamed. It even hurts when I swallow. Thankfully I had a slow day today and have been resting and icing it. Wish I had the magnetic already!

It's still raining.....going on a week straight, basically. It's warm out, still, but I'm ready for the sun to come back.

### endless — 2015-06-05T06:21:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-166649>

I did that last night before bed, then iced it again....had a horrible night because moving once I lay down was almost impossible! I was wishing I had a rope hanging from the ceiling to haul myself up with...lol
Today it's migrated over to the left side and now feels like my first rib is out. I can't yawn or sneeze....it's bizarre. My body stops the process before the part where I'd have to fill my lungs completely. Of course I had to deinstall a 70 pound lottery ticket dispenser at the casino today too. So it's back to the chiropractor again in the morning and hopefully he can fix me up for the weekend. I have to mow my lawn, it's gone crazy after all this rain.

I've been really enjoying some of the conversations in the quantum group over on FB, but I think it's starting to deteriorate....maybe getting too big or something. It's too bad because it's really interesting how many tangents one conversation can go off on, and I learn a lot. I wish it was that lively over here!

Neck is getting better, after another chiro visit this morning and CT today. I'm buying blocks of ice and one block and my 6 frozen 2L pop bottles keep the tank at 56-58 for the 30-40 minutes I need it to. I need to figure out how to make my own blocks....

### prAna303 — 2015-06-06T11:44:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-166779>

> [endless said:](/goto/post?id=166649)
>
> I've been really enjoying some of the conversations in the quantum group over on FB, but I think it's starting to deteriorate....maybe getting too big or something. It's too bad because it's really interesting how many tangents one conversation can go off on, and I learn a lot. I wish it was that lively over here!

Since i do not use Facebook i do not know whats going on there but i have an idea.

Perhaps the ideas spread fast but what about thought and application? Also like many times before and it is speeding up everywhere, it goes heywire. Today we are so into understanding that everyone gets into it like crazy but forget to use it in real life. Instead we discuss things on levels that sometimes is to nerdy, some can talk math but others see it in pictures, others just live it out loud. We are so much searchers today, we want to live forever and be king of the throne but forget to use the sience we learn.

On Facebook you get a flamethrower kind of discussions, it is fast, instant and smack up your face. On a forum it is slower, more time to think, reflect. In real life it is thinking and doing at the same time. I tend to like forums better, like writing a real letter but with modern tools. In digital world it is easy to drown in the buzz, one can think a lot but forget to act.

> Also, some folks were critical of the lighter stuff here .... which, personally, I learn a lot of life lessons from.

Let them be critical, we need those who sometimes kick back, barefeet in the sun with a straw hanging from your lip. The deep science need deep mystics and weirdos who do not forget the big thing, to live.

What i done from november of last year is to use application more, sure i read some things in ones and zeros land but i sort it big time. I tend to not write as much, i can not use words for what i feel now, to narrow bandwith. Digging in books, one each day and finding it hard to like stuff there also. The only thing that gives me kicks now is humans in natural states, grass in morning sun and sea at afternoon.

There will always be a risk that Quantum things become the new Paleo but the science will still be there when the wind of trend has blown past it... Sometimes we just need some time to digest the information just like the light.

### endless — 2015-06-06T22:09:48+0300

Peter, you are right. I am trying to spend less time online so it's my time here that has suffered, when I should actually use my time more wisely and hang out here more. I just upgraded to gold (!) so am distracted by catching up on all the webinars I missed over the past year.

### endless — 2015-06-08T06:33:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-167007>

Interesting day. For one thing, I actually turned my phone OFF all day, for the first time in over a year. I had the day off, but because my relief worker is still fairly new I generally keep tabs on what's going on. Today I did not an it was awesome!
I just finished a 4 week experience of micro-dosing with iboga root. A family member became involved with a couple who have been working with plant medicines for several years (mostly treating addiction, which is what my family member was seeking help with). In the course of the (successful) treatment my family member decided that this is something he feels called to do as well and is now in training to help facilitate plant medicine treatments and ceremonies. I don't have addiction problems, but I do believe strongly in the ability of plant medicines to help us access areas of our subconscious and our spirits that are generally 'off-limits', and so participated in this trial for my own self exploration and growth and to try and work on some phobias I would like to get rid of. I was helpful to him as well for 'practice' before working on clients. I really enjoyed the connection with the plant medicine....setting intentions every time I ingested it, noticing what was coming up in dreams and during the day....it was a gentle corrective process, compared to the 'flood dosing' used with substance addiction that can be quite intense. Using something like that with intention is so wildly different than doing it recreationally, which I never got into.
Today, three of us who had been involved in this 4 weeks of exploration closed by sitting in ceremony and taking another plant medicine (a cactus called Peruvian Torch). I am a total coward when it comes to mind altering substances...I didn't even do acid in high school (I realize that neither did a lot of people, but I was in the group that did.) so wasn't sure what to expect. I was pleasantly surprised and we had a very pleasant day with waves of ...ultra-awareness, I guess. It was a nice way to close the experience and make me more at ease with the process....something I am working towards with the goal of participating in an ayahuasca ceremony, possibly even this year. Still nervous about that but know it would be immensely healing.

### endless — 2015-06-11T01:48:26+0300

Ugh, it has been SO HOT here lately, I feel like my core is overheated. Too busy to CT for the last 3 days too so that hasn't helped. I have been sleeping outside and still too warm. I'm going to be up early, since it is getting light at 4:30am now, and jump in the CT tub early tomorrow before it gets hot.

Reading Wim Hof's book right now. It's interesting but he is not really a writer. I keep feeling like I've missed something, he will be discussing an event or time and the next chapter is on to something else, leaving me with a lot of questions about the previous one. I want to know HOW and WHY he got into doing the cold stuff in the beginning, and so far there is no satisfactory explanation of that.

### endless — 2015-06-13T23:33:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-167993>

Had a good turnout last night to our 'smart meter resistance' meeting..... we are one of the last areas in the province to get them and there have been bullying tactics used by the company. Lots of people unsure what to do. After showing some clips from Resonance and Take Back Your Power, we heard from a retired electrician/activist about how to go about not giving consent, and from a guy in the okanagan who is involved in resisting the swaps there. Overall pretty good. I am still torn between registering for 'RF Off', and trying to just go for full on refusal of the swap. I'm risking disconnection if I refuse, and I don't have a backup to protect my two freezers, so I have to choose carefully. The guy from Kelowna said that in that area, people who have posted notices and locks on their meters are not being bothered, but the readers here are telling people that if the meters are locked the power will be disconnected. How much are they bluffing?

### endless — 2015-06-21T06:44:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-169117>

Thank you WereBear! I'm still not sure what I'm going to do.

Well, it's finally happened....I've gone off chicken. Back in 2012 I remember Jack saying he never ate poultry anymore except for wild duck, and while a bunch of people immediately followed suit, I didn't because I had good quality local chicken in my freezer. I don't eat a LOT of it, maybe once every 2-3 weeks. But the last 2 times I have found it distasteful. I have changed SOME stuff just because Jack did, but overall I like to see how things apply to my own situation first. I guess I'll be giving the remainder of my chicken to my brother's household. Good thing I still have salmon and elk in the freezer!

The pre-cruise bash must be in full swing by now....I'm sure everyone will have a great time on the trip. I would like to do something like that with this group of people, but honestly cruise ships have never been my idea of a good time. I'd rather camp on a beach.

### endless — 2015-06-21T17:51:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-169169>

Well, day one of trying my UFO light. I broke down and bought one online because they seem to be getting scarce and I wanted to have it around. I decided to use it on my abdomen today, so I just sat in a kitchen chair with it balanced on it's edge on my legs, about 8 inches away from my abdomen. That way the fan in the back is unobstructed. Did 15 minutes (fits in to the routine well, I can do my oil pulling at the same time..) and we will see if I notice anything! I might alternate, do my lower back tomorrow.

### WereBear — 2015-06-21T17:53:40+0300

### endless — 2015-06-24T03:55:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-169414>

Well, nothing earth shattering to report after two UFO sessions....I skipped a day in between so did my lower back this morning. Had to skip CT this morning too...sacrificed it to taking the dogs for a sunrise walk instead. At least I got my morning sunlight in. Having trouble with breakfast lately...I'm inclined to skip it but unless I get more organized I dare not because then I make shitty choices later in the day when I'm unprepared. This morning I went for the high DHA option and made a smoothie of raw whole herring with parsley from the garden, dulse powder and lemon juice. Lunch of raw cherries newly in season and dinner was raw elk, avocado and steamed broccoli with lots of butter. I HAVE to find another salmon roe source, really been missing it.

### endless — 2015-07-03T08:17:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-170490>

So, interestingly enough, I got a taste of what getting cut off from power would entail...we had a kick ass storm while I was away for 3 days and my freezers were fine. Go figure.

I just drove 1400km to buy and pick up my used 20 gauss magnetico! It was a whirlwind trip and I am bagged from all the driving, but I did get an ocean swim and a sushi meal out of it so all good. I am slightly concerned about the ability of my futon bed frame to hold up under the weight, but already working on solutions. Last night was my first night on it, and I honestly had a hard time getting to sleep and felt like I spent a lot of the night awake, but that might have been a combination of anxiety about my bed frame and the full moon. Reserving judgement for at least 6 weeks. It's too small for my bed but the area not covered is really easy for me to avoid.

### Psyche — 2015-07-03T14:54:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-170506>

You are doing great! As someone who is 4 weeks in on the leptin reset its tremendously helpful to read some one else's experience to date and compare notes.
I woke up at 3 am a lot the first week or so. It passed. I can relate to a lot of what you're going through.
I wanted to send you a note of encouragement and tell you to hang in there. You are making a ton of progress. The harder you work for something the more value it has.

### endless — 2015-07-04T23:20:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-170835>

Thanks Psyche.

My sleep last night was great, so I think I am adapting to the magnetico fairly well. Still have to consider upgrading the futon bed frame with additional 1x4 slats, and a few extra centre supports. It's fine for me but if I give up my bed to a heavier guest there could be a disaster......
It is very hot here right now and I am recovering from heatstroke, suffered during work 2 days ago. I had to wear jeans and my steel toe boots that day and it did me in, but 3pm I had to quickly bail on what I was doing and rush home and into a cold shower. I'd stopped sweating and my heart was pounding. Scary experience and left me with nausea and a headache for the following day. This morning I used all the ice and frozen jugs I had to get the damn CT tub cold, and still only managed 60F....felt good though. Even the river is warmer than that now, so I've been craving colder water.
Another 100F day and more coming up....I'm not going into the sun till at least 7pm!

Garden is in full production now...I ate spinach, arugula, cukes, onions and tomatoes out of it last night for dinner, with salmon.

### endless — 2015-07-04T23:39:13+0300

### endless — 2015-07-07T00:20:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-171188>

Awesome day for a Monday! I didn't have to leave for work right away, so had time to do my morning routine: outside at sunrise, 15 minutes oil pulling and UFO light while prepping CT tank, 40 minutes CT (well, kinda.....still struggling to get the water temp down. 60F was all I could get.), breakfast outside while naked sunbathing. My neighbours were both gone so I just stayed naked for a couple more hours and did some work in my garden. Other than a slight mishap involving a nipple and some stinging nettle it was great to be able to work nude.
Got all my garlic harvested, hauled a couple loads of manure to spread on the soil, fed and watered my compost bins and refilled the dog's wading pool after using the yucky old water on the raspberries.

I'm definitely noticing better sleep with the magnetico. I've been wanting to sleep outside but if I do that I will be missing sleeping on it I think. Was thinking about storing water on it, but I think I'll stick with using the ceramic block magnet I've been using, because I can keep it in the fridge and on the magnet at the same time.

### fitness@home — 2015-07-07T02:27:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-171200>

That's a lot accomplished for a Monday!

How cold do you like your tank for CT? I'm sitting in mine right now and we chill to 55 degrees. Now that it has a foam lid, the temp only goes up 1-2 degrees in 12 hours (next morning). Chiller is set to turn on at 2 pm and turn off at 6 pm. I get in after work. I should add 30 minutes in the morning too.

We've had the Magnetico for a good month now. Sleep is better but still could improve. In bed at 8:30 pm and up at 4:00 am. We didnt have any detox symptoms at all.
