### endless — 2015-07-12T05:56:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-171728>

I got my bed frame reinforced today.....I was a little concerned about the extra weight because it is just a futon frame with 1x4 slats and only two small supports in the middle. I doubled the number of slats and added 4 2x4 supports to the centre rail, and now I feel ok about sleeping on it. Not to mention having bigger, heavier guests sleep on it. I usually give up my bed to guests since I don't have a guest room. I'd hate to have someone visiting and have the bed collapse on the floor.....

It was extremely frustrating work because I needed a metric hex key which I didn't have, and I stubbed my toe on a section of Magnetico and broke the nail halfway down which really f\*%king hurt! But it's done. Even gave the room a good smudge with sage afterwards to clear all the negative\]ity I was generating...lol

Not sire if anyone remembers but I was debating whether to buy this 20g Magnetico because it was a double and my bed is a queen. Heres my frame with the upgrades, and how much is covered by the magnetico. I have had no problem staying away from the edges.

### caroline — 2015-07-12T07:14:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-171736>

After all your hard work Krista ....the universe will probably drop a significant other into your world! But you will only use half the bed anyway - so no need to worry!

You sleep on a futon? I want to know more.....did you see the link that this bird has wings posted? I want one of those! My stupid latex mattress is driving us nuts!

### endless — 2015-07-12T09:37:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-171739>

Hi Audrey,
No, I don't sleep on a futon, I sleep on a memory foam mattress, but the bed frame I bought was originally made for a futon. I'm finding the memory foam HOT even with just me on it so I am looking for a cooling topper....

### caroline — 2015-07-13T01:38:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-171780>

It seems to me that sleeping so "HOT" cannot be good for us ...apart from the obvious - There is just so much pent up heat that doesn't get dispersed - and when you multiply that by two ...YIKES

I am going to find that link ...really interesting. I am not sure the guy really understands what he is talking about - but he makes some really good points.

### endless — 2015-07-15T20:41:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-172029>

I'd like to see that, Audrey.
I actually have a bamboo fibre mattress topper now that works well for most of the year, but this extra hot summer requires more.

My garden is exploding! I'm picking long english cucumbers daily, and flogging lettuce and kale off on all my family. We haven't even gotten to the carrots and beets yet, but some are ready in 'baby' form. Bush beans are up next...I am enjoying eating some of each thing as it comes. and my family is enjoying the rest.

### caroline — 2015-07-16T00:32:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-172063>

[www.zafu.net](http://www.zafu.net/)

Krista - let me know what you think.........

### endless — 2015-07-24T08:08:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-172871>

Not sure Audrey...what product are you looking at exactly? The futon? All looks like pretty good stuff!

Nights are cooler lately and I am enjoying that because as much as I enjoy sleeping outside normally, I want to sleep inside on my magnetico! Now it cools down nicely in the house once it gets dark.

Started a jar of fermented salsa today, all from the garden(well, except the lemon juice). I finally have tomatoes ripening along with the jalapeños and onions. Garlic is all harvested and curing. I need to clean a few spots and replant cilantro, spinach and arugula, and plant some fennel seeds for fall bulbs. I've got HUGE zucchini plants and NO zucchinis..... Blossom end rot. Caused by calcium deficiency and uneven watering. Now I know. Tomorrow I have to attack the aphids on my brussels sprouts, and spray all the cucurbits with milk again to keep the powder mildew to a dull roar. My early cabbage is ready so next chance i get will put on a batch of kraut, or maybe half kraut, half cortido.
The raspberries are pretty much done already. Amazing how early everything is this year. I have a beautiful ripe cantaloupe I picked today, going to wait till my dad arrives on Saturday to share it with him. I have a few more coming that will probably go to my brother's family so I will enjoy this one for sure!

I need to order some more UVEX, and possibly something to use out of the house. I can't keep getting up in the night for emergency calls without using something, the lights in the fire hall are really bright.

### caroline — 2015-07-25T04:58:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-172920>

I need to learn how to grow stuff ....... I love it when you talk about your garden!

We have a huge old glass aquarium - it seems to me that it could be put outside and used to start/grow things?

Krista - do you grow mushrooms?

### endless — 2015-07-26T08:34:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-173029>

Well, I've only grown a certain variety...  But I could grow edible ones too I'm sure.

If I had a glass aquarium I'd make a terrarium out of it and grow some cool tropical plants.

### endless — 2015-07-31T22:15:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-173638>

Wow, lots going on around here this week. Summer is always busy. The 'shift' has happened though, nights are cooler, and my CT tank is colder out of the tap, finally! 54F this morning, without ice.
I had the opportunity to participate in some healing ceremonies too, which are still ongoing. Will report back later....

### endless — 2015-08-02T22:07:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-173807>

I think I finally managed it.....consistant am full body sun seems to be working finally. No more sunburn! My skin is darker than it has ever been, including belly, breasts and upper legs. I will miss it come winter, that's for sure. Might need to continue short tanning sessions.

I've been doing the UFO on my belly about 3x week for 15 minutes in the morning too. Feels great, I love the way the warmth penetrates. Still haven't tried nicotine. Not sure I will.

The garden is in full on production, I have to get started making kraut with some of this cabbage. Ate my very own home grown honeydew melon last week, amazing! I have 3 more in there that will be shared out to the family. I'm finding myself quite satisfied with a single meal of each summer fruit as they pass through. I have a small stash of raspberries and blueberries in the freezer that I will use over the winter for making contributions to family dinners etc.

Disconnecting a bit from the FB group. Lots of amazing information flying around there, fast and furious, but it's too hard to keep up without spending WAY more time on there than I want to. Better to spend it reading all these books I have accumulated, now that the tv is dormant. I think by the end of this week I will feel even more inclined to step back. As exciting as all this is, I have other things going on in my life that I want to spend time on too. Easy to get caught up in the panic about 'keeping up' with the blogs and the discussions. It causes stress. I admire Jack's brilliance and will never totally understand all of what he writes, so I will plod along here at my own pace and be content with that for now.

### nicld — 2015-08-04T16:48:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-173912>

I have 8 cabbage plants that will be used to make sauerkraut too!!! Nothing better then knowing that you grew it.

Oh and I will be trying to make some refrigerator pickles this week. My pickle cucumbers are getting ripe.

### endless — 2015-08-11T05:42:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-174399>

I just spent a weekend away, and came back to a huge list of garden chores that have to be done.....onio0ns are curing and need to be cleaned up, got to finish cleaning the garlic, pick and clean jalapeños to make lacto-fermented hot sauce, pick and roast eggplants, and of course get started on the kraut. The red cabbage is for sure ready to use now, so will start with those. I add beets and small amount of onion and apple to that. I made a jar of green cabbage/juniper berry/peppercorn and one of green cabbage/dillweed last week. With the late cabbage I might make some cortido and some more variations of basic kraut.

Also need to recuperate from some very un-optimal activity.....late nights full of LOUD electronic music and lots of flashing lights. The music went all night till about 7am, though I went to bed by 2:30 all three nights it was tough to sleep since the bass was rattling my tent. Fun, though.

sounds like a fun weekend! I am in awe of your gardening abilities .......... I think if you put up a bunch of yurts ....they will come!

### endless — 2015-08-15T08:17:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-174716>

This is nerve wracking....we are surrounded by unconfined wildfires (at a safe distance, so far...), it's hot, dry, and windy, and the smoke is so think in the air that my eyes are burning and throat is sore. We are in no danger from the existing fires, but the biggest one in the area grew from a small spot fire to 25km sq. in 24 hours. The conditions are the same here now. Everyone is praying for rain, no more lightning, and the wind to die down. I'm gathering a few things together in a safe place just in case.....cat carriers, passport and photos. Better safe than sorry I figure. Of course I'll probably be trying to haul the Magneticos off the bed if I have to leave in a hurry. And then of course there's my CFB/CGB and my UFO light.....hmmm. Might need to buy a trailer.

I finished harvesting all my garlic and onions today....think I'm set for sulphur for the season!

### caroline — 2015-08-15T09:07:53+0300

stay safe Krista ...xoxoxo

Glad you are prepping for that "just in case" scenario. Be safe!

It's scary when you can see and smell the smoke approaching. There were a lot if wildfires here a few years back during a drought.

### endless — 2015-08-17T03:37:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-174885>

It was a scary couple of days or sure, but the fires are at a distance from me. I was just concerned that we might have one start here. 26 homes were lost and 11 more properties lost other structures and equipment. The campground burned with vehicles and trailers. Lots of damage. Just hoping now that all the animals that were set free are found unharmed!

Just got off the Q&A.....I need to take a couple days off with my phone unplugged just to get though Life on the Edge! I try to read in bed for a bit but since I got the magnetico I fall asleep to fast.

### cinnamon — 2015-08-18T01:17:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-174974>

Glad to hear you are safe, Krista!

### endless — 2015-08-18T22:15:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-175064>

Thanks! Other than that one sketchy night all was pretty safe here.

My ex is staying with me right now. It's kind of challenging and makes me realize that sharing my space is something I will have to weigh carefully if I get into a relationship that goes in that direction. I'm finding myself annoyed at how he does things in my kitchen, for example. Trying to find a balance between voicing what I want things to be like and not being a nagging bitch. He's gone all day working (although often so am I) and here in the evening so I am feeling the lack of solitude. Working on going with the flow. It's not terrible...we know each other well and I am comfortable around him, plus he helps me out with expenses while he is here. Every day I see things that remind me why we are no longer together, though.

### endless — 2015-08-20T06:41:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-175190>

So Alyson gave me some homework.....after being single for 5 years (and celibate for 4 ) it's really time to get back in the saddle, so to speak. I am going to write out exactly what I want in a partner. I don't think I'll do it here, at least not yet, but maybe I will add it here at some point. I already had someone on the FB group say that I was setting myself up for failure because I've chosen to not have casual sex....maybe that's true, maybe it isn't. I have had enough casual sex in my life though, and now I want to save that connection for someone who deserves it. Just need to get my nerve up and start looking a bit.

### caroline — 2015-08-20T10:46:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-175196>

I made a list in my journal - just accidentally ...or maybe it was incidental to what someone was asking me about. I really just wrote it off the top of my head without any prior thought at all. I had never even thought of making a list....

when I went back and read it a few months later - I was blown away ....Tony ticked every single box. How's that for magic and quantum entanglement?!

We really have to think about the things that are very important to us ...and the things that are deal breakers.
I remember at first being so agitated because I thought I shouldn't be choosing some one on looks .....but you can't be with someone you think is gross!

### endless — 2015-08-22T05:53:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-175360>

I was thinking of you Audrey.....hopefully I have similar results!

Ugh, turning off the computer and reading tonight....tired of all the chest puffing and posturing I'm seeing in my newsfeed today. People are so weird..... Back to Life on the Edge.....

### caroline — 2015-08-22T08:21:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-175363>

It seems Gretchen had a melt down today ...LOL.

The funny thing about my list - Tony did tick all the boxes ...but there is so, so much more that I hadn't given any thought to. It seems, all of a sudden , everything is in harmony ...we are in harmony and we bring out the very best in each other. Going dancing is so sensual, and he sings to me and looks at me like he has waited forever for me to show up .... and I am the same.

It is magnetism to the nth degree - I have never experienced anything like this - not even close...... and we give off something that attracts others.

I wasn't even looking for someone really ....just trying to make myself move forward. It is funny to admit this - I think I was expecting a big, tall, wonderful man like my DH or like Jack who I think the world of. But guess who shows up .... a little pommy guy - not on my radar at all! Little guys have huge benefits I want you to know!

When we are dialled in, the universe knows what we want and need it seems ....

### endless — 2015-08-23T04:30:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-175416>

I'm really happy for you Audrey! I'm going to make sure my expectations don't blind me to the right person when he shows up. I've never been all about the appearance, even as a teen, so I don't think that would make me miss out but you never know!

All this talk about roe on the forum makes me realize how much I do (will) miss things that are no longer around....I've always taken it for granted that someone will show up with a couple huge freezers of whole fresh salmon on the back of a truck for me to buy each year. It might not happen this year and if it doesn't I will really miss having abundant salmon in my freezer all year!

### endless — 2015-08-24T05:24:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-175513>

I bought a used greenhouse frame today for $50. I'm trying to save money for the Quantlet and various other things, but this came up and was a good deal. It's 11 x 17ft, so big enough to grow a lot in. Who knows if next year is the year I will have to grow all my own food (and hunt/fish). This extends the growing season considerably so I figure it's a good buy. I will likely be here for several years before I can consider a move.

Garden is in full production mode. I have tomatoes coming out my ears now. Made a batch of lactofermented jalapeño hot sauce and starting a batch of fermented salsa. Setting aside the seed garlic and divvying up the rest among the family....I'm going to have a lot of winter squash and potatoes for them as well. My winter veggies will be mostly sauerkraut and kale, I think. Well, and garlic and onions of course. I actually grew some damn nice potatos this year....I will have to eat a couple here soon at least.

Used my last jar of salmon head stock yesterday....and still no word on salmon for this year. I might need to start stocking up on other options....

### endless — 2015-08-25T21:24:47+0300

Definitely heading into fall now....cool/cold nights and mornings and the days are getting shorter. I'm looking ahead to putting the garden to bed and getting ready for snow. (Well, not for 2 months plus, but still...) In the meantime I am enjoying the seasonal goodies from the garden before I give the rest away....in years past I would be keeping squash and potatoes, carrots and beets into the winter. Now that will go to my brother's household and I will keep onions,leeks, garlic, cabbage and kale. Well, and maybe one or two squash.... I'll be making tomato sauce and will keep a couple jars of that as well.

### endless — 2015-09-08T06:35:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-176715>

I really enjoyed 'summer CT' in my barely cold tub, basking in the morning sun and full body exposure in the sun afterwards.....but the cooler temps now are what make me want to CT more. The tank is dropping to better CT temps! I miss warming up in the sun (and will miss it even more when it gets colder outside) but enjoy the need to actively warm myself now. Still haven't shifted my diet much, as I am still enjoying seasonal stuff. Tonight had some local chantrelles with onions and kale from my garden and bacon, and cooked one of the red kuri squash I grew.

It occurred to me today as I was doing some cleaning in the garden that even if by some bad stroke of luck I had no seed saved and next season I had to be self sufficient, my garden could still feed me. Between the large variety of edible and medicinal 'weeds' that exist there and the volunteer plants that come up I would just have to let things grow until they are recognizable and then transplant. This year I had a lot of the usual volunteers, dill and chamomile, tons of sunflowers, but also some unusual ones, like a bunch of tomato plants! I\[m letting the cilantro and basil go to seed in there, and the peas and beans dropped some seed too. My more relaxed gardening style over the last year has some hidden benefits.

I've got enough naturally growing greens that I don't really need to plant any cultivated ones, actually. Purslane, chickweed, lamb's quarters, sorrel and sunflower sprouts make a great salad.

### Inger — 2015-09-08T16:56:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-176737>

Krista I love that my rain-barrel water has gotten cooler too now... feels so refreshing so dive in there every morning

I love wild gardening too...... I like that kind of gardening way more..... easier, takes way less time.. it is full of surprises.. and I have the feeling stuff grows better when things are mixed and growing wild... so cool.. and the "weeds" are often foods too
so I like to leave them there to snack on

### endless — 2015-09-10T08:28:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-176900>

Hi Inger  Yes, I have some basil that is ready and I think I will make some pesto with chickweed in it. Might as well try some weed recipes!

Dinner tonight was %100 local.....local lamb sausage, cabbage and onions from my garden fried in duck fat, and grilled carrots, zucchini and wild chantrelles. Gotta love mushroom season.

I drove a LOT today for work, and was acutely aware of the sun shining through the windows of the car into my eyes and on my skin. Whenever the angle was right I rolled the window down beside me to get the real thing. I'm going to move the daytime Blutech glasses up on the priority list, I spend SO m uch time in the car it's basically the same as being at a computer all day. I figure I can wear the glasses while driving, push them up onto my head when I am walking outside to and from my service calls, and put them on again when I have to go into jobs. I have no problem getting the am light unadulterated, although it's sometimes just that, light, not actually sun. Going to order a new pair of Uvex and maybe one of the Dewalts too, for home. Eventually will get some stylie night time blue blockers like Jack had on in his last Quantum video, but I think the day ones are more urgent.

### fitness@home — 2015-09-10T15:03:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-176906>

Krista, it's getting harder for me to get my morning sunlight before work (shorter days).

I am actually going to switch from stopping at the park on my way in to just parking on the top floor of the parking garage, because i will actually be able to see the sun easier from there. At the park, I have to wait for the sun to rise above the buildings and trees...

You should see me on the way home every day between 40-50 pm. Sunroof open, opening windows to collect more sun when the angle is right, and my glasses OFF (which could be a bad thing as I should be wearing them to drive). Good thing I've driven that same route a gazillion times and know it like the back of my hand...

That afternoon drive home recharges me.

P.s does anyone know why those smileys get inserted into my text? Are those keystrokes a shortcut or something?

### nonchalant — 2015-09-10T17:39:31+0300

>
> You should see me on the way home every day between 40-50 pm. Sunroof open, opening windows to collect more sun when the angle is right, and my glasses OFF

Dawn, if you have tiny glasses they can just sit down on your nose, below your focal point, and you can just slightly tilt your head back to look at signs or whatever.

I finally bought a new pair of glasses, with plastic frames (but with a wire core). So, so much more comfortable than wire-rims. None of those pesky zapping feelings on my temples or near the nose. I only wear them to drive or when looking for something particular, like locating sardines in a new grocery store. However I don't like they way they block my peripheral vision.

So a 0 and a : make that smiley? 0: nope.
4:00
Maybe it's just you, Dawn. ;-)

I did, actually. Still have not heard back from Blutech regarding their offer to locate a provider so I will go back to the one I was in before. I worked inside a building with no windows all day and drove to and from the job an hour each way. 12 hours total trapped in horrible florescent light and behind glass. I felt like I was drugged, and had a hard time thinking. There were other crappy things in the environment as well.....had to talk on my cell phone extensively NOT on speaker, and at one point had the cell up to one ear and a cordless on the other side. . ### nicld — 2015-09-18T16:28:02+0300

### endless — 2015-10-02T06:00:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-178420>

I'm still without BluTechs but it's basically a funds issue, so whatever. It'll have to wait. For now, the routine is: up before sunrise=black light on. Once sun is up, outside for as long as is manageable (usually 10-15min). If I have to work right away, that's it. If not, I try to jump in the CT tank for 30 min(outside) and warm up in the sun (if any) naked before I go anywhere. i-2 mornings a week I also do the UFO on my belly for 15 min.
Just bought more black light bulbs so will be increasing the intensity in the AM.

### endless — 2015-10-05T06:38:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-178630>

Thanks Jack, good to know. I will look into that.

Audrey, no, that's not the deal. I will have to make it work there....he doesn't want his place empty for months or his daughter staying alone. It'll be a challenge!

I hope so rlee, but you know how teenage girls are....she mostly just thinks I'm odd.  I will do my best to influence her.

It occurred to me today that if I was actually following what NATURALLY grows around here instead of what I am able to cultivate, I would be keto a lot sooner.....I'm fooling myself because I have carbs in my garden but I couldn't go out in the forest right now and pick a winter squash! So....plentiful now are wild apples, rose hips, elderberries and mushrooms. I'm giving myself until the end of October to fully switch to this new revelatory plan, mostly because I put a lot of work into this garden, dammit, and I want to eat some of it! Everything is out now except the kale and broccoli and fennel anyway, but I have carrots, beets, squash and potatoes harvested. Onions and garlic I will eat year round.

QUESTION for anyone: the only 'mismatches' I am reluctant to give up are coconut products and avocado. Neither of these things grow in Canada so I suppose that theoretically I should NEVER eat them. Problem is, I love both of them and rely on them both for fat sources (palatable fat sources!) in my diet year round. How much do you think this is likely to fuck me up? I love me some coconut milk in my chaga....

### endless — 2015-10-05T08:11:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-178636>

There is an amazing list of wild plant foods in my area....I just spent hours on one website that listed a ton of thewm in a highly unpleasant format. So....if I DID decide to really go with the seasonal/local thing i9n a seriopus way, I probably could. Mind you, it would take a lot of my time to collect these foods, so it's not completely practical. But here is a list of the things I know I can readily obtain:

hazelnuts
wild mint
wild carrots
lily bulbs
wild onion
wild garlic
wild chives
fiddlehead ferns
pine nuts
walnuts
nettles
sorrel
chickweed
lamb's quarters
plantain
portulaca
clover
cattail
wild asparagus
pine nuts
pine needles
sunflowers
oregon grape
huckleberry
wild strawberry
saskatoon berry
elderberry
wild apple
mushrooms

There is more, but I am unfamiliar with them and could not identify. I think I will take a class. Ironically, several of these are 'weeds' in my garden that I pull out to make room for my seeded plants.
I'm going to start to think how I can incorporate as many of these as possible into my diet without having to quit my job to forage....I already use some of them, but if I was actually relying on them for food I would need more. I have LOTS of saskatoon berry and elderberry around. Lots of wild apples. Lots of greens, in spring. The challenge would be in finding starchier carbs. I know they exist here but don't know what/where they are. Will enlist some help to find them. There are LOTS of amazing wild mushrooms here, right now in fact. Lobsaters, chantrelles, honeys, shaggy manes, morels (in spring), pine. It's huge industry here.

### endless — 2015-10-09T05:52:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-178904>

Got in the river today while walking the dogs....wasn't exactly sunny out but it was very mild, and no wind. Water was probably 48-50. I am going to miss it when the temp drops below 40....I will only be doing quick dips then.

### endless — 2015-10-10T05:32:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-178983>

I'm still a bit confused about the black light conversation...have been unable to find out the precise measurements of my cheap CFLs so far. One website I found had a bunch of options for UVA, some with clear/white bulbs instead go black. I need to learn more before I experiment further. For now I have one beside my bed and one by my computer.
Frustrating day with no opportunity to CT or bask....I drove all over hell and back. Opened the window when I could to get some sun on skin action at least. When I got home I sat outside grounding feet in the grass for a half hour, helped a bit.
My chiro appt. today was much needed.....my neck was badly out from all the overhead work I've been doing lately and I've been feeling dizzy and nauseated often, especially when I look up. Which isn't ideal when I am on a ladder holding a tv.  He said he got a good adjustment so hopefully it'll be better soon, although today I felt like my equilibrium was off.

### endless — 2015-10-10T19:55:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-179001>

I am becoming such a sun junkie....so precious this time of year! This morning I was grumbling in my CT tank because it was windy, and the clouds were dark, and I was thinking about how much more I enjoy CT when it involves warming up in the sun afterwards. I went in after my 30 minutes and resigned myself to warming up under my own power. Then the sun appeared! I literally leaped up and ran outside naked to suck up as much as possible....
I am now a very happy little sun worshipper, tank is recharged for now.

### endless — 2015-10-11T19:41:10+0300

Hi Jude.  It's easy to take for granted, I know, even here. I have gained a new appreciation for it over the last 3 years thanks to Jack though!
Nicole, sun is just starting to peep out here so I'm heading out too.

Had a rare night out last night, to an event held in a nightclub in town. Alex Grey and his wife did a live painting event. I wasn't sure what to expect but it was fascinating watching a blank canvas turn into this in less than 3 hours. He was amazing to watch as well, really in his own creative place and feeling the music at the same time. I enjoyed it a lot.

### endless — 2015-10-13T04:47:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-179277>

Thanksgiving dinner was a success....had my mother and stepfather, brother and nephew here, plus my ex who has been staying with me. He deboned the turkey, which was interesting. Great because the bone broth was started the day before the meal and in the freezer before we even ate.  I also cooked a small local ham in the crockpot, and we had mashed potatoes and roasted veggies from the garden. I made a salad of black kale and chard with apple, walnuts and blue cheese that was really good too. Wallowing in the last seasonal carbs before I go keto for the winter.

Seriously, how many times do I have to type 'keto' before my computer stops changing it to leto?? My spellcheck must be a secret Dune aficionado......

### endless — 2015-10-13T20:37:34+0300

Finally starting to crave ketosis....I have a houseguest for one more week and then I am going to get into it. I could do it now but I've been sharing my space for 3 months and I will probably go through a little 'reclaiming' period.....there is food in the cupboards and fridge that I want to get rid of, etc. putting things back the way I like them in my home. I really enjoy my space, especially if I have been sharing for awhile and getting it back to myself is a bit of a ritual.

### endless — 2015-10-17T19:13:05+0300

Another brilliant blue day starting...every one is precious as the clouds and rain are on the way. Time to go jump in the tank and soak in the sun....down to 48F. I'm re-adapting as it seems to be dropping a couple degrees daily.

### endless — 2015-10-18T01:07:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-179758>

The sun is visible here now from 8:15am till about 2:30pm. The downside of living in the mountains. Today, I was lucky to spend all of that time outside with minimal clothing and barefoot. Other days I will find that entire time eaten up by work and not get any at all. Hopefully my supplementing of light will help offset those days.
Today was productive...after CT and nude basking at breakfast I got my garlic bed planted and mulched, cleaned some more spent stuff out of the garden (fed it to the horses) and brought all 25 of my winter squash inside to store under my bed. I'll be doling them out to my brother's household as needed but it's cooler here so they will store better.
Now 3pm and I feel like the day is over because the sun is gone! I could jump in the car and drive to the sunny area of the river, or get to work on my GST remittance for this quarter.... Sadly, the paperwork is going to win today.

### endless — 2015-10-21T04:23:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-180044>

My long term houseguest left today. I almost feel guilty about how relieved I am! My dogs are despondent....he spent a ton of time with them. He was a big help around here too, helped me with the rent, did some cooking, etc. Hauled a bunch of horse shit for the garden. But he's my ex, and all the things that USED to bug me about him still do. I know he'd like us to be a couple again, but it's just not going to happen. I will always love him but that part of our relationship is over so I feel uncomfortable about that even though it's unspoken on both our parts. All in all not so bad to have around but today I finally have my house back!!!! Hunting down and disposing of the stuff left behind that is not ok for me to have in the house right now...rice noodles, gluten free crackers, etc. I've still got the carb mania cravings happening so minimizing exposure is important.

Happy the election is over, and I can quit annoying everyone on FB with my political posts. Happy the fucking psychopath is gone. Hopeful about the future.
My back and neck are really fucked up right now. Still getting dizzy spells and arm numbness at night, and my thoracic spine is seized up tight. I will need to seek treatment other than the chiro....the adjustments don't last. Acupuncture is my best bet to get a handle on it. I also realized today that one of the self care things that went by the wayside when my ex was here is my supplement routine. I've been off magnesium for at least 3 weeks and that's not good with muscle spasming. Tonight after fire practice I will get in a warm bath \[fuck I forgot to buy epsom salts today\] with some mag chloride and see if it helps, and take a large dose of mag bisglycinate before bed.

### endless — 2015-10-22T08:06:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-180129>

Haha, thanks all!
Maybe I'm a bit OCD, but when I have someone staying, I have to clean when they leave before I feel totally relaxed again. Today after work I cleaned the bathroom, and with a few more small chores it'll be all mine again. Good grief, how am I going to manage if I ever get into another relationship?? Separate homes, methinks.
I am so over this dizzy, nauseous feeling. Back to the chiro in the morning and hopefully some more relief. I think I need acupuncture, and if that doesn't help I might go see the doc.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-22T15:23:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-180144>

My bet is the lack of powerful sun has a lot more to do with how you feel than anything else based upon latitude and time of the yr. Go to a tanning salon and see if it helps more than any doc. Cheaper.

### endless — 2015-10-22T23:05:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-180186>

Will do Jack....got my tanning package all ready to go.

I DID go back to the Chiro this morning after doing some more extensive foam rolling/tennis ball stuff on my thoracic spinal area last night and he got some good adjustment of my upper neck. He also did a procedure called 'canalith repositioning' that had him moving me into different positions so my head was at different angles. He said if my eyes did some kind of movement (nystagmus?) it was likely inner ear related but I had no sign of that.Funny though, I do have all the same symptoms as the description of Benign Paroxysmal Positional Vertigo, which is what he was checking for. CRAZY dizzy when I sat up, and felt nauseated for several hours. I'll wait it out and see what happens.

Came straight home and got naked in the garden in the full sun. Feeling better now.

### caroline — 2015-10-23T06:50:54+0300

### endless — 2015-10-25T07:41:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-180396>

Awesome, I will have to try it...I love going out in winter for a full moon xc ski, but I'm pretty covered up of course. Summer might be better for a full body moon bath.

Looks like my mom's house survived the hurricane, with no structural damage but it made a hell of a mess and destroyed most of the roof tiles. Her town was the site of some of the worst damage . Lots of trees and power lines down and the roofing materials of a lot of places didn't survive. Saw a video today of the town and it's a mess, but cleanup has already begun. Nobody died, that's all that matters.

I'm having another wonky cycle....day 15 and I started to bleed again, heavily. Looking back over the year, I've had all relatively normal cycles, between 25-29 days long (average 27 days) except I had one 21 day cycle in September, followed by a normal one, and now only two weeks! Not sure what to think. If anything I am much more conscious of getting sun this fall than in previous years, and it's been uncommonly dry and warm. I guess I should go in for blood work again.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-25T07:53:32+0200

He does........and is right now. Naked as a jay bird in Bourbon street sitting on the porch. No one even notices as I listen to Clocks ticking.

so ..... tell us more.......you haven't mentioned naked moon bathing before - have you????

I asked Tony to hook up an outdoor shower today- so we can take a cold shower before rolling around on the grass ...... he doesn't even blink an eye any more!

of course ..... we do need pics

### Inger — 2015-10-25T10:35:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-180404>

I get moonlight on my face at night in the horse barn... through the open window.... I love it.... I never ever use curtains...........
Yesterday I stood nude in the terrace door sucking up the sunbeams as I talked to the ex and I did not realize that my landlords wife was in the garden with her grandchild... right in front of me... oh my god..... pretty embarrassing...lol

### endless — 2015-10-25T17:07:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-180421>

Haha, Inger...we are all a bunch of exhibitionists, really..... I don't care if someone sees me naked, I am usually only modest to avoid their uncomfortable feelings about it. I was raised in a hippie commune with a lot of nudity. Even afterwards, my 'family dinners' usually involved lighting the wood sauna and everyone doing saunas and cold dunks before dinner, all together. I miss having that sauna.

Audrey, look who's talking! I want to see pics of you and your man in the outdoor shower!

Praying for an easy work day today....I have not had a morning at home all week to do 'me stuff'. This morning I will be here for the sunrise. I'm doing my oil pulling and UFO on my belly while I wait, under my black light. CT tank is likely 40F this morning, if I am still at home when the sun comes up fully in I go.

### endless — 2015-10-27T18:43:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-180571>

Feeling better about my winter food supply now with my whitetail in the freezer! I need at least one alternative to seafood and this is it. Last year it was elk, this year venison. I didn't buy chickens this year, so I guess I'll be getting creative with what else I have. Managed to save the heart but the liver was damaged. Looking forward to making amazing bone broth from it too.
Never made it into the tank....had second thoughts due to this heavy bleeding. I was headed there this morning too when work interfered. I'm going to have to adjust my schedule somehow......I can't get up and do it outside in the dark. Well, I could, but I'd rather do it outside in natural light and sun if any.
Still toying with the idea of making a doc appt. The BPPV (if that's what it was) seems to be abating but this bloating, cramping, irregular cycle thing is starting to piss me off. I can't think of anything that's changed, really.....if anything I've gotten way MORE UV light heading into winter this year than I usually do. At least more with intention. Less CT, maybe.

### nicld — 2015-10-27T18:57:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-180574>

That's awesome you got a deer. We are hoping to get a deer again this year and hopefully NOT with the car. There are new dead ones on the road everyday and I see that as such a shame and a waste. I hit 2 so far in the past couple of years and did not get to harvest either one of them (they ran off).

It maybe worth just getting checked out. Do you get your hormone levels checked regularly? I am NOT looking forward to the day when mine start getting jacked up due to age but to not cycle is somewhat appealing.

### endless — 2015-10-27T21:53:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-180584>

Nicole, I used to be obsessive about labs, and I kind of burned myself out. Now I'm not as diligent as I probably should be. Last time I went (March) I had low E, low T, PG/E2 ratio of 218. Low DHEA. I should probably go back and see where I'm at.

Well, I got an unexpected break in my day so forced myself outside into the tub. Wasn't all that excited about it even though the outside temp is pretty good (10C/50F). Water was 45F. I only lasted 10 MINUTES! Totally wimped out. And the stupid part is, I could easily stay in longer, but I'm anticipating how much it sucks when I get out! I get SO cold, and now that there's no warm sun to warm up in I just walk around my somewhat cold house naked and uncomfortable. Putting a robe on just makes me colder. I did get out and use the UFO light on my belly and thighs while they were still extremely cold. I think I'll be doing shorter stints now that the water is colder....I have to train for next weekend so I don't wimp out at the lake with Alyson and Jena!

### endless — 2015-10-28T03:38:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-180603>

Hit the tanning bed for the first time today as it seems that my sunny days outside are (finally) done. I've been incredibly fortunate this year to have the weather be so nice for so long! BUT...damn I love tanning. I have never been a tanner for the sake of tanning, just for vit. D or before a trip. I went for 10 minutes today after my 10 minutes of CT (which left me chilled for a long time) and it felt really good.

### endless — 2015-10-29T18:01:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-180722>

Jack was in my dream last night.....it was great, some kind of member gathering and he was helping me work of my fear of flying. Obviously my subconscious knows it needs to speak to me in the form of someone who's knowledge I respect and that I will listen to....haha!

### endless — 2015-10-31T20:09:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-180845>

What a dark and gloomy day......I failed miserably the 'CT outside in any weather no matter what' test.  To be fair, I didn't have time this morning, and while I was in town for work I snuck into the tanning salon for 10 minutes. No water CT today though. Took there pups for an underdressed walk in the rain this afternoon but that's it.

Was thinking about relationships.....I have a relatively enlightened and totally supportive person who I think would be happy to renew our relationship (my ex, the one who was staying with me this fall) but who I am completely not attracted to anymore. I love him and we are great friends but the nature of the relationship has changed. Too bad, because he loves Jack and listens to webinars with me when he is here. When he is not here (spends winters in India/southeast Asia, lucky guy) and he calls he will say 'is that Jack'? if he hears anything in the background.

On the other hand, the idea of dating is daunting.....there are so few prospects in my area. I'm actually quite content alone for now but I keep getting outside pressure that I shouldn't be. Not sure what to do. Am I avoiding and need to 'get back on the horse', or is it ok to enjoy my life solo until I meet someone who makes me want to change that?

### caroline — 2015-11-01T11:02:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-180882>

I think your worlds will collide at the right time ..... You are content with you - that is awesome - in my opinion. What business is it of anyone else's????

Enjoy every single second of your life ........ and when lightening strikes - you will know .... make no mistake about that! It will be crystal clear when someone comes along that really rings your bell and you want to spend the rest of your life with.

no matter what ....don't settle - ever - in anything. You want it all.........the whole enchilada!

No one could have been more reluctant than me - especially at my age...... but we have inspired so many people - so many people repeat our story.

Strangers just beam at us ....for reasons I don't exactly get ...... it seems to be quantum entanglement that draws people to us.

We were at a Jazz festival over the weekend, in the wine country ....... Tony won the tickets for the festival and a really nice hotel....amazing luck once again!

It was drizzling rain but everyone was having a ball ...and a lot of bonding going on - it could have been the wine as well I suppose. An English woman, who had had more than her share of wine kept stroking my face and wanting to hear our story ..... as did others - they seem to be completely fascinated.

A friend of ours, a teacher, spent an hour telling her kindergarten kids our story ......they were so interested for some reason...

### endless — 2015-11-02T03:13:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-180945>

You are my inspiration Audrey! You're right, no settling here.

I'm doing the three day fast thing. I always have trouble this time of year transitioning into winter without sliding into carb induced torpor.......I start to dread the cold and want to curl up under a blanket and eat winter squash soup and mashed potatoes. 3 days to reset and start out heavier on the seafood again.

### caroline — 2015-11-02T03:35:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-180947>

fasts are so hard when it is cold.....good luck!

### endless — 2015-11-02T20:37:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-181001>

So far so good Audrey! Day two and feeling good. I did a short CT this morning despite REALLY not wanting to (I tend to get cold when I'm hungry...) and survived. I AM daydreaming about what I'm going to eat for breakfast on Wednesday......Alyson's pic of bacon and salmon on the QH group this morning was tempting but I'd probably do better with a soup made with my homemade halibut stock with some scallops, shrimp and clams. And possibly some kelp noodles. Yum. Can't wait. This is the magic of short fasts for me...not the physical benefits so much as the mental refocusing. When I am daydreaming about fish soup instead of pizza I know I'm back on the right track.

### jenaf — 2015-11-02T20:59:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-181008>

so the fast is just to refocus on the seafood? short term 3 days only?

and note from the last page: my last two periods have been screwed up and that has been since i chucked out the contacts and started using UV light bulbs. my second period (about 2 wks ago) had no cramps, no signals that i was going to get it, and was super light. then day 3 (which is usually my last day) i got SUPER depressed, unmotivated, fatigued. I actually stayed home from work and laid in bed all day. So i KNOW the UV is doing something to our hormones.

i guess i should start a journal. Alyson has inspired me.

### endless — 2015-11-03T02:36:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-181031>

> so the fast is just to refocus on the seafood? short term 3 days only?

Mostly....just to focus on good habits in general, too. I end up with a much greater appreciation for the things I 'should' be eating/doing after being deprived for a few days. I've always loved that about fasting, but I don't do long (10-14 day) fasts like I used to because I do not think they are healthy for me now. Back when I used to do them I felt fantastic and rarely wanted to start eating again though. I love that feeling. The shitty part about doing 3 days is that the first 3 days are the hardest.

Good to know about the cycles....mine are super weird. Interested to see what will happen next!

### endless — 2015-11-03T08:23:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-181046>

Got my light order from Amazon today....two red LEDs for my overhead lights in kitchen and living room and two 365nm LED black lights. The incandescent black bulbs I bought were very dim and put off a lot of heat. I'm liking these better so far.
Also found and couldn't resist buying the box set of Madeline L'Engle's 'Wrinkle In Time' quintet! I'm excited to read them again, it's been a long time.

### Inger — 2015-11-03T16:16:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-181062>

I feel you on the dating, Krista. I seem to fall so much in love.... but it is not always good...... and I get so hurt.. like crushed, really bad. No idea why I love so much.. idk why I hurt so much
I maybe forget to think about it forehand if this is really a good thing... like.. for everybody! I get so fascinated... from the beauty of man
I get fascinated from people anyways.... they fascinate me... every kind of
But I take the pain..... and I know I will come out again on the other side of the dark tunnel...... I can always see the light.. not one second in my life that I lose that, no mater how dark
maybe it is something I have to learn. For sure it is!
Truth always win no matter how much it hurt..... because when you do the right thing, it might hurt but it is a different kind of pain, and you know in the end everything will be better because of it

Good thing is I like to be alone too. I have beautiful moments.. a lot .. just with me..... makes it easier
I think I am gonna marry the nature and the wild...lol
what to do

I am not going on any dating site... because i have important stuff to do...... before I have it done no dating for me....
I like to be alone too
maybe we are getting old?

can you give me a link to what red led lights you have been buying, Krista?

### endless — 2015-11-03T17:52:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-181079>

I have not tried that Nicole.....been tempted a few times but in my early CT (indoors) days I found that CT in the evening makes it really hard for me to sleep. I really prefer it earlier in the day. I should try though, it has been 3 years or so since making that observation. Of course, having another person to cuddle up to afterwards would be helpful too!

Inger, I bought these bulbs, [http://www.amazon.com/Sunlite-LED-A...1446565679&sr=1-16&keywords=sunlite+led+bulbs](http://www.amazon.com/Sunlite-LED-A19-Medium-Listed/dp/B00R9MXXBU/ref=sr_1_16_m?s=hi&ie=UTF8&qid=1446565679&sr=1-16&keywords=sunlite+led+bulbs)
I like them, but definitely like candle light better! Just trying to replace the overhead lights for times I need more light.

I think I'm still not ready to try dating sites. I found it kind of depressing sifting through the many profiles that just didn't seem suitable. It's kind of like trying to pick a movie to watch on Netflix....if you just came across the one movie, you'd likely give it a try, but with so many choices all at once, it's hard to choose anything!

### endless — 2015-11-04T08:05:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-181117>

I really have to get something to wear for practice nights....all the lights in the damn fire hall are super bright motion activated ones. I'm constantly getting flashed. Uvex are too big. Maybe I'll look at the lowbluelights ones.

### endless — 2015-11-05T07:52:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-181193>

Holy fuck, I got bitchslapped badly by my day today....had all these great plans to come home at a decent hour and prepare for my quantum blondes weekend, but I ended up with a problematic install today that took a lot longer than planned. My poor doggies sat waiting for 11 hours and then got no walk. I sometimes wonder if working this job so I can live here is worth it. I work damn hard. I don't even work in my 'chosen career' of nutritional consulting because Jack ruined it for me..... Plus it's impossible to make a living here like that. If I moved to the city I could likely find a much less physically demanding job but I'd be living in a worse environment. So yep, I guess it is worth it.
It's tough being blue collar! Especially as a woman. It always amazes me how many times a day I get comments from men because I am wearing a toolbelt, or up on a ladder, or laying on the floor with my entire upper body hidden inside a cupboard, pulling cables or something. Why is this so fucking amazing to them?? As luck would have it, after a long tedious install I had a service call to a pub at 6pm. I swear it's like the men around here are having their realities challenged by seeing a female tech. I am SO TIRED of being chatted up by the half cut guy at the bar while I replace a computer lottery terminal and answer his inane questions. YES I have a brain AND a tool belt and know how to use both. Sheesh.....

### endless — 2015-11-10T03:19:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-181551>

What an amazing weekend with Jena and Alyson! It was refreshing to hang out with people who understand me

We spent the weekend CT-ing in the lake, gathering UV in the hot tub and eating seafood. Bit of wine and sleep as well. Jack joined us VIA FaceTime for a wee bit on Friday night and that was fun.
There is an exceptional seafood market in Vernon, if anyone can use that information....we got oysters, mussels and clams live plus huge wild shrimp. Had a fantastic seafood bake brunch with Clay on Saturday.

I don't get many weekends off work, so this was a treat! Not only because of that, but also because the weekend was devoted to the pursuit of optimal....everything we did was to that end, and it was a lot of fun doing it with friends.

### Shijin13 — 2015-11-16T21:47:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-182096>

> [endless said:](/goto/post?id=180945)
>
> You are my inspiration Audrey! You're right, no settling here.
>
> I'm doing the three day fast thing. I always have trouble this time of year transitioning into winter without sliding into carb induced torpor.......I start to dread the cold and want to curl up under a blanket and eat winter squash soup and mashed potatoes. 3 days to reset and start out heavier on the seafood again.

Krista

I keep toying with doing a fast. I've never done fasts well, in the past. I'm a cranky bitch and DH hates it when I get that way. but over the past 3-4 weeks, I just feel so bloated after eating (but I'm not bloated). It feels like I just stuffed my face at thanksgiving dinner.  There are mornings where food is un appealing - so it doesnt occur until btwn 11-2. This morning I woke up feeling full.... but i knew i wasn't ready for a fast.

I think I need to just draw a line in the sand and do one.... and see how I feel after...

As for CT. its been non-existent in my life this past month... I've been really struggling with Cold due to my thyroid being all off. I've been doing coherence testing on myself, and discovered that I wasn't taking the correct thyroid meds, or dosage. So I've adjusted - and I'm beginning to start to crave the cold. But the lack of interest in cold may also be the result of days where we're hitting the mid 70's!

### endless — 2015-11-17T17:08:12+0200

I felt better after the 3 day fast, but I'm still having digestion issues.....basically just bloating. Its driving me insane. My belly swells up like crazy and I look and feel like a fat ass. I've started liver support again , and HCL with every meal to see if it makes a difference.
Gretchen, I AM craving cold but only in the morning and my god damn job is preventing me from getting it. The best I can do is cryohelmet in the truck, when I really want to get into my tub outside when the sun rises....usually I am gone by then though. I start feeling chilled in the evening and CT is completely unappealing then.
I'm tired of everything today.....must be low dopamine. I just want to go back to bed and snuggle with my dogs, but instead I have to drive over a mountain pass in a snowstorm for work. Fucking awesome. I'd better stick my head in a snowbank while I'm out there and try to snap out of it.

### endless — 2015-11-20T03:29:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-182397>

Nothing to report withy the dating site yet. I'm being passive, because I am not excited about it right now. It's definitely brought up issues I need to address. Such as: The guys I find physically attractive, I dismiss automatically because I am convinced they will not be attracted to me. Yes, I know that's fucked up but it's what's going on. The ones I think are 'in my league' are not attractive to me. This is why I've always avoided online dating I think. I need to really MEET people and see past their exterior to know if I'm interested. Problem with that is I live in a rural area...to meet most of these guys requires travelling, and I'm not sure it's worth it. I still think that when I am ready the right guy will appear. Maybe next week when I'm not crampy and irritable I'll feel more positive about it.

### Linz — 2015-11-20T14:10:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-182416>

Krista, I think you might be surprised who finds you attractive. I live in a rural place too but went to a big light exhibition in the city to try and find some inspiration for house lighting. A horrifying experience as every exhibitor was promoting LED as this is what the low energy regs permit and the technical experts are mostly engineers with no knowledge of living cells at all. Some were using wireless to make the lights change colour and a very few trying to promote this as healthy 'circadian' lighting despite wireless control and using light with a very poor spectrum. I asked a few superficial questions about lighting options and what was available with improved spectrums and the responses were eye-opening. Many immediately said no-one has ever asked that before and I was introduced to several bosses very keen to give me their business cards. Professional masks slipped off everywhere along with confessions about the technical problems. Only one (involved with industry lighting) walked rather too briskly towards the exit when I asked him if he had thought about putting up a human equivalent of the plant light that was on display to illustrate the effects of light.
What really surprised me was how many real people surfaced from the crowd. If I was single I don't think I would have found any of them on a dating site but I might consider travelling to another light exhibition!!

### endless — 2015-11-22T19:54:12+0200

Thanks Linz, I shall definitely keep that in mind and keep my eyes open for 'face to face' opportunities!
CT tank is frozen over this morning and I'm just not up to it. Instead I'm sitting outside naked in the sun, bare feet in the crunchy frozen grass. It's -6C or so but the sun feels amazing. I'd stay out here for hours if I could, but unfortunately I have some work to do today that won't wait.
I'm getting better at catching bits of sun during the day.... Now on my drives if it is sunny I roll down the window enough to get the sun in my eyes and on my face.

### endless — 2015-11-26T04:35:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-182689>

Well, I sold it anyway.....I've made $200 selling stuff I don't need this week. That's 2 months of gold membership!

I'm starting to get less hungry in the evening. ALMOST skipped dinner tonight but I am trying to be very strict about not eating after 6pm and I didn't trust that I would not get hungry before bed. I can't sleep if I'm hungry. Plus I had some oysters to use. Fried them breaded in pork rind crumbs and butter. Also had a few scallops and sone homemade sauerkraut. 2 squares of 85% Lindt. Actually I ate quite a lot for not being very hungry!

I'm going to spend an hour on the floor stretching while I watch Survivor, then treat myself to an Alitura face mask in the tub!

### endless — 2015-11-26T19:36:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-182714>

So much for the tub.....fell asleep before the end of the show. That's why I rarely watch tv or movies, puts me to sleep. Now I will have to go online and watch the end to see who got voted off the island.

Collecting my little whitetail from my brother's place this morning and taking it over to the meat shop for processing. Good timing, as I am down to my last package or two of elk from last year. Getting all the bones for bone broth (and maybe a few for the dogs) and again, good timing as my halibut broth is getting low already. I will be waiting for frames to go on sale again at the fish market. Unfortunately they don't sell many anymore, or heads, because they've discovered that it's more profitable to make the stock themselves and sell that. I have become a head-hunter......

I'm back to face dunks every morning. I had stopped for awhile (a long while, actually) in favour of full immersion CT but I am enjoying this a lot. It's a manageable part of my morning, while trying to fit CT in doesn't often happen and leaves me feeling stressed. Now if I don't get any other CT in during the day, at least I've done my face. This morning I followed it up with 5 minters of UFO light on my face afterwards while my skin was cold. I'll do that a couple times a week and see what, if anything, I notice. My skin felt dry, so used argan oil on it today.

### endless — 2015-12-04T17:51:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-183287>

They were travelling through Sinaloa state....cartel territory, apparently. My mom lives on the west coast in a small town. Just flying into PV and driving down there for a couple weeks. ### endless — 2015-12-05T04:07:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-183353>

So, the Quantlet launch thing happened and I didn't get one like I had planned.....it just isn't in the cards financially. If it had happened at a different time of year, maybe. But, I'm ok with waiting....be nice to get some feedback from others in the meantime. If I can save the money for it I'll go for it in the future.
Supposed to be heading out to a club for a burlesque show tonight but I'm going to stay home. I've been feeling really antisocial lately. Not to mention it's a 30 minute drive each way and I don't have any suitable eye protection for nights out yet. I don't feel comfortable driving in UVEX. Another thing I'm saving for.... I don't know what is better, to force myself to go out and be social (I always have fun once I'm out) or stay home where I can turn on my red light and read a book and hit the magnetico early.  I'm kind of afraid that I'm going to end up optimal but alone. The slightly concerning part is I'm not sure I care. I think I have been suffering a bit of depression lately though. ### endless — 2015-12-05T22:56:39+0200

Thanks Audrey!
It's gloomy outside again today, so I ended up spending a few hours doing a good house clean. I was only going to do a few things but discovered one of my cats has been peeing in a corner which necessitated a lot more work and evolved into full blown cleaning the house. Animal hair control is always an issue, in my house. I use a swifter vac daily but to really get the hair up I need to damp mop everything too. Offending cat got face planed into a snowbank and told to go think about it in the woodshed for awhile. I've doused the area with peppermint oil and will be keeping my sprtay bottle of vinegar water handy as a behaviour modification tool.
Now done all my little chores so I'm going to get these mutts outside for some light and exercise.

### Inger — 2015-12-06T14:55:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-183504>

Krista, if you feel depressed maybe try more raw seafood and meat? High meat? Raw seafood is so "happy-making"........ for me at least
and light... I try to go out every day when I can. I cant always. Because I work so much. But I have my reptile light then.......... today it is so stormy here I cant go out in the woods it is a little dangerous
But being outside even if rainy and cloudy is a life savior... I always look up into the sky too.. suck in the light

For CT you could just dip in a barrel or a outside tub? I dip in my barrel every single day. Always. I just stay in a minute or two..... and then I dry off and stay there nude on my terrace and let my whole body feel the light. Just for another minute... it is so beautiful....... The best moment of the day. And it takes almost no time, but it charges me big!
Maybe you could do something like that? Then the stress will not be there about having to CT for hours... when you have no time...... I think important is that you do it every day. I know for me it is.
It is a habit you do not want to miss.... and it feels like no stress... the opposite

Something has to work because even if it is very gray and rainy and stormy here I do not feel one bit depressed.. quite the opposite. Lots of challenges..... but I feel like unstoppable...lol
I need to run run forward..... I can manage any challenge..... it is so sexy to feel powerful like that. IDK if it is the reptile light... the raw seafood..... the CT... the horse barn.... or something else but I do feel very happy inside despite my life is chaos as always...lol Sometimes.. I think maybe I have gone mad   Something inside me creates this happiness, it is so weird!

it is like I cant live without it.. a part of me dies without suns light and I am not willing to let it

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-183825>

Yes, the 660 nm red one.
I'm doing my underarms because I want to see if it has any affect on my two skin tags that have been there forever. They rarely get any light being right in my armpit.
I went to the doc for a rare visit yesterday. Mainly to get a prescription for Ativan for my upcoming air travel in case I need it....being terrified for hours is too exhausting. I've also been using a subliminal recording every day so I'm hoping it will be enough and I won't need the medication.
I also got req's for some bloodwork since it's been 9 months since the last set. I asked him about ways to check into vagal tone and he had a HeartMath device right in the drawer that he brought out and hooked me up to. He suggested I look into getting one. Just went on their website and it's 25 off right now....hmmmm.
Also going to get an overnight pulse/ox test to evaluate me for possible apnea. Saw a new dentist recently and she said she suspects I might have it. That'll likely happen in the new year some time.

I am slowly getting ready for my trip to Mexico....made an appointment to wax the old beav and get a pedicure. My splurge. I could really use a massage but my Jeep decided it needed a spa day so I'm spending $400 on that instead. Bloody vehicles!

### endless — 2015-12-20T17:27:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-184594>

I took advantage of a sale to get an Inner Balance device for my phone, to take to Mexico primarily for help with my fear of flying, and the damn thing stopped working after 5 sessions. Now that it's too late to get a replacement before I leave. I was really looking forward to using it on the plane. I wonder if I made a big enough stink about it if they would overnight a replacement for no extra charge.....doubtful at this time of year.

### Inger — 2015-12-20T18:40:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-184607>

When I fly, I like the relaxing feeling about te fact that I am stuck and cannot do anything.... just relax...lol so I enjoy it!
If you are scared that the plane will drop down you must just remind yourself that flying in fact is way way less risky than driving a car!
and... if your plane crash you will move into the beautiful universe pretty sure.... in a car crash it is possible to get injured and disabled for life. Are you scared of driving?
Use the flight to explore the feeling of letting go  it is cool
and maybe watch the people around... I find that fascinating too.... people are fascinating
If I sit by a man... I use to think if something scary happens I will grab his hand and hold it tight....... so I have no fear

### endless — 2015-12-22T05:52:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-184741>

Thanks Inger.....I will keep these suggestions in mind for the flight.

Also, my HeartMath device is NOT broken, after all....it just needed a reboot. Yay! I'm planning on spending a lot of time on the flight working with it so really glad it's working again.
What a day.....started with discovering my old kidney impaired cat had pissed all over the dog bed AGAIN , and had to try and wash it in the bathtub since I need it clean and dry in 5 days when they go to the sitters. Then discovered the washing machine was falling apart. Literally....two of the control knobs seem to have spontaneously disintegrated. Took the top apart and discovered the plastic brackets that hold the control modules to the housing were completely gone. Strange that two of them failed at the same time. Cheap ass plastic. I'm going to try to use some epoxy to reattach them but probably not till I get back.

I've been woefully low energy/mood/compliance lately.....my dopamine must be suffering. I'm keeping up with the tanning 2x week and I'm getting morning light (not sun) but CT and DHA have fallen by the wayside in the last couple of weeks. Other than general cold temps in the house and working outside lightly dressed. No deliberate water CT lately. I'm so looking forward to the ocean....I think the place we're staying for one of the weeks has a pool, too, so I will do morning CT there as well. Starting to load up on DHA for the flight, more so because I've sucked at it lately. I've been craving red meat so have indulged that with elk, beef and venison in place of seafood. Today I got back on the boat with mussels for breakfast and cod for dinner. Tomorrow I will eat one of my precious jars of salmon roe provided by Jena, and I have another 2 jars set aside for the days leading up to my trip. Maybe some salmon patties for breakfast......

### endless — 2015-12-24T16:37:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-184866>

Thank you, I will! I have never needed it like I do now. Funny, after a much more 'aware' spring, summer and fall in which I was much more focused on light and Ct, I crashed HARD in mid November and am still struggling.  Weight gain (but I suspect bloating/fluid retention mostly), carb cravings, low energy/mood......yuck. I am going to watch the sunrise every day I am gone, hopefully from the beach or in the pool, get my morning 2 hours of sun and eat fish every day if I have to go stalk the fishermen to buy it!
Got two tanning bed sessions in this week at least. Morning UV has been spotty, generally I am catching a glimpse here and there through the open car window.

### endless — 2015-12-25T08:39:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-184916>

I've got stress related abdominal pain......here I thought I was doing ok leading up0 to this flight but my body thinks differently I guess. I recognize it because it happened once before, the night before a flight. I actually went to the ER that time because I was in so much pain and I didn't know why. It hasn't gotten as bad this time, probably because I know what it is and am working to manage it. I'm using the Inner Balance app to try and ease it. I doubt anyone is on today but I'm open to any other suggestions if anyone sees this.
Hope everyone is enjoying the holiday!

### endless — 2016-01-06T04:51:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-185536>

It's going to be hard to leave here and go back to the snow and dark! I'm still finding it too hot for a lot of the day but getting my morning/evening sun in and staying under an umbrella between 11-3. No sunscreen, no sunburn! No bug bites, either....some of the family are getting eaten alive by Mosquitos, no-see-ums and sand fleas but I'm not bothered. They bite me the odd time but no reaction or itching.
Right now it's 9pm and I'm sitting outside in my bikini listening to music wafting up from the square below....drinking a piña cada on the rocks. Tough life! I'm going home to a lot of snow so going to enjoy this while I can.

### endless — 2016-01-07T06:46:46+0200

### caroline — 2016-01-12T10:31:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-186057>
