### endless — 2016-01-12T17:43:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-186085>

I am home as of last night. I'll do a longer trip report later, have to get ready for work. I'm just doing my oil pulling/UFO session now. My gut is definitely a little off from the travel so seems like a good time to get some red light in there.

I hate how stunned I look in that pic....I was really tired after a bad sleep and had been waiting around for my mom at several mechanic shops for an hour before we stopped in at the base to see if the commandant was in. Back story....I took one of the ball caps from my fire dept with me in hopes of trading it for one from the local FD there....a common practice in fire depts. I went there the first full day we were in town, and was told I needed to come back later in the day. I couldn't, because the single vehicle we all had use of was elsewhere. Stopped in again the following week, same story, come back tomorrow. Again, I couldn't, but my mother was using the car to go to market in the nearby town (she sells coconut oil products at the local markets) so I gave the hat to her and asked her to stop in for me. She did, and did the trade, but said the commandant really wanted me to come in so he could meet me. So the next day after driving someone into town to meet the bus to PV (and getting several mechanic estimates for my mom's car), we stopped in and he was there. Really nice guy, wanted to come over to La Manz and take me out but was on duty until after we left. When we left he took his necklace off and gave it to me, and said he hoped I would come back soon. Maybe I will.....

### jenaf — 2016-01-12T20:09:26+0200

### endless — 2016-01-13T16:57:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-186177>

Ahhh, I don't know what's going on right now..... I'm really struggling. I thought coming home after a good hefty amount of UV exposure and all I would be feeling good and instead I am just plummeting downhill emotionally. Waking at 4am and experiencing really profound depression and despair.
Realized this morning that I am feeling exactly as I did exactly 6 years ago during the absolute darkest days of my life. I had just struggled through a difficult 6 months with my fiancee who was a narcissistic, sex and cocaine addicted compulsive liar who I loved but who really made my life hell. I had finally convinced him to go to recovery and was looking forward to moving forward with him in a new way when he came back and dumped me unceremoniously. I had nowhere to go and ended up living in my brother's basement for 8 months in severe depression. It was awful. It took me a long time to recover. I spent a LOT of time on myself afterwards and came to a good place eventually about my experience and even forgave my ex. So why do I feel like this current state is somehow connected? Other than the time of year being the same I can't figure it out.
I'm actually pretty scared of this....I don't think I could stand being in that state again. I don't have time for it. I just want to stay in bed and not engage with the world but I have work and responsibilities and nobody to do any of it but myself.
Kind of wondering if meeting someone I might be interested in is triggering this in some way...I've been single since 2010 when that happened. Gun shy about putting myself out there after a rough ride and having my self esteem completely destroyed. I'm still working on that. I guess I try not to think about it but I really am very lonely. I miss human contact.
Fuck, if this is the aftermath of vacation I think I'm better off staying home.

### fitness@home — 2016-01-13T18:13:09+0200

> Kind of wondering if meeting someone I might be interested in is triggering this in some way...

Sounds like it may have triggered an emotional response in you. On top of that you came home to a hell of a lot less UV.

More work to do on yourself, but you will get there. I have never perceived you as a quitter. Explore the emotions and feelings...follow them to their source.

Sending support from afar

### endless — 2016-01-18T02:02:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-186604>

Working on it....thanks Nicole.
Ok, I think I'm getting a handle on the situation....had a hellish work week coupled with a super heavy period and it kind of threw me for a loop. Getting back on track now. I needed to get a few things accomplished to get back in the groove. Today was my first 'day off' (sorta) so I started out with some morning light exposure by snow blowing/shovelling outside in tank top and shorts for an hour or so. I briefly considered hopping in the CT tub (inside, the tank is frozen outside) but was feeling motivated to other things so I got my 4th quarter GST remittance done instead. Cooked up some squash from the garden and made some 'veggie pucks' to add to the dog food with garlic, kale and turmeric. Payed a few bills and did a few chores, and I'm starting to feel a little better. Tomorrow I'm hitting the tanning salon and seeing the doc for some test results.
Finally listened to the January webinar and Q&A today, too. It's particularly apt for me right now with my dopamine issue. I didn't realize how well I had been doing before until lately. I tried to read Time 7 too but I'm not comprehending it as well as I'd like. Time to pull some of my precious salmon roe out of the freezer and see if I can give my brain a boost! Got the UV lights back up and running today too and used the UFO this morning on my gut again. Finally starting to return to normal bowel habits, more or less. Whatever it was that hit me (all of us, really) the first night in Mexico seemed to have lingering effects and I've felt off ever since.

Sun has been scarce here. It's been raining or snowing mostly non stop since I got home, with clouds hanging so low in the valleys that you feel you can reach up and touch them. I'll be driving through them today as I head over the mountains to the other side for a job. I'll have to rely on the tanning salon for now.
Yesterday there was a bit of clear sky so I was driving with the window down as long as I had it in sight. Amazing how warm it is on the skin even at this time of year. I can't wait for the day I can be at home in the morning on a sunny day and strip down in my snowy garden!

### endless — 2016-02-03T19:20:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-188086>

I'm starting to have hope for an early spring. The last several days have been brighter, no precip, with actual blue sky at times, instead of the low cloud that has been hanging here for months. A very welcome change. I can see the outlines of the garlic bed in the garden under the snow and I know that they are beginning to sprout even now. One of my favourite parts of early gardening is watching the pale little sprouts green up almost instantly once they reach the light.

Gave myself a pretty good knock on the head yesterday...dropped me to my knees for a few minutes. I was working so couldn't do much for a few hours, but iced when I got home. It really hurts to the tough so couldn't tolerate the cryohelmet unfortunately, just a gel pack that I wore to bed. I'll take the cryohelmet out to soften and use that today instead.

Hi Audrey....I'm ok, thanks! My head is better, I wore my Cryohelmet all evening and no headache today.
Actually saw some sun this afternoon which was a nice change. Continuing to roll with the punches here financially.....more bad news about my vehicle today and I won't be able to pay my rent until the middle of the month, but it is what it is....I'll just have to keep plugging away and do what I can. I dropped the gold membership for awhile, just can't justify spending $140 a month on it.

Hoping for some time at home tomorrow during the day...I want to try and excavate an area in the garden to set my lawn chair back up so I can take advantage of the sun when it decides to make an appearance. I can shovel a 4x4 spot down to the grass to make grounding easier and be surrounded by snow for reflection. 17 days to vitamin D!

### endless — 2016-02-15T23:32:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-189126>

7 days till UV! I've been trying to make it to the tanning salon twice a week as well, but I'm looking forward to the real thing! Unfortunately my brother's place has less sun than mine, I may have to create a little time each morning when I go over to feed the cats to strip down for a quick UV session. Mornings are so busy...

### endless — 2016-04-01T08:25:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-191810>

Yikes, time to pull my poor journal out of the weeds......

11 days till I'm back in my own bed in my own house!! I've been astonishingly busy since moving in here at my brother's place so the time has flown by. Winter is well and truly over, the temperature is climbing (22C today) and I'm ready to be home so I can hop in the tank first thing and hang out naked in the garden again.
Had a bit of a health crisis since I've been here, some excess overall inflammation as well as some acute inflammation in my index finger that seemed for all the world like gout! Josh F. gave me great advice and it resolved really quickly, thankfully. Working with ones hands is tough when you're down one dominant finger....
Still working on the overall inflammation but getting better. I am on the last day of a 5 day 'fast mimicking diet' like Ruben was talking about. I ate only 3 avocados a day with salt, and 4TBSP of greens powder. Plus water, of course. Abdominal bloating is WAY down and experiencing the usual lack of hunger from ketosis. I can see doing this once a month, for sure. I love the re-set I experience from a short fast, and the renewed appreciation for simple food. I have fresh greens for tomorrow morning (dandelion, cilantro, parsley, spinach) to blend with lemon juice in the morning, and a chunk of sockeye to go with it.

Have been communicating with my Mexican friend, el commandante.....many thanks to Lahelada for proofreading my Spanish before I sent my email! I don't know if anything will develop there since I will likely not be back to Mexico anytime soon. He is travelling to Utah in May, but that's not going to work either as my employee is already scheduled to be away. For the same reason I am going to miss the Quantum Canuck gathering in Vancouver! Bummer.....
I did meet another interesting (and attractive) guy last week who just bought one of the lots across the river from me, where I walk my dogs. I don't know if there's anything there, but I will see him frequently as he is building his house. Although, I suggested he jump in the river the other day and he informed me that he hates cold water. Hmmmm, that might be a problem!

### caroline — 2016-04-02T07:34:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-191862>

Hi krista ..... sounds like all is good!

### endless — 2016-04-02T08:18:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-191870>

Yes, all is pretty good Audrey, how about you?

Went out after dark for the first time in months....and forgot my UVEX. Oh well, just reminds me that I need to get a couple more pair for now until I can get the BPI tints for night done. Still want some BluTechs too, both are on the priority list.

First day back in the CT tank and it was awesome! 48F, full sun, I stayed in 25 min followed by an hour of naked sunbathing. I've missed that so much. Makes a world of difference. Wanted to listen to the April webinar but time got away from me and had to leave it. Probably Sunday....

Upside to being out tonight, I got to enjoy grilled ahi salad and mussels in Thai coconut broth, and a large glass of Malbec.

Well, because I think short fasts are beneficial, but I have a hard time doing just a water fast. This was easy and supposedly gives the same benefit. I just wanted to give it a try and see how I felt. I liked it. Like a little vacation for my digestive system without feeling like I wanted to eat my own arm...lol

Good idea, jack....I'll just start swimming past his property like a mermaid.  Speaking of which, today was absolutely amazing and I met some friends up at the nature park for some dog playtime. There was about a dozen people there, basking in the sun, but I was the only one in the river. Water felt like about 40. I wouldn't be in long at that temp but my tank is 50 and I could definitely wallow in there like a hippo.

### endless — 2016-04-04T19:02:13+0300

Interesting...I've basically been in ketosis since last week when I did the 5 day FMD, until last night when I went out for some food with family and ate potatoes. My stomach felt uncomfortable for awhile later in the evening, but when I woke up this morning I felt more 'normal' than I have in a long time. Got LOTS of sun yesterday, not sure if that was part of the reason. I woke up with a flat stomach, which didn't even happen when I was sorta 'fasting'. This inflammation and bloating I've been dealing with for the last several months has been persistent, but this morning it was totally gone. Think I will experiment with that a bit...

### caroline — 2016-04-05T04:07:23+0300

We continued to have GF butter and the other nite I guess I overdid the butter on my veg....altho I really did not have much to eat at all.

I felt so stuffed for hours and I was so uncomfortable I was beside myself. I have come to realise that it is the butter and so made a couple of batches of ghee yesterday.

One good thing about the whole30 .....it is easy to focus on what could be a problem pretty fast.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-14T20:43:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-192701>

Don't survive, thrive. Do life soaring. Our cells read and react to our environment; it shapes us and we are shaped by it.
The six best healers in the world that I know:
1\. Sunlight
2\. Un-fluoridated water
3\. magnetism/grounding to Earth/CT protocol
4\. seafood
5\. Self Confidence
6\. Friends
That is all...........Carry On.
[http://www.nature.com/articles/srep12029](http://www.nature.com/articles/srep12029)

Here is the key that most of the critics miss..........the order of the recipe is critical to the success. Your perception will be thrown off if you do it other ways. I can think of 3 recent members who went of the rails this way. The last 4 series pointed this point this out but few understand the implications. Neil BB and I have been offline talking about it. Your BUN/creat ratio and ketosis must be started before DHA loading. the blogs have been clear that the exclusion zone inside of cells manifests due to proteins within becoming hydrophilic first. How do you do you make things hydrophilic? You slowly add in electrons. The way to add them is step 4 above. The closer you are to the equator means you have more push from your current (DC) and then you may not need as much........unless there is something peculiar about your environment, job, eyes, skin.

Several of the people well versed in physics have contacted me offline and are simply stunned by the implications of this webinar. Good. Nature should stun us.

Even though I have no science background, I was able to perceive how phenomenal the April Webinar is intuitively.

I think for a lot of us, we may never be able to understand what you are trying to teach at the academic levels others are able to. But this does not damper my enthusiasm one bit. I feel so privileged to be able to share in the excitement of your vast realm of knowledge, expertise, infinite wisdom, and awesome ground breaking theories. I am so proud to be accepted as a member of this tribe.

I have a very simple way to look at complicated knowledge that is beyond my 'paygrade'. If it is true, then it must manifest itself in some form that we can relate to. And that always mean there should be a way we can experience and/or manipulate (biohack) it. It doesn't matter if you don't understand how A gets to Z. As long as Z is true, then it can become one's reality if one is brave enough to embrace it.

(Going one step further, from a spiritual perspective, I think even if Z may or may not be true, it can become manifested as one's reality...and that really can get me into trouble sometimes...)

Thank you again for everything you have done for us. We are truly blessed.

### endless — 2016-04-19T20:06:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-193036>

So it occurred to me this morning, while I was standing on a ladder in my white 'Barefoot Dreams' bathrobe, barefoot, hammering a nail into the side of my garden shed, that there might be a reason the neighbours think I'm a bit strange....
Often when I go outside in the morning to walk around and ground, I get distracted by things that need to be done, and so find myself doing them...usually in my bathrobe or naked with a sarong wrapped around me and bare feet. This morning it was hanging out my duvet in the sun, inspecting the raspberry canes, replacing a nail that I needed to hold my rolled up tarp up on the shed, hauling out the lounge chair into the yard and picking nettles for breakfast. Free-form multitasking.....

And it looks like I have a blind date, set up by my sister in law...... That ought to be interesting!

Doris you are new here but I must say you are breath of fresh air........you really have made this website fresh for me. It was getting stale and then you came in and pushed some of the dead air out.........I rather like that and I wanted to say it here. Embracing the beginner's mind is how I tripped over these things.......it came from nothing I knew or was taught. That is why I am sure any of you can do what I did because we are all starting at the same point.

I've come full circle on education and experience, especially when it comes to knowledge. People see thing with the lenses they learned. It takes character to divorce you mind from beliefs. It took me 40 years to get that courage. Medicine applies technical solutions to adaptive challenges today and this is why it is failing. Instead we should all follow our internal compass again and again, and soon our life seems to gain a revolutionary momentum for our soul. Embrace its pull and its gravity. Sometimes, when you ride nature's waves you begin to trust the revolution brewing within. You begin to innovate your life with your real intent and your "WHY" begins to manifest our of the ashes of what was.

Yesterday, I went for a haircut in Austin, and my stylist’s jaw dropped. She could not believe how much hair has grown since she last saw me at the end of January. She said it was like I got a whole new head of hair. So I started to talk to her about the sun, and other important teachings of yours. She was so excited that she wanted to learn more so she can help some of her young clients who have thinning hair and beards.

I would also like to report that since February, I have started at least 16 people on sungazing and eating seafood. Some of them live as far away as in Hong Kong, Toronto and Vancouver!

I have been wondering if it would be okay for me to start a journal on my starfish experiences?

### endless — 2016-04-22T23:51:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-193275>

Welcome to journal land, lohd2015!

I realized today when I was cutting the grass that the amount of time I spend grounding in spring/summer/fall is huge, and in winter it's pathetic. Unless I'm leaving the property I'm usually barefoot. In fact I can't bear wearing shoes, even little summer flats. I overheat badly when my feet are enclosed. I might not wear socks again till late fall, except in my bunker boots.

### endless — 2016-04-24T03:35:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-193335>

So I had my first osteopath appointment today! Not an osteopathic physician, but a practitioner of manual osteopathy. It was fantastic. I almost fell asleep. He worked on my extremely tight hips and also did some work around some of my organs. I randomly had mentioned my CT tank when we were discussing magnesium supplementation (in the context that I add a lot of mag sulphate to it...) and he asked me if I was a follower of Jack Kruse. First time I've randomly met someone who is familiar with the protocols! I will definitely be going back.

### endless — 2016-04-27T02:13:07+0300

Some serious nuttery going on around FB these days.....good reminder to not buy into the crazy shit!
Speaking of nuttery, woo alert ahead. I came across this guy [www.vegastarhealings.com](http://www.vegastarhealings.com) on another members FB post, with a positive testimonial. Normally I would think twice or more before considering it but my intuition told me it was a good thing for me to do, so I did. I had the chakra balancing (crown through heart were strong, solar plexus through base were blocked) and energetic cord cutting done. I submitted a list of people from my past and present that I suspected of energetic parasitism and 14 of them were, when he scanned. I had those fuckers chopped! Lol. So, it's been a few days. I feel lighter and looser in my solar plexus area, normally a place of tension. I haven't had too much interaction with the former parasites yet so not sure what I will notice there but I'm hopeful that it will positively impact those relationships!

### caroline — 2016-05-07T02:51:07+0300

### endless — 2016-05-08T19:10:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-194381>

Finally got into the garden yesterday. It feels good to spend the entire day barefoot in the dirt. No sunburn. Well, a little pink on face and upper chest but it's faded to tan today. I'm sitting naked in the garden now after my CT and just shading my head and chest under an umbrella while the rest of me soaks it up.

### endless — 2016-05-11T02:08:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-194530>

Heading to Vancouver tomorrow for a visit with my 91 year old Oma. I'll be staying in the guest suite of the extended care facility she's in and trying to mitigate as best I can. Taking 4 gallon glass jugs of my own water which I will supplement with Gerolsteiner, my UVEX, some seaweed and my bathing suit in case I can find a quiet corner on the grounds for morning UV. Then 2 days with Jena before coming home! It's nice to get away for a few days but I really love it here this time of year so I will also be happy to get home again.

### endless — 2016-05-13T18:47:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-194705>

I've discovered a possible reason for the longevity of my Oma, who I've never considered to be in particularly good health. She's now 92 and has various long standing health issues but she was born and grew up in Husum, on the North Sea. They ate a LOT of shrimp and fish, as that was what was cheapest. She was born in 1925 and lived on the coast of Germany until emigrating to Canada in the early 50s (to Edmonton).

### caroline — 2016-05-14T07:19:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-194762>

The things you discover! Did you have a nice visit? How is her cognition?

### endless — 2016-05-22T07:41:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-195173>

The visit was challenging...her cognition is really bad. She has no short term memory and gets very confused about the past now as well, not able to identify people from photos. She doesn't understand distances anymore, and gets pissed off that people who live far away don't visit her more often because she thinks everyone lives nearby. I'm glad to have had some time with her, even if she likely won't remember it. Each time now could be the last time.

Did have a great 2 day visit with Jena and got some good nature time, CT in the ocean, and tanning in! Also a quick visit with the VIGR boys who were in town for the CHFA trade show. We met up for oysters downtown.

I started methylene blue today....using 100mcg once in the morning. I noticed that I was NOTICING more, if that makes sense....while driving a route I drive almost daily I was seeing things I had never noticed before. Could be a coincidence, but I thought it was interesting. No obvious physical effects yet.

### endless — 2016-05-22T20:54:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-195188>

It absolutely amazes me how childish people can be on the internet....it's like stepping back into high school sometimes.

Finally got my hanging baskets planted and I'm so happy! I love this time of year, beautifying the space where I spend most of my time is a great way to relax and enjoy my home and my yard. No naked gardening today, it's cool and rainy, but the plants are digging it. Off to walk the doggies and then plant some more stuff in my veggie garden.

### caroline — 2016-05-23T05:02:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-195194>

It keeps me outside a lot, but I still have time to keep up with my friends online. Wouldnt want to have to hire a social media team to keep my online life in order!

The forecast is for two more days of rain and cool weather, followed by the return of the sun! It's actually working out fairly well, gardening wise.... got my seed crops planted in the cool and damp so I don't have to stress about them drying out during the day while I am working, and next week when it gets sunny again I can plant my warm weather starts outside and they will be happy in the heat.

I'm excited to get back into my morning routine of sunning outside, with or without CT. I have a work project for the next two months that is going to keep me busy from early in the morning some days and cut into my am light time, so I'll have to make up for it on the rest of the days and try and stay home as long as possible.

> So I had my first osteopath appointment today! Not an osteopathic physician, but a practitioner of manual osteopathy. It was fantastic. I almost fell asleep. He worked on my extremely tight hips and also did some work around some of my organs. I randomly had mentioned my CT tank when we were discussing magnesium supplementation (in the context that I add a lot of mag sulphate to it...) and he asked me if I was a follower of Jack Kruse. First time I've randomly met someone who is familiar with the protocols! I will definitely be going back.

I've never heard of an Osteopath. I too have tight hips and psoas muscle, and chronic lower back stiffness. I've been to different kinds of physiotherapy, chiro, massage and back specialists, but nothing seems to help. I do have adrenal fatigue, which I believe is connected to my hip/back issues. I'm working on improving now with sun and seafood etc., however I'm wondering if your Osteopath visit gave lasting improvement. I've had physio treatment that would help for only a day or two. Thanks.

### endless — 2016-06-10T16:59:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-196312>

it didn't give lasting improvement, but only because (I think) I haven't been back since the first treatment. I think if I can go once a week for a series of treatments it will definitely be long lasting. I have a bad habit of prioritizing other things over my self care. Thanks for reminding me to make another appointment!

in response to your osteopath post: if it wasnt lasting then two things may have occured = the primary lesion was not addressed and therefore everything returned to the way it was, or stability/movement at the most basic level was needed to integrate the changes, but wasnt so then the brain reverted to what it has patterned to do. from my perspective

### caroline — 2016-06-20T07:16:52+0300

### endless — 2016-07-08T10:02:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-198049>

I'm 47 years old, and over my lifetime I have been blessed (?) to have three men at var0us times in the role of father. Honestly not sure 'blessed ' is the right word

My biological father was not around long for my childhood....early divorce. Visitation didn't really develop a strong bond when I was a kid, because his next wife treated me like an unwelcome guest. It wasn't until I was an adult that I developed a closer relationship with him, working around his lifelong severe clinical depression.

My current stepfather is slowing down....when he first came onto the scene he was someone I HATED with a passion.....but also loved his 4 kids that came to visit from Denver CO every summer . I was conflicted....life was good Sometimes.  We have a much better adult relationship.

The third 'father' in my life was Randy, who came into my life when I was too young to remember.My mom met him after leaving my dad and moving to Calgary. I was too young to remember the progression of that meeting, but as far as I was concerned, Randy was my daddy. He was my daddy for the happiest time of my life. I look back on this and am amazed that he just took me on with no question, even though he didn't have kids of his own.
Some of my best memories are related to Randy. I remember trips to visit his mother in rural Alberta. I was only 3 years younger than his twin sisters, so we were inseparable!
Anyway, he passed away last week...so I'm reflecting on my time with him and on fathers in general. I feel lucky to have made it this far without losing any parents. Knowing what I know now makes it harder because I see some of them doing things that I know will affect their health and longevity but there isn't much I can do about it except try and be a good example.

### [email redacted] — 2016-07-08T10:40:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-198050>

I am in London has to do with 'chemtrails' anyway I have a question and hopefully Dr Kruse or someone else can help. I have a good friend here and I am shocked to see him blinking his eyes all the time sometimes really kind of scary. Is that likely to be some kind of 'light issue'. I have been here a week and have NOT seen a sunrise or a sunset a kind of grey murk most of the time. Even some guesses on this 'blinking' issue would be appreciated very much

### Jack Kruse — 2016-07-08T14:29:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-198055>

You blink a lot to cool the cornea so that you can absorb more light that is present. This happens when you live in a place like London or have a low dopamine state.

### [email redacted] — 2016-07-08T21:10:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-198073>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=198055)
>
> You blink a lot to cool the cornea so that you can absorb more light that is present. This happens when you live in a place like London or have a low dopamine state.

Thanks very much for that Jack, it makes thing in relation to my friend a bit clearer. One thing I notice in London and I have been here only 4 days is forget about sunrise and sunset. You do NOT see either one and the people I checked with said that is 'normal' there. I put a fair amount of that down to 'chemtrails' there was one sunny day and the spraying was INTENSE. Worse than anything I have seen in LA.

Most of the time you have this dome of cloud I won't say 'clouds' as it is like one big continuous dome of cloud. I was also in Yorkshire for a week and it was much better there real clouds, sunrises and sunsets a bit at least yes it was cloudy but 'real' nice white puffy clouds. I think London is a dreadful place to live and my friend though a super 'smart' guy and the author of several excellent books is suffering I would say and all the blinking sort of shows it. Thanks again for the quick responce Jack

### endless — 2016-07-09T18:29:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-198134>

Make yourselves at home, guys.....

I discovered SUP recently and LOVE it! I was lucky to find a used board for a really good price and couldn't not buy it. It's amazing for core and stability. My SIL convinced me to try a stretch of the river with her the other day (I was a little nervous, and had only planned to do flat water for awhile) and it was great! I need to get an ankle strap but I managed to not fall off, even in the 'rapids' we have (class II). And I can feel it in my ass, legs and torso today. Moving water while standing is a whole different animal than I am used to. Can't wait to do it again!

### endless — 2016-08-24T03:36:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-200109>

Ok, the FasciaBlaster has arrived (thanks Dawn!!).....and I'm scared of it!  Interesting insight though. I've been reading the damn FaceBook group and watching YouTube videos about it so I've seen and heard a ton about people's experiences already and thought I would be ready to jump right in there but.......I'm scared of pain. Having realized that, I started reflecting on all the things I have avoided because I am scared of getting hurt. There's a lot. I have a big ass tattoo and can sit completely calmly for blood draws and stuff, but pain from other sources is very scary to me.

This is the same thing I experienced when I started to CT. I used to be scared to be cold. I would never consider leaving the house without enough clothing that I would never be cold, no matter what time of year. If I ever did get cold, I would panic. I had to overcome that fear in order to CT in cold water and take walks without a jacket, etc. Now I am often the one who goes to an evening event in shorts and a t shirt with no sweater or whatever while everyone else is bundling up. So, I got over it. Now I have to get over this!

### endless — 2016-08-24T19:29:07+0300

So, oddly enough, the first thing I did after admitting to being scared of being hurt was fall on my steps and hurt myself. Now I have a big swollen bruise on my left quad...probably very similar to what I will get from the blaster! It's like the universe is saying 'See? It's not so bad'.....

### fitness@home — 2016-08-24T20:46:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-200147>

> Does she use the nugget on you? I'm having a hard time understanding how people use that....

Yes, she does. For me to apply the pressure I want on my upper thigh with it, I place it between me and the wall (while standing) and lean into it.

When I did this on that area of fascia in my upper left thigh and hit it just right, I could feel a sensation of it in my thigh wrapping around to my hip area. I use inoculation method. Press, hold for 30-60 seconds, move on to the next point about 1/2 inch farther.

### endless — 2016-09-02T06:53:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-200693>

Well, well.....my appetite for seafood has finally returned. Wonder if it's the days getting shorter. I'm still eating a fair amount of seasonal carbs but just this past week I've been back to eating fish after months of not being attracted to it at all. Doesn't hurt that I had this amazing Thai seafood salad for lunch the other day....mussels and prawns and fish and squid in a typically flavourful dressing with a few veggies (celery, onion, tomato, lettuce). I already want that again...might have to start making it myself.
The fruit consumption this season has been great! I've indulged in each thing as it has been at peak season, enjoyed it over that week or so, and moved on. Most recently I've been eating prune plums, next up is apples and pears. I've also eaten a LOT of watermelon this summer. I'm not going to miss it when the colder weather hits.
Theres a forest fire burning on the other side of the ridge from me....earlier today it was a bit sketchy with high winds and I was starting to get ready for possible evac. notice, but now the wind has died off and light rain has started so hoping by daylight it'll not be any closer!
I made myself a mini fascia blaster, by removing two of the claws on the big one. I don't want to cut it until I can order another and it's expensive and a pain to ship it here.

Stay safe and get the hell out of Dodge if the fire heads your way!

Aahhh, the sun is weakening!  I'm still getting full sun on skin but the intensity is way down. I love lots of things about this time of year but that isn't one of them. Shorter days are upon us. I bought a bunch of spring salmon and the freezer is filling with that too. Sauerkraut is made, garlic is curing, carrots and beets are ready to pull.

Considering buying the Carbonshades, for night time use out of the house. I just don't wear the UVEX out. Not that I go out at night much anyway, but it would be good to have them for the occasional time I do. Still waiting for more people to post pics of some of the other styles!

### Jenny S — 2016-09-13T02:15:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-201363>

Just found these [http://www.zennioptical.com/beyond-uv-blue-blocker](http://www.zennioptical.com/beyond-uv-blue-blocker) I checked & they block 60 % blue light & you can get prescription or just readers if you like. Or these block nearly all but are yellow [https://www.amazon.com/Cyxus-Vintag...1\_1?ie=UTF8&psc=1&refRID=NA0ZACY7FS96D7XH7N9Y](https://www.amazon.com/Cyxus-Vintage-Blocking-Headaches-Computer/dp/B01AHK7LMW/ref=pd_cart_vw_1_1?ie=UTF8&psc=1&refRID=NA0ZACY7FS96D7XH7N9Y)

### caroline — 2017-02-08T09:22:22+0200

### endless — 2017-03-04T18:57:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-209618>

Haha, unfortunately not much Audrey! I've just been busy and my computer time has consisted of jumping on FB. I've been staying part time at my brother's place and I find it frustrating trying to type more than a few words on my phone.

What a winter! We've had everything from deep cold to huge dumps of snow to huge dumps of rain. Lot of sun for part of it, and then gloomy socked in days for weeks. I'm really ready for the end but apparently mother nature is not as we are still hovering around freezing and getting more snow. Melt, dammit!
I've been far too sedentary the last month as well. Before that I was getting into a groove on the xc ski trails, getting both me and the dogs exercised and uv'd most days. Then the conditions went to shit and I couldn't get out for awhile and now I'm finding it harder to get back into it. Just bought a TRX-style suspension trainer that was immediately borrowed by a friend, so when I get it back Im looking forward to trying it out!
Ct has been sporadic, not much in water, some in snow and some just in cold air. Once the river is accessible I will start back doing dips there, right now that isn't possible because of snow and ice.

I'm caring for my brother's dog for a couple of months while he is overseas, and learning the ins and outs of cannabis cancer treatment. He was diagnosed right before they left. They refused the biopsy, not wanting to put him through anesthesia, but the vet was pretty sure. SO every day we have a regimen of proteolytic enzymes, cannabis oil, turmeric and medicinal mushrooms going on. I've taken on a lot in addition to my already busy life but hey, it's my pattern....

So I finally had success with the glasses I ordered from Zenni....the 550 turned out pretty good and so did the 500. The lack of anti-reflective coating is a bit annoying but I can live with it. Now have to find the time and $$ to go get REAL glasses (prescription) to tint for night driving.

I watched that movie, 'Captain Fantastic' last night. It was interesting because I actually DID grow up like that, for awhile. Or, at least, what COULD have been like that if the adults in my life had made my upbringing a priority as the father did in the movie. In my situation the kids were definitely not the focus of the whole experience. I'd MUCH rather have been taught the way they were than left to my own devices to work my way through the correspondence school curriculum on my own. Nevertheless, I learned a lot of things that most of my peers did not. It was a definite advantage in ninth grade (my first year back in the 'real world') when our class did a 'survival' camping trip in November, north of Edmonton and I was the only one who could start a fire.  Damn city kids.....

Off to walk the menagerie.

### OptimalSkr — 2017-03-05T22:33:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-209711>

Just wondering. Which lenses did you order from zenni? And how much did the bpi tints costs? I was thinking of doing the same thing. Have ordered from zenni before now just have to contact bpi. I have a pair of blutech that I wear all the time but I want a true blue block for night with a prescription pair.

### endless — 2017-03-09T21:08:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-209970>

I ordered plain, uncoated lenses from Zenni (which are free with the frames). You have to make sure to NOT get anti reflective coating, that will screw it up. The dyes were $30US each (I got both the 450 and the 500/550)and I bought a lens holder for $25. It was fairly easy to do and I'm pretty happy with the results.

### OptimalSkr — 2017-03-09T22:22:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-209988>

Thanks for the info. I'm gonna order another pair from zenni and contact bpi to get this done. So do you wear the 450 in the day and 550 at night? I see the 600 is complete blue block. Wonder if that would be too dark at night might be interesting.

### endless — 2017-03-09T23:24:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-209994>

I will use the 450 if I'm indoors during the day with no natural light, or doing computer work. 500 or 550 at night. I think the 600 would be hard to function with, but maybe ok for bumbling around at home. Don't forget the light hitting your skin has impact too, so reducing ambient light is also important. If I'm hanging out by candlelight at night I might not even wear my glasses, but if using amber bulbs or anything higher lux I put them on.

### endless — 2017-08-01T08:52:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-220590>

Hi Audrey,
No, I did not get a Quantlet....perhaps in future though. I'm place marking yet again because I really do intend to post in here again. I think the thought of 'catching up' has been to daunting, so I will not try, and just start from now.

Fully into the peak of summer now here, with garden burgeoning with food and the river beckoning every spare moment. I resent every moment that work ties me away from my little oasis. The heat has been brutal...many fires north of us but so far so good for my area. Thankfully my skin is now used to the intensity from my morning full body exposure. Still have to seek shade midday since my heat stroke experience last year but no burning!

Finally having some success with reducing inflammation by following the low lectin diet plan espoused by Steven Gundry. I've come to the conclusion that food DOES matter, at least for me, at this time. Light hygiene and EMF mitigation is of course important but my persistent 'puffiness' has finally subsided by eliminating a few things from my diet.

I bought a good quality Far IF sauna and am looking forward to using it but with these temps it's been the last thing on my mind. CT, on the contrary, has been welcome and amazing. We (the pups and I ) spend time in the river every day, and occasionally take the paddle board out.

T minus 2 days and counting till the first family arrives for the family reunion/memorial for my stepfather who passed last July. Roasting a pig and spending a weekend on the river. I'm hoping my garden is up to the task of salad for 200 people.....

### caroline — 2017-08-01T11:18:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-220602>

Hi Krista ....It sounds like you are really enjoying your summer and especially your river.

I had a look at the lectin diet .... It is very interesting. I am sure I would feel a lot better if I stayed away from high lectin foods. I have always known that I do well/best with seafood and a bit of GF beef and not a lot else. I can manage greens okay and try to stay away from fruit now that it is winter.

Are you serious ....200 people???????

### drezy — 2017-08-01T17:26:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-220623>

> [endless said:](/goto/post?id=220590)
>
> \\Finally having some success with reducing inflammation by following the low lectin diet plan espoused by Steven Gundry. I've come to the conclusion that food DOES matter, at least for me, at this time. Light hygiene and EMF mitigation is of course important but my persistent 'puffiness' has finally subsided by eliminating a few things from my diet.

I think you are on to something there and also may have a good indicator for the future. My mother slowly acquired a massive health library. Sadly she's crashing her health anyway. I remember the D'Adamo "Eat Right..." series was the first time I heard about lectins and it explained so much about many of my issues. Gundry takes it to the next level. Did you hear his interview over at self hacked? You're likely ahead of that level of info but I always like to hear the voice of people if I can (maybe just my quirk). Potatoes and lentils used to be a killer for me. Today I mostly avoid them.

I stumbled on the idea of using your kryptonite foods as a redox test accidentally by eating something at a party. When I've been doing Docs routine right I have zero to low reactions to my leptin foods. About once a month I'll down something that would usually leave me hurting just to see if my "environment" is still right for me. I've found that to be very helpful as a mini BUN/creat like measure.

Enjoy your time with family. Sorry to hear about your stepdad. My own mother is seriously falling apart in front of me and part of me doesn't want to admit that at this rate she won't be around much longer, but it's true. I can tell that I'm not going to be very good at losing people close to me. It feels lie a kind of claustrophobia.

# Thread: Dosing DHEA using labs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dosing-dhea-using-labs.3227/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bigknitwit — 2012-08-24T18:09:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dosing-dhea-using-labs.3227/post-46736>

How do you settle on a DHEA dose using serum labs? My serum DHEAs was 72 (range of 40-325mcg) and my ASI DHEA (DHEA + DHEAs) was 6 (range 3-10). I had been doing 4mg sublingually, but had to stop temporarily. Now I want to restart. I'm also wondering about doing the 4mg sublingual like before, and then adding in my 7-keto DHEA. But I'm wondering if the 4mg has to come way up. I figure there is better absorption sublingual avoiding the liver, but I'm not sure to what extent. Also noticing the vast difference in units of the supplement (mg) versus the lab values (mcg = factor of 1000 less).

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-25T01:35:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dosing-dhea-using-labs.3227/post-46861>

I'd say Dr K can probably judge that by experience a bit but really everybody is unique. I'd say the best way to go about this is to guestimate your dose, and then retest and adjust as you go along.

### shilohman — 2012-08-25T02:08:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dosing-dhea-using-labs.3227/post-46870>

Soul is absolutely correct. You need to supplement, test, adjust and retest. 4 mg is very low. Personally, I take 200 mg of DHEA and 200 mg of 7-Keto DHEA. With DHEA, you are better off starting high and working downward. Dr. Kruse could tell you after seeing your labs in a consult or you could just do your own N-1.

### vkiernan — 2012-08-25T02:49:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dosing-dhea-using-labs.3227/post-46881>

> **shilohman;47287 wrote:** Soul is absolutely correct. You need to supplement, test, adjust and retest. 4 mg is very low. Personally, I take 200 mg of DHEA and 200 mg of 7-Keto DHEA. With DHEA, you are better off starting high and working downward. Dr. Kruse could tell you after seeing your labs in a consult or you could just do your own N-1.

I wonder what the conversion rate is though when taking sublingal? It may sound low but may not be. Any idea?

### bigknitwit — 2012-08-25T03:05:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dosing-dhea-using-labs.3227/post-46891>

I'm also wondering about better conversion sublingually. I think you lose 90% (if I remember correctly) of oral hormones thanks to the liver. That's why progesterone creams have a much lower dose. I would \*assume\* the conversion rate would be similar for sublingual DHEA. So 4mg sublingual would be like 40mg in capsule....?

Shilohman - how did you know if your dose was too high??

### Jack Kruse — 2012-08-25T03:11:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dosing-dhea-using-labs.3227/post-46894>

SL topical and PO are all different......for example I found in me topical DHEA works great. Oral too. SL not so good. Everyone is different.

### Endless — 2012-08-24T18:18:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters.3228/post-46738>

Does anybody know if the raw oysters sold shucked in the little plastic tubs are ok to eat raw? Or must you stick to live ones...

### SeaHorse — 2012-08-24T19:20:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters.3228/post-46749>

I only feel comfortable eating oysters raw that have come out of their shells alive. They do live out of water for much longer than mussels and clams....up to a week or more so you can keep them in the fridge...I'm lucky to be able to buy live shellfish. I think I would cook any that were pre shucked, but not processed.

### Endless — 2012-08-24T19:33:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters.3228/post-46753>

That's kinda what I was feeling too....I wish I had better access to live shellfish!

**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bigknitwit — 2012-08-24T20:15:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-k2-in-osteoporosis-dosages.3232/post-46766>

Is anyone doing this dose (45mg/day)? Where did you source your K2? Butter oil? Fermented cod liver oil? Some food source? My K2 supplement has 2mg per capsule!

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-24T21:02:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-k2-in-osteoporosis-dosages.3232/post-46780>

Thorne Research K2 in MCT oil suspension 1mg/drop.

Lef.org K2 @ 15mg/pill.

I'm doing it and have been for 10months now. I got a DEXA scan this week.... waiting for results. Kinda nervous.

You can use pasteured butter or cod liver oil or organs as add ons but if you're seriously deficient, it probably won't be enough I think.

### ealachan — 2012-08-24T21:11:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-k2-in-osteoporosis-dosages.3232/post-46784>

Man, here I thought I was hardcore taking 90mcg instead of the 45mcg recommended on the bottle of Jarrow that I got...lol

### nonchalant — 2012-08-24T21:20:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-k2-in-osteoporosis-dosages.3232/post-46786>

I use Carlson's vitamin K2 Menatrenone 5g. I noticed after I started taking it that all these needle jabs aren't bruising me like before.

### Shijin13 — 2012-08-24T21:21:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-k2-in-osteoporosis-dosages.3232/post-46787>

This is what I take... I take 45mg/day (max dose)

[http://www.vrp.com/bone-and-joint/ultra-k2](http://http://www.vrp.com/bone-and-joint/ultra-k2)

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-24T21:37:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-k2-in-osteoporosis-dosages.3232/post-46792>

> **Nonchalant;47196 wrote:** I use Carlson's vitamin K2 Menatrenone 5g. I noticed after I started taking it that all these needle jabs aren't bruising me like before.

bruising actually is related to the amount of pressure they put on the vein after they pull out the needle... ask me how I know

The more pressure you put on and the longer, the less bruising tends to happen. You gotta do this yourself b/c the people who draw your blood usually have something else to do instead. But yeah, ever since a nurse told me that recently, I've stopped looking like a junkie finally.

### Jasmin — 2012-08-25T09:15:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-k2-in-osteoporosis-dosages.3232/post-46970>

I was told by dr. K that I should consider taking 15mg K2 along with 10000IU D3 a day.

# Thread: Creme Bulgari
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/creme-bulgari.3234/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-08-24T20:48:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/creme-bulgari.3234/post-46773>

Thinking of using the yogurt starter to make it with Heavy Whipping Cream, this is called creme bulgari. Do you think putting this in hot coffee would kill the good cooties?

### ealachan — 2012-08-24T20:54:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/creme-bulgari.3234/post-46775>

> **Cavemam;47183 wrote:** Thinking of using the yogurt starter to make it with Heavy Whipping Cream, this is called creme bulgari. Do you think putting this in hot coffee would kill the good cooties?

It depends on the strains of bacteria - some can tolerate heat better than others. Some die at 120 degrees, some hang on up to 140-160. I would check the starter you're using and see if you can Google up the heat tolerances of the specific bugs.

### kathylu — 2012-08-24T20:55:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/creme-bulgari.3234/post-46776>

As long as your coffee is not more than 125 degrees F (which is very hot to the touch), the little buggers should be fine.

# Thread: Hack my (non) sleep.....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-non-sleep.3236/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 66
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Souldanzer — 2012-08-24T21:33:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-non-sleep.3236/post-46790>

I seriously need to fix my sleep. My cortisol is "okay", I take progesterone, I take DHEA, my lights never come on at night, my computer has f.lux, a dimmed screen and I wear blueblockers, my room has a blackout cover over the window at night and I sleep with ear plugs. I stop eating at least 4 hours before bedtime.

I thought those were the major offenders. I fall asleep fine though sometimes it takes a while. Most nights I wake at around 3am unable to get back to sleep until 6am or so. It ruins my day. It takes the little bit of brain function from me I might actually still have and it kicks off major headaches.

Is the only thing left that could be destroying my sleep that I am one of those "extremely sensitive" to light people and I need to turn my computer off at sunset? Or am I missing something?

### Pamela — 2012-08-24T21:38:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-non-sleep.3236/post-46793>

Have you tried L-Tryptophan or Melatonin before bed?

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-24T21:44:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-non-sleep.3236/post-46798>

> **Pamela;47203 wrote:** Have you tried L-Tryptophan or Melatonin before bed?

I've tried melatonin a few times but it won't prevent the awakenings. Melatonin also will prevent the prolactin surge from happening for women during the night so that's why Dr K doesn't recommend it for longterm use I think.

I'm on a neurotransmitter support program through Dr Kalish so I am getting tons of 5-htp and tyrosine with all the co-factors. I just got my results (still low) and have been ordered to add L-dopa to the mix. Not sure if that would mess with my sleep.

### Pamela — 2012-08-24T21:48:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-non-sleep.3236/post-46800>

Personally I am good also on falling asleep and wake up pretty regularly 5 hours later. What I have found works for me is to take a cal-mag capsule and about 3 drops of liquid melatonin (NOW brand) which is really very little. I usually fall back asleep within 10 minutes or so.

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-24T21:52:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-non-sleep.3236/post-46802>

Thanks, Pamela. My calcium is over the range, so no calcium for me. I could try to do some sublingual mg threonate maybe. My melatonin is sublingual, too, but I've tried that before and it just made me really groggy during the day b/c then I still didn't sleep.

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-24T22:16:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-non-sleep.3236/post-46810>

You might try an evening CT session. Personally I sleep much more deeply after CT.

### nonchalant — 2012-08-24T22:21:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-non-sleep.3236/post-46815>

I think you should try turning off the computer at dusk. I'll send you some paperbacks.

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-24T22:32:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-non-sleep.3236/post-46817>

> **Nonchalant;47225 wrote:** I think you should try turning off the computer at dusk. I'll send you some paperbacks.

hehe yea, we keep taking actual books to the goodwill, I'll start saving them for you.

Perhaps look in a different direction for help too - like in setting your own circadian things. . .look at what happens in the a.m., perhaps use an alarm for a bit and at the same time every day get up, get out and stare at that damn light. Maybe anchoring that side of things will help your body and brain sort out the night time side.

I know that means some days will be not enough sleep - just nap those days, but make sure at the same time each day (or put that smartypants brain of yours to use) and calculate for each day when the sun will be in the same relative location in the sky so you adjust wake up time based on sun location.

Once we started anchoring the a.m., nighttime just fell into place nicely. Of course being able to step out my back door in pj's, and having a child that gets me up helps.

One last thing, meant to say this when I saw a post last week. I saw a study that people who "Watch the sun set behind mountains or over the horizon" jump start the melatonin and get good and sleepy. Like we are wired to see the sun set behind those mountains and our body will respond.

I've really started to enjoy our evening time of sitting outside watching the sun go down.

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-24T22:55:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-non-sleep.3236/post-46824>

> **T Field;47220 wrote: You might try an evening CT session. Personally I sleep much more deeply after CT.
>
>
> I do..... no go
>
>
>
>
>
> Nonchalant;47225 wrote:** I think you should try turning off the computer at dusk. I'll send you some paperbacks.

Thanks. Thing is, my brain is broken and reading skill is on of the things I lost 4 years ago  Websites and forums is about all I can handle without getting a major headache for remembering nothing in the end. I have a stack of books on my nightstand.... to be read..... not giving up yet

>
>
>
> I've really started to enjoy our evening time of sitting outside watching the sun go down.

Thanks, Cavemam. I've been doing the sunset watching and I do like it. I go for a walk or hike usually, sometimes I work in my garden and watch it. Or I'm on my bike. It does do something to my brain and feels like an "off" switch. I think it helps me fall asleep. It doesn't help me \*stay\* asleep.

I've been thinking about the wakeup time issue for a while now, too. I think my issue with that has been that I need around 10hrs of sleep, which means i would have to go to bed darn early! Which wouldn't be so bad actually..... b/c my brain goes on \*off\* at night anyways and I'm just doing mind numbing things. I think I might try it. But yeah, the tired days that come with that.... they s\*ck. Not that I'm not tired anyways if I don't sleep correctly so it might be a no-loss trial overall.

### Shijin13 — 2012-08-24T23:05:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-non-sleep.3236/post-46829>

> **Souldanzer;47200 wrote:** I s
>
> Is the only thing left that could be destroying my sleep that I am one of those "extremely sensitive" to light people and I need to turn my computer off at sunset? Or am I missing something?

I'm one of those people too... Jack suggested to me in my consult I should consider melatonin during the summer ... I'm doing 3mg right now...but maybe you need more? IDK, it seems like your circadian cycle is off... have you asked Jack in your consults if you might benefit from cycloset? I used to have crazy insomnia - and worked night shifts for several yrs... I'm finally seeing some normalization in sleep and waking cycles with the use of the cycloset. you probably need more dopamine, considering you're trying to "re-train your brain"

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-24T23:10:17+0300

Nope, Dr K has not suggested Cycloset to me. I've been staying away from the melatonin b/c of the prolactin issue that was brought up in one of the blogs with melatonin. I've tried it and all it did was make me groggy the next day it appeared. I'm still low on neurotransmitters and I don't quite understand why my NT support program hasn't been upped after the last test, all they added was the l-dopa. ### Endless — 2012-08-25T07:15:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-non-sleep.3236/post-46967>

How about audiobooks as an alternative to the computer?

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-25T10:39:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-non-sleep.3236/post-46973>

> **Endless;47386 wrote:** How about audiobooks as an alternative to the computer?

I've tried the audio books after my TBI. The problem with those is that they are even harder than books for me I think.... b/c I check out and miss stuff without really being able to slow it down or go back frequently to re-read. With books, when my attention goes, I just stop reading or realize I didn't get that last paragraph. But the audio books... they just blabber away on me and I give up.

But technically, I think those are probably a great idea for the non-TBI affected.

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-25T10:41:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-non-sleep.3236/post-46974>

Note time of posting: I spent the day yesterday without artificial lights even during the day and had my F.lux on all day to block the blue light from my screen.

No sleep

Hate it. It's the weekend and I'd like to have some fun with some friends. Really. But at this rate, I'm going to be half asleep all day and waiting for it to be over. I guess I really need to lose the computer at night. But there's only so much day dreaming even I can do!!

### vkiernan — 2012-08-25T13:28:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-non-sleep.3236/post-46976>

Morning sunshine. You are suppose to be sleeping. I have to get up for our fiscal yr end inventory audit. Have you been still taking a lot of dhea? Just thinkin' out loud (yes, it always gets me in trouble) seems like if I go overboard my sleep is trashed. I wonder if there is an over limit and what it can do to you? If I get acne, I back off. I also know my labs were over the limit too though. You really seem to go in ups and downs. Maybe today for a project you could go thru this journal and chart when your sleep is good and when your sleep is trashed and see if anything correlates. If nothing else, maybe it will bore you into sleeping. Sometimes you are telling us you are sleeping 10 hrs or more and other times you can't sleep period. I realize it might just be that you aren't fixed enough yet too. Maybe go outside and play today and get some cold water and maybe you will sleep tonight. My personal experience is if I go out on the lake and play all day in and out of the water, I sleep great that night. Hoping to be doing that today after work. I hope you have a nice day despite this whole not sleeping issue. Give your brain a rest and quit worrying too. Go play!

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-25T13:41:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-non-sleep.3236/post-46977>

Hey V. Still no sleep  It's getting worse.

I do sleep in phases. Some nights a lot, some nights I have a lot of trouble. The trouble is more often than the lot. I was supposed to be sleeping better with the progesterone added but it only helped for a few days or so. I guess I can try skipping the DHEA today. Might be a good idea. But then I'm kinda due for a better nights sleep. Eventually I get so exhausted I sleep 11 hrs in one night.

This isn't the worst it's ever been. But bad enough. During AN there were times where I just plain wouldn't sleep for weeks/months at a time. They had to give me special consideration at school b/c of my insomnia.

I get what you're saying about getting outside and playing. Thing is, for me that doesn't help. Ski days/weekends used to be some of the worst sleep nights. Also when I get a big hike in or am otherwise really active. The best sleep I get when I haven't slept for a week... b/c I'm exhausted and my body just gives in at some point.

Tonight I have tried taking melatonin, using f.lux all day and no artificial light exposure at all, taking activated B6, listening to a webinar replay, boring myself to sleep with reading Hinz research papers.... I don't know  And lots of day dreaming. I figure if I'm awake I might as well manifest some things I'd like to see happen  But yeah, it's about 4 hrs later and still no sleep.

### nonchalant — 2012-08-25T16:39:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-non-sleep.3236/post-47003>

I think you need to wait a bit, if possible. When I was on the LR, my sleep settled down (eventually) beautifully. Then I added CT and yikes! My sleep was terrible! For a few weeks. Then it settled down, and it became even better than before. When you wake up during the night, don't think of it as a failure, just use the time to imagine hiking or viewing the sunset or whatever. Even if you are awake for the rest of the night. Stay in bed. Act like you're asleep.

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-25T20:06:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-non-sleep.3236/post-47071>

CT used to help my sleep... not anymore. My sleep is getting worse i would say right now. Staying in bed and acting as if I'm asleep only works for so long. Then I go nuts. Then I turn on my laptop. Bad, I know.

### Lyndra — 2012-08-25T20:44:28+0300

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-25T21:04:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-non-sleep.3236/post-47088>

There is this place in Crestone, CO that I checked out a few years ago when I was at my breaking point with AN. It's a buddhist retreat center in the mountains. No acces by vehicle you have to hike in. I did when I checked it out. It is for people who are seeking solitude to deepen their practice. They have tiny little cabins far spread out over a huge property so you won't run into other's who are retreating in silence. Very affordable. You can stay as long as you like and they make sure to replenish your food supply so you don't have to leave ever.

I do think there is a food problem b/c they probably won't allow epi-paleo. They are vegetarian. And I'm not one to secretly break these sacred places' rules. Too bad.

### Richard — 2012-08-25T21:43:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-non-sleep.3236/post-47100>

Souldanzer, I sometimes have the same problem-waking around 3 am. My NP recommended Calcium Malate. It seems to help. Also, some nights I drink Sleepytime Tea, the blend with Valerian Root. Occasionally I eat a small protein snack right at bedtime. For some people early morning waking can be caused by blood sugar dropping. There are some iPhone apps that I listen to if I can't get back to sleep. My current favorite is SleepStream 2. Lastly, I read somewhere to think about something SMALL that you are grateful for when you are lying there. The last time I couldn't go back to sleep I thought about my toothbrush, how important it is, how many bristles it had, good brushing technique.......ZZZzz

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-26T00:50:54+0300

I think you did find it . I hiked into the property when I went to see if I wanted to spend some considerable time there in solitude and silence. I went to the common house and the shrine room and the Stupa. It was wonderful. Very quiet. Great views. Very nice people nice to be around them. Lower your gaze kinda people. I think I could maybe reset my circadian cycles with a couple weeks there. I'd have to use the cheapo student option probably  or see if they will let me camp somewhere

### Stavity — 2012-08-26T01:15:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-non-sleep.3236/post-47146>

When my vitamin B's are up there, sleep is much better. I also associate internal organs with the time I wake, if too early. Liver is what I am dealing with and my wake up time, no matter what, is 4 am. I know when my liver is better sleep will be a breeze. I wake up, and need to stay up for about 45 min then fall back to sleep for about 3 hrs, but that doesn't cut it and is not optimal sleep for me.

Maybe try upping your B complex? Also, if I drink chamomile tea it might help but no more then 1 or it can have the opposite affect on me.

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-26T01:36:44+0300

>
>
>
> Maybe try upping your B complex? Also, if I drink chamomile tea it might help but no more then 1 or it can have the opposite affect on me.

I'm taking B-vitamins and now just started on activated B6 which I guess could make a difference. I think I might be attacking this from the wrong end. I'm trying to tell my body no to the adaptations it is making considering my environment (lights at night, noisy, etc).... I"m sure some people can live like that. My body has proven to me several times it does not thrive in this kind of environment. I don't know if there's anything I can do about this, right now, really. It's been a goal for years to change my environment.

But maybe I need to open up to new ideas about how it could look like.

### cjb — 2012-08-26T17:03:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-non-sleep.3236/post-47233>

I would try finding a guided meditation to use when you wake up and can't go back to sleep.

Another option would be to get a cd of ocean sounds or another sound that you find relaxing and play that when you wake up. Then you have to let your mind take you where it wants to go and don't stress about being awake and not able to sleep. Once you start thinking about not being able to sleep, I think it becomes a self fulfilling prophecy.

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-26T17:31:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-non-sleep.3236/post-47242>

Thanks, cjb. Tried the meditation and the nature sounds.... no go. What being awake during the night really does to me is it makes me afraid to go to sleep in the first place.... I stay up longer and longer b/c I'm afraid if I'm not tired enough I will wake up during the night and not be able to go back to sleep. Very. Very. Counterproductive. B/c usually I stay up using my laptop. Um.

BUT. Good News for a change  Slept last night. Not the best and deepest sleep ever but it was sleep anyways! Went out to see the sunset, came back, realized I had forgotten my blueblockers to navigate the very illuminated hallway, tried to get the keys into the door holding my eyes shut with the other hand (lol, I hope nobody saw me). Had a moment of \*aha\*.

Well, there's light shining under the door from the hallways. I knew that and I've covered that up before when forced to leave my bedroom door open for airconditioning purposes during the night. But I realized how little light it really takes to throw things off after listening to the webinar. That's really part of where my dissatisfaction with some of the world is coming from..... and why I'm longing for a more natural surroundings. It's just so hard to avoid some things if you happen to be "sensitive".

So covered up the door issue as best I could. I decided temperature was another issue and put the A/C on high for the night. Which then required a whole lot more of covering up light sources. See.... my bedroom is "safe". No light Period. But the rest of this place is a trap hole.

Next I doubled up my neurotransmitter support for the day at night. I figure, I don't got enough dopamine in my system yet I have a dopamine related sleep issue. Dopamine needs to come around first. Also took some melatonin.

Was in bed by 8:30pm, turned off the computer by 8:45pm, took a while to fall asleep, woke up a couple times during the night, and the next thing I know is the sun peeking through the little corner of my window that's left open for that purpose.

I woke with the sun this morning  Let's see if I can repeat this.

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-26T17:34:40+0300

### Zorica Vuletic — 2012-08-26T23:32:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-non-sleep.3236/post-47343>

Oops, nevermind...I figured it out. I just had to slide the little ball over to 'halogen'.

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-26T23:33:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-non-sleep.3236/post-47344>

> **Gladina;47783 wrote:** Btw, how do I set my f.lux to 24/7? I try putting the south pole...b/c it's 'winter' there now...doesn't compute. I don't see a setting to have it 'turned on for 24/7'.
>
>
>
> Thanks.

You click on the little symbol (top right if you have a mac, it looks like a little analog clock).

Choose "preferences"

There will be two little slide bars one for "daytime" and one for "nighttime". Slide the "Daytime" button all the way to the left until it says 2700, just like the nighttime one.

CLick "done" and you're good to go.... your F.lux will never turn off.

### Shijin13 — 2012-08-27T16:23:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-non-sleep.3236/post-47478>

> **Souldanzer;47784 wrote:** Shijin, how are you managing the extreme light sensitivity?

Since my last consult I've added melatonin back in to my night time routine 1hr before when I want to go to bed. Also, we've been pushing hard the no light at night - the only light we use are red head lamps for reading, as well as candles (the non flame time). orange glasses, as soon as it starts to get dark, limited computer time after 6pm (I do admit I do check FB in the evenings - but I put it to bed NLT 9pm but I need to get better w/that) Any reading is done w/my kindle touch or a regular book using the red head lamp... next week going to start studying for my professional exam in the evenings again... using the same stuff.

I also plan on buying orange bulbs (blue light blocking ones) to put into our lamps for this winter when It gets dark (DH hasn't fully committed to no lights at night - but I'm working on him.) This past week - we've started eating by candle light - working slowly towards preparing every on for the winter....

Add the cycloset to it... & the RX of orgasm before bed, and after sunrise.... things seem to be getting on track ;D

### Glamazon — 2012-08-30T07:10:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-non-sleep.3236/post-48308>

> **Souldanzer;47759 wrote:** that must be new... don't remember that being there when I went to check it out

I stayed there a few years ago for a long weekend. You can drive up to it. Their food is organic; they grow much of their own. They are totally veggie. Before I knew how sensitive I really was to carbs I would pass out for an hour after each meal because it was so carb heavy. Very tasty though. I would bring cans of salmon and some pastured eggs (eat raw). They do have eggs and dairy for meals. It's probably about 10 minutes to drive back into Crestone which does have just a few places to eat. It is very quiet there at night. Just hear the animals roaming thru. They do go silent retreat for the winter. I went up to visit first in the spring when the monks were coming off a silent retreat. The monks were a bit pale, gaunt, and acting a little bit fried and strung out it seemed post silent time.

It is a beautiful, serene place. The dorms are nice too. The cabins are tiny. They also have a TV in the main lodge where the meals are served.
