# Thread: candida
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/candida.3301/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jonty — 2012-08-28T00:11:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/candida.3301/post-47563>

What would Dr Kruse's philosophy regarding the treatment of candida be?

### Patty Cakes — 2012-08-28T00:20:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/candida.3301/post-47566>

Get rid of it by all means possible! I believe he used words like that on more than one occasion! But you may want to make sure you have candida (maybe you already do know). Metametrix GI fx test is a good one to use.

### Jonty — 2012-08-28T01:10:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/candida.3301/post-47571>

Is that even possible? I dont know the answer, perhaps reduce influence, but for individuals who were given lots of antibiotics during childhood, getting rid of it seems unlikely.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-08-28T01:20:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/candida.3301/post-47575>

so why did you ask then?

### Jonty — 2012-08-28T02:45:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/candida.3301/post-47642>

Just trying to get my head around this candida thing.

It seems forums everywhere are full of folk trying to be rid of candida. Then there's every elimination therapy under the sun each promising to be 'the answer'.

Personally, I have tried many alternatives and have explored many philosophies regarding candida but I have not found definitive success.

Dr. Kruse, I was hoping to hear your candida philosophy, as it is always good to get different perspectives on issues.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-08-28T03:38:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/candida.3301/post-47661>

small bowel fumigation with diflucan and then re establish the flora.....6-8 months of work It is doable but takes a boat load of time for doc and patients

### kathylu — 2012-08-28T03:49:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/candida.3301/post-47666>

I found that 3 months of 200 mg diflucan daily seems to have fixed the problem. Strict low carb eating, coconut oil, oil of oregano and lots of garlic help too.

### IceBarbie — 2012-08-28T08:23:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/candida.3301/post-47732>

Colloidal Silver works for me. I've been using the Silver Biotics brand from iherb.com lately. 16 oz costs about $26. I use about 3 oz in the morning and again at night. I've tried lots of Diflucan in the past and didn't get as good as results as I did using CS. I also take 50 Billion Probiotics from Now brand. I've backed off that too lately since my symptoms are currently gone (1 capsule every other day or so). And I've had it bad too in the past--raw sores on my body and chronic athletes foot. I haven't had those symptoms in a few years now. Every 6 months or so I use CS again if I start to notice any discomfort from the Candida--usually after a sugar-binge (yeah, I know!). The CS works well. Allow a few weeks (or 1 16 oz bottle of CS) for a significant improvement. Trust me, I've spent THOUSANDS on my Candida in the last 35 years. CS is pretty cheap compared to all the other things that didn't work for me.

### stephaniesherie — 2012-08-28T11:58:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/candida.3301/post-47738>

IceBarbie - what symptoms do you get after a sugar-binge?

### vkiernan — 2012-08-28T14:39:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/candida.3301/post-47752>

> **IceBarbie;48183 wrote:** Colloidal Silver works for me. I've been using the Silver Biotics brand from iherb.com lately. 16 oz costs about $26. I use about 3 oz in the morning and again at night. I've tried lots of Diflucan in the past and didn't get as good as results as I did using CS. I also take 50 Billion Probiotics from Now brand. **Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/candida.3301/post-47800>

> **vkiernan;48203 wrote:** You are saying that 1 bottle gets it back in check? I still am not sure if I have it or not. My blood work back in March said I did and again a couple months later. I did a GI Effects test in July and it said none found but I had worked hard at getting rid of it. What would you make of it?

I would say you are clear but need to watch it always to keep it from coming back. The Metametrix test is very sensitive. I'm still working on mine!

### vkiernan — 2012-08-28T20:02:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/candida.3301/post-47813>

> **Patty Cakes;48251 wrote:** I would say you are clear but need to watch it always to keep it from coming back. The Metametrix test is very sensitive. I'm still working on mine!

Thanks Patty. I have swallowed so much to get rid of it. Sure hope it is gone. I am curious what symptoms I would get to know if it came back since I didn't know I had it in the first place. Any idea? The only one I know of would be sugar cravings.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-08-29T01:58:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/candida.3301/post-47983>

> **vkiernan;48264 wrote:** Thanks Patty. I have swallowed so much to get rid of it. Sure hope it is gone. I am curious what symptoms I would get to know if it came back since I didn't know I had it in the first place. Any idea? The only one I know of would be sugar cravings.

That's one and some people get itching, sores, or thrush in the mouth. I'm sure there is a host of others but I don't know of them.

# Thread: Ketosis musings
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-musings.3303/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TribalSpice — 2012-08-28T00:45:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-musings.3303/post-47568>

Please be gentle, I am only new here.

I have been completely absorbed in reading the blog (and blowing my mind at the same time). For the past 7 years I have been supporting women in birth and about 2 years into it I thought I was starting to become psychic, alas I had no super power but realised that I could smell on a woman's breath when she hit that 8-9cm mark. Not only that, a lack of change in smell always marked either an obstructed labour or some other sort of pathology (ie sick baby). What I have noticed over the years is that no matter how long a labour is, be it 3 hours or 3 days, mum kicks into ketosis mode before pushing starts. This doesn't happen though if she has an induction or pain relief.

I am wondering if this is some sort of protective mechanism for bub when there is lower oxygen levels to the brain during 2nd stage. The more I am reading here, the more this change in energy systems seems significant.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-08-28T01:31:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-musings.3303/post-47584>

I buy this.......oxytocin is a phermone and it has amazing absorption on the nasal mucosa. I think I am quite sensitive to oxytocin as well for a variety of reasons.

### TribalSpice — 2012-08-28T02:38:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-musings.3303/post-47639>

Oh yes, oxytocin is an incredibly powerful pheromone. If you want to know what love feels like, sit quietly in a room as a woman births a baby unhindered. It is intoxicating. I believe a lot of the worlds problems could be solved with a big dose of oxytocin. More altruism, more empathy, deeper bonding and connection, increased levels of trust just to name a few.

### nonchalant — 2012-08-28T03:51:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-musings.3303/post-47668>

Hi TribalSpice, glad to have you here. So you can smell acetone-like breath when the mother is ready to push? Perhaps the ketosis kicks off some big hormone changes.

### TribalSpice — 2012-08-28T04:20:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-musings.3303/post-47681>

Thanks for the welcome. Yes, that is the smell.

Ah and here is the question. Does ketosis kick off the hormonal changes or do hormonal changes kick in ketosis? There are a lot of hormonal changes that occur at that transition time of labour, adrenalin increases, pulling mum slightly out of here beta-endorphin induced off her face away in labour land state and helping facilitate fetal ejection reflex. Prolactin increases allowing bub to cope better with hypoxia as well as putting mum into mother bear mode. Up goes the oxytocin...

What I didnt understand was why ketosis kicked in. It is pathologised in birth because it could also be a symptom of pre eclampsia, but reading about myelinisation it is sort of starting to make sense that this is the healthy response for the major hormonal and. Neurological changes just about to occur.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-08-28T15:51:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-musings.3303/post-47762>

ketosis is needed for the babies myelination......children are ketotic for quite sometime when they are on the tit......again something else that the paleo world needs to learn. Ketosis is critical for human myelination.

### Shijin13 — 2012-08-28T15:55:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-musings.3303/post-47763>

This is amazing.... that you've figure this out... going to share w/my MW/Birth Doulas etc.....

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-28T20:45:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-musings.3303/post-47848>

> **TribalSpice;48090 wrote:** Oh yes, oxytocin is an incredibly powerful pheromone. If you want to know what love feels like, sit quietly in a room as a woman births a baby unhindered. It is intoxicating. I believe a lot of the worlds problems could be solved with a big dose of oxytocin. More altruism, more empathy, deeper bonding and connection, increased levels of trust just to name a few.

Think babies have more of that oxytocin thing lingering right after birth? You know, that baby smell that is so endearing?

### nonchalant — 2012-08-28T21:12:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-musings.3303/post-47862>

> **TribalSpice;48132 wrote:** There are a lot of hormonal changes that occur at that transition time of labour, adrenalin increases, pulling mum slightly out of here beta-endorphin induced off her face away in labour land state and helping facilitate fetal ejection reflex. Prolactin increases allowing bub to cope better with hypoxia as well as putting mum into mother bear mode. Up goes the oxytocin...

Interesting. And here I just recall that it REALLY HURT at about this time...

### bigknitwit — 2012-08-28T23:53:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-musings.3303/post-47925>

Nothing to add here, except to say I enjoyed reading your observations. I have 4 children, and the last 3 were all born at home without any interventions. What a rush of all the senses, but it sure didn't get any easier lol!

### nonchalant — 2012-08-29T00:13:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-musings.3303/post-47933>

> **bigknitwit;48376 wrote:** Nothing to add here, except to say I enjoyed reading your observations. I have 4 children, and the last 3 were all born at home without any interventions. What a rush of all the senses, but it sure didn't get any easier lol!

Awesome, bigknitwit!

### TribalSpice — 2012-08-29T04:51:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-musings.3303/post-48022>

> **Jack Kruse;48213 wrote: ketosis is needed for the babies myelination......children are ketotic for quite sometime when they are on the tit......again something else that the paleo world needs to learn. Ketosis is critical for human myelination.
>
>
> Yes, I have just been reading this and it is making so much sense. I have always instinctually cringed at the idea of packing a lunch box to take your kids out anywhere. You see it all the time, rice crackers, sultanas, chopped up fruit... All for what might be an hour maybe 2 out of the house. It is like training you kids to snack and whine for food. I have always had an urge to say something about it, but never had anything to back it up. Thank you, now I do and it is vitally important.
>
>
>
>
> > **Nonchalant;48313 wrote:** Interesting. And here I just recall that it REALLY HURT at about this time...
>
>
>
> Yeah, I never got one of those orgasmic births, but hey it was really an awakening moving through that intensity.

Nice to see another homebirther here.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-08-29T06:04:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-musings.3303/post-48030>

> **TribalSpice;48473 wrote:** Yes, I have just been reading this and it is making so much sense. I have always instinctually cringed at the idea of packing a lunch box to take your kids out anywhere. You see it all the time, rice crackers, sultanas, chopped up fruit... All for what might be an hour maybe 2 out of the house. It is like training you kids to snack and whine for food. I have always had an urge to say something about it, but never had anything to back it up. Thank you, now I do and it is vitally important.

>

>

> Nice to see another homebirther here.

Another homebirther. I agree, it HURT. A lot. But recovery was a breeze. And my older child (then 3) was present... she and daddy cut the cord and were in the birth pool with me!

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-29T06:49:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-musings.3303/post-48032>

> **KiwiLauren;48481 wrote:** Another homebirther. I agree, it HURT. A lot. But recovery was a breeze. And my older child (then 3) was present... she and daddy cut the cord and were in the birth pool with me!

I wish I could change the way mine went - didnt even go into labor as my doc only did surgery on mondays. I didnt make milk either.

I like looking on the bright side - that epidural was so totally rockin! Best high ever.

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-28T01:43:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-are-flameless-candles-ok-or-are-they.3305/post-47594>

I've seen posts about buying flameless candles, but I don't understand how they could be ok to use since they still use artificial light--and often LEDs, which I think are worse than other bulbs. Does wearing the amber glasses while using them negate any bad effects?

I should've asked before I bought a set of 7 from Costco.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-are-flameless-candles-ok-or-are-they.3305/post-47624>

# Thread: Modern Living
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/modern-living.3307/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AKMan — 2012-08-28T03:02:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/modern-living.3307/post-47650>

Hey, guys; Back from my moose hunt--have a freezer full of moose meat, organs and bones to get me through the winter. Just want to share this Facebook post that was on my wife's wall. Does this exemplifiy everything that's wrong with modern humans or what? From a 40 year old woman:

"feeling a bit battered this morning. I hate to admit it but this age thing kicks my butt sometimes, a couple of flexiril's later and a few more hours of sleep, feeling almost like new... ok maybe not new but better. Hubby did wake up and went to this new bread place (Great Harvest Bread Co.) OMG amazing bread!!!"

first comment: "I'm going to have to try their bread. A friend once told me that getting old isn't for sissies and boy was she right. I hate it!!"

### Jack Kruse — 2012-08-28T03:22:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/modern-living.3307/post-47655>

and I bet they went at night and sat in fake light..........she better get more flexeril.......and reservations for a knee and hip replacement.

### Lyndra — 2012-08-28T18:31:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/modern-living.3307/post-47794>

I used to love GHB, especially the 5-grain wheat. That was for lunch; for breakfast I'd have Kashi Go Lean. Gotta get in those 6-11 servings of whole grain every day, don't ya know.

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-28T18:49:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/modern-living.3307/post-47801>

Recovering Kashi Go Lean addict here, too  And balance bars omg. I still crave those once in a while. Wonder what they put in there.

### fitness@home — 2012-08-28T19:30:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/modern-living.3307/post-47804>

I think it's sad that a 40-year old woman feels "old". This may only be the halfway point in her life! What will she feel like if she makes it to 80?

Kasha Go Lean Crunch was one of my favorite snacking foods in the past. Haven't had any in about three years now...

### Endless — 2012-08-28T19:49:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/modern-living.3307/post-47811>

### AKMan — 2012-08-29T00:21:23+0300

### Zorica Vuletic — 2012-08-29T19:13:01+0300

...yikes! Sounds like she's headed towards cancer then...:-(

### Zorica Vuletic — 2012-08-29T19:16:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/modern-living.3307/post-48113>

Of course my mother had to sneeringly say: "See...proves that you never know and YOU could get cancer no matter what ...you think eating healthy will 'save' you...What a \*\*\*\*ty mentality...

But in general to counteract that argument though...it would be: OK, so if I DID get cancer (but don't think I will at this rate) and I did everything 'right'...well then I wouldn't be able to blamed then right? At least I would not be to blame...

I know I'm so prone to any and all of these health problems due to my hormonal status...SO...I don't cry over it. I fix it.

...Got a craving for seaweed and sardines right about now...

# Thread: OK to get a little of the bad stuff if you are getting a lot of good stuff?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ok-to-get-a-little-of-the-bad-stuff-if-you-are-getting-a-lot-of-good-stuff.3308/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-08-28T03:28:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ok-to-get-a-little-of-the-bad-stuff-if-you-are-getting-a-lot-of-good-stuff.3308/post-47656>

I'm trying out a new medical clinic that has me really excited. They have a lot of MD's & a couple of naturopaths. The MDs and labs are covered by my insurance. While waiting on the results of my comprehensive stool test, the naturopath has set me up with a supplement plan to help heal my gut & also to support my adrenals. It includes upping my L-Glutamine to 5g/day, switching to one of their probiotic supplements that include Immunoglobulins, using a cream for adrenal support & taking 2 scoops of their Foundation Gut Enhancement powder. The Gut Enhancement powder has a lot of good stuff that I need, but also a little "bad" stuff, namely Sunflower Oil, pea protein isolate, Rice Protein concentrate, Carageenan, evaporated cane juice & Brown Rice Syrup solids. A total of 16g carbs. I haven't decided if it is something I want to take, but I also don't want to throw out the baby with the bath water.

Any thoughts?

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-28T03:37:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ok-to-get-a-little-of-the-bad-stuff-if-you-are-getting-a-lot-of-good-stuff.3308/post-47660>

I wouldn't but that's just me.

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-28T21:19:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ok-to-get-a-little-of-the-bad-stuff-if-you-are-getting-a-lot-of-good-stuff.3308/post-47867>

Poison is in the dose?

If it were me, I might point out to them you are "uncomfortable" with all of those other ingredients and even ask if there is something you could do instead to get the good stuff without the bad.

Is it the kind of thing where that's a profit item for the clinic? If that is the case, they won't be open to discussions. . just pass.

### ealachan — 2012-08-28T21:31:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ok-to-get-a-little-of-the-bad-stuff-if-you-are-getting-a-lot-of-good-stuff.3308/post-47873>

> **kjgraffin;48107 wrote:** I'm trying out a new medical clinic that has me really excited. They have a lot of MD's & a couple of naturopaths. The MDs and labs are covered by my insurance. While waiting on the results of my comprehensive stool test, the naturopath has set me up with a supplement plan to help heal my gut & also to support my adrenals. It includes upping my L-Glutamine to 5g/day, switching to one of their probiotic supplements that include Immunoglobulins, using a cream for adrenal support & taking 2 scoops of their Foundation Gut Enhancement powder. The Gut Enhancement powder has a lot of good stuff that I need, but also a little "bad" stuff, namely Sunflower Oil, pea protein isolate, Rice Protein concentrate, Carageenan, evaporated cane juice & Brown Rice Syrup solids. A total of 16g carbs. I haven't decided if it is something I want to take, but I also don't want to throw out the baby with the bath water.
>
>
>
> Any thoughts?

It sounds to me like they're trying to get you to pay for and take stuff that makes them money. If that's the case, I'd pass.

### Shijin13 — 2012-08-28T21:41:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ok-to-get-a-little-of-the-bad-stuff-if-you-are-getting-a-lot-of-good-stuff.3308/post-47879>

> **ealachan;48324 wrote:** It sounds to me like they're trying to get you to pay for and take stuff that makes them money. If that's the case, I'd pass.

THIS.... just say no. my chrio tries to get me to buy stuff I don't need... I tell him I've got all the sups I need.... DH on the other hand...is buying the stuff chiro is selling him... but he's got to figure out what he needs/doesn't need...

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-29T02:48:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ok-to-get-a-little-of-the-bad-stuff-if-you-are-getting-a-lot-of-good-stuff.3308/post-47996>

So I found a powder on Life Extension's website that has almost the exact same nutrients, but not any of the things I wanted to avoid in the powder I got from my doctor's office. There is a bit of a trade-off as the Life Extension product has maltodextrin derived from corn.

Acceptable alternative? (The clinic will take back the other powder since it is not opened.)

# Thread: DIY fecal transplant
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-fecal-transplant.3309/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 120
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bigknitwit — 2012-08-28T03:34:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-fecal-transplant.3309/post-47659>

Am I the only one who's thinking about this?! It doesn't seem like it would be that difficult a procedure to do (haven't exactly researched it yet though). I think the biggest thing would be choosing a donor. Well, I could use one of my children. It certainly wouldn't be perfect, but it end up better than where I'm at right now... Besides, it's seems less disgusting to deal with the poop of one of my kids, given all the years I've spent cleaning their rear ends already lol! Seriously though, I'm going to have to look into it a little more. I'm wondering if antibiotics are used beforehand though, to "clear some space." And then, I'm not sure if I'll just be inheriting the problems and challenges coming their way, as they mature and their hormones change. None of the 4 are perfect donors... 2 have autoimmune issues, one had digestive issues at a young age (constipation). My son (with celiac and hashimoto's) has pretty good body comp though, has eaten pretty limited grains instinctively for several years (before I knew about all this stuff) and really decent quantities of fish (sardines and salmon) for several years. Not to mention his dentition is excellent, and the orthodontist says so far it looks like no braces will be needed (he's 9)... Otherwise, there's my daughter (6), with no autoimmune issues (so far), but not nearly the fish lover that her brother is....

### kathylu — 2012-08-28T03:58:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-fecal-transplant.3309/post-47670>

Here's a link to a thread discussing a paper from Journal of Gastroenterology on DIY fecal transplants. The procedure is described in the paper. Seems very simple. You need an enema kit, a blender (I know...yuck!) and some coffee filters. And good poop, of course. Let us know if you try it!

[http://forums.phoenixrising.me/index.php?threads/fecal-transplants-fecal-bacteriotherapy-diy-home-version-very-straightforward.13744/](http://http://forums.phoenixrising.me/index.php?threads/fecal-transplants-fecal-bacteriotherapy-diy-home-version-very-straightforward.13744/)

### Patty Cakes — 2012-08-28T05:51:36+0300

Personally I like the idea of a fecal transplant because it fixes so much so fast. I watched the video posted on the forum a short while ago and found it fascinating. Of course I suppose after the transplant you would have to make sure your lifestyle was dramatically different so you didn't return to the same ole gut issues!

### Kiera2 — 2012-08-28T06:44:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-fecal-transplant.3309/post-47723>

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-28T06:54:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-fecal-transplant.3309/post-47726>

lol..... not really with ya' all with the fascination about stranger's poo. I did recently have an applicant for my own though, hehe. I think it was the calorie intake to weight gain ratio that was attractive, not necessarily my pitiful state of hormones  Gotta have your priorities right. Ok, it wasn't really serious....

The weird thing is that I always get people asking me if I can "give them a little bit of anorexia". I used to get real angry at that. But I guess it's possible after all?

### bigknitwit — 2012-08-28T14:50:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-fecal-transplant.3309/post-47756>

For some reason, it doesn't creep me out too much to think about doing it (probably because my youngest has only been a year or so out of diapers)... Those areas are designed to deal with all kinds of bacteria pretty well. In fact, I remember watching "Survivor Man," a few years back, and when he didn't have access to any clean water, injecting the unclean water up there was a way to get some fluid absorption without worrying about the bacteria too much. Can't vouch for how true this is. Also, my guess is that ideally you want to use someone from your own household, rather than starting with a foreign bacterial ecosystem. But then there's the trade-off between using an "imperfect" household member, or a "more optimal" foreigner (worse ick factor here too!)... Back to that native bacterial species thing... Hmm, so maybe we should be using our parents feces for transplant..?! Mine still live in the house we moved to at age 14, 1 mile from the house of my early childhood.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-08-28T15:05:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-fecal-transplant.3309/post-47757>

Soon this will be the gold standard for gut flora issues I predict.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-fecal-transplant.3309/post-47767>

### ealachan — 2012-08-28T16:55:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-fecal-transplant.3309/post-47773>

Wouldn't your body, if suddenly inundated with a whole bunch of new bacteria, have some kind of reaction? Is there a risk of the "transplant" being rejected? Also, I'm totally curious as to how quick the results would be. Would it literally be a case of "insert poo, start losing weight", or would there be a period of time where the new bacteria would need to build up in your system, etc? And even then, would the new, better bacteria just make it easier to lose weight with proper diet and exercise, or would it really be a matter of your body starting to shed weight even without exercise?

Part of me thinks "yeah sure, why the hell not, it's not like you have to EAT the poop"...but another part of me thinks it all sounds way too easy and there must be some kind of catch.

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-28T17:08:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-fecal-transplant.3309/post-47778>

I find it creepy. I mean, based on what we're learning about gut floras' impact on health, well-being, and also the brain..... I wouldn't want somebody else's to take over. What is it really going to affect all.... my thinking???

I understand for people who are in dire need of it.... somehow I don't see myself in that place. A few years ago, maybe I would have. Actually, I'm pretty sure I would have.

### nonchalant — 2012-08-28T17:25:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-fecal-transplant.3309/post-47781>

you might not realize this but your insides of your gut ate a continuation of the outside of your body.......that is why the immune system is in the intestinal wall. It is akin to getting bacteria on you skin.......if your immune system is good you wont have any reaction......if its not then maybe

### Jack Kruse — 2012-08-28T18:03:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-fecal-transplant.3309/post-47785>

eating a SAD is pretty drastic from an evolutionary context.......and we do it everyday to destroy a gut flora.......? This is not that drastic......it is just new. And with newness comes fear.

### bigknitwit — 2012-08-28T18:10:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-fecal-transplant.3309/post-47788>

> **Jack Kruse;48235 wrote:** you might not realize this but your insides of your gut ate a continuation of the outside of your body.......that is why the immune system is in the intestinal wall. It is akin to getting bacteria on you skin.......if your immune system is good you wont have any reaction......if its not then maybe

This is also why I think it's "safer" to go with someone in your household, optimal or not. You are already exposed to their fecal bacteria all the time (especially with kids in the house), so it wouldn't be that foreign, I would think. Once I deal with my new high iron status, improve my am cortisol (hopefully cycloset here), and improve my T3 status (not tolerated well currently), and improve my vitamin D status, as well as DHEA, then, if things are still not dramatically improved, I may have to have one of my kids do a "poop catch" for me... I got really deep into this mess doing a drastic elimination diet to help my (nursing) toddler, now it's my kids turn to help get me out of this!

### ealachan — 2012-08-28T18:19:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-fecal-transplant.3309/post-47790>

Also, if one DID have a reaction...does that negate any of the good effects the transplant might have?

I don't think I'm entirely in the "this is creepy" camp...I'm just curious and still a little skeptical...

### Inger — 2012-08-28T18:32:29+0300

### vkiernan — 2012-08-28T20:09:39+0300

If you look at it with remembering that poo starts with food; it just ends up a lot smellier along with a different texture but our body is what made it that way.

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-28T20:10:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-fecal-transplant.3309/post-47819>

I think the hardest part about doing this on a practical basis, is figuring out what poo to look for, and finding a donor. . seems like if you are thinking of this to optimize, vs get rid of a specific infection, that you would want to test the donor to see if they had some awesome bacteria to provide you that you were missing. You could easily give yourself lots of bad thing (law of un intended consequences) like candida or something else your body might not be prepared for, right?

With biggies like CDiff, it makes perfect sense. . .but when fine tuning "optimal" it's a lot more complex as far as what poo would be of benefit.

### Zorica Vuletic — 2012-08-29T00:34:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-fecal-transplant.3309/post-47937>

I am a huge fan of fecal transplant. It seems like a good idea.

Here's why:

Well, a lot of ppl trying to reverse the diseases they have...and sometimes the method is AWESOME, but sometimes it's like 'pissing in a lake that's rising'. Know what I mean? This would be like a super booster charger.

In regards to the donor....I don't know if their diet is THAT important...b/c if they can eat \*\*\*\* and STILL not have inflammation (which is 'any' disease) and look amazing for it too...wouldn't that clearly point to them having a SUPERIOR gut flora? Makes you wonder.

Then once transplant is finished you can resume with epi-paleo....since these foods simply taste better anyway. lol (And for future immunity building).

So back up to what I mean about 'pissing in a lake' (to which Dr. K has said to me before...:S). So you do epi-paleo...or you take BHRT or whatever many interventions described on here....but ONE little TINY 'error'...like if you have to frigging have a damn cupcake for social reasons like ONE time...and that ONE little error can totally set you back when you're still in the healing process...how is that even fair? Like for crocheting...you can pick the one thread and unravel it within seconds...but it took maybe hours to make... Makes you wonder....

\*But on the other hand...I think with 'crocheting' you can use that to your advantage too...go back to the 'error' which is easy to unravel, and start over again.

Now, saving one's poop right before they know they have to take abx for whatever test seems like a super idea.

Also, if I ever encountered something like c. diff or some infection from over seas...yes I would absolutely use fecal transplant NO MATTER WHAT and skip the anti-biotic stage completely...or if in even worse scenario and had no choice with the anti-biotics...then I'd do the transplant as soon after that as well.

For the issue of possible 'changes in behaviour', I think that would be true, but is it so 'outrageous' of a concept as some portray? For example...if someone with candida cannot stop eating sugar and processed stuff...b/c the 'bacteria' is telling them to, then having healthy gut flora would then change their behaviour so that they don't NEED to eat that crap anyway....and their tastes would change. That is a change in behaviour and there is nothing mystical or 'scary' about that. I don't think it would change at some freakish rate like with the Phinneas Gage dude who got implaed in his frontal cortex and changed personality for the worse.

Long rant over. Hope I don't come across as annoying. Sorry if I do. Thanks for reading.

I think you prepare the same way you do for a colonoscopy, by drinking a gallon of PEG (polyethylene glycol). That clears out the contents of your GI tract, takes the biofilms out too. Then you "re-seed" with the good stuff.

### [email redacted] — 2012-09-19T22:37:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-fecal-transplant.3309/post-52759>

How do you guys know when you can't "tolerate" certain things? Are there obvious symptoms? I keep hearing that I might be intolerant of X or X, but my poops are find and I don't have any negative processing problems so I'm curious how did you know you could only eat one veggie etc.

### bigknitwit — 2012-09-19T22:51:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-fecal-transplant.3309/post-52760>

> **Cavemam;53387 wrote:** How do you guys know when you can't "tolerate" certain things? Are there obvious symptoms? I keep hearing that I might be intolerant of X or X, but my poops are find and I don't have any negative processing problems so I'm curious how did you know you could only eat one veggie etc.

Usually my only symptom is dramatic weight gain (over the course of 24-48 hours from ingestion) anywhere from 1.5 to 3.5 pounds. Usually the max is at 24 hours after eating. It holds until 48, and then starts to fall over the next 2 days or so. A 4 day event. If I eat another offending food, it just adds to the total (another 1-3ish pounds). I gained 15 pounds over 3 weeks once before I panicked and started low carb eating. I gained 8 pounds over 6 days over last Christmas - New years. I know Jack says the scale is not part of the leptin reset, but for me, it's critical. To figure this all out, I had to drop down to turkey only for 2 days, and then add in slowly from there (had I known about JK back then, I would have picked fish!). HTH... I literally have no other symptoms. I've had all kinds of allergy testing done (skin prick, serum, intra-dermal). Nothing tells me anything reliable compared to full elimination...

### [email redacted] — 2012-09-25T02:30:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-fecal-transplant.3309/post-53944>

Illuminating discussion of gut microbiome by U.K. Dr. Myhill.

[http://www.drmyhill.co.uk/wiki/Fermentation\_in\_the\_gut\_and\_CFS](http://http://www.drmyhill.co.uk/wiki/Fermentation_in_the_gut_and_CFS)

The relevance to this thread is that bacteriodes are supposed to be the predominate colon bugs. They are anaerobes and largely responsible for fermenting cellulose and resistant starches into SCFA that are critical for colon health but also used for fuel body-wide. Being anaerobes they only survive a few minutes outside the body, and cannot be supplemented like acidophilus, bifidus, etc. If your personal colony of bacteroides gets wiped out by antibiotics, or is starved because prevotella (bacteroides in places they're not supposed to be) in the small intestine is fermenting cellulose into SCFA before it reaches the colon, the ONLY way to replenish is a fecal implant using stool from a healthy donor.

Comments on various blogs dealing with FI seem to indicate that stool from small children is not as effective as that from an adult. This somewhat off-putting procedure is likely to become the preferred treatment for ulcerative colitus, crohns, etc. A google search will return lots of links.

### sjoshua — 2013-08-26T19:31:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-fecal-transplant.3309/post-93826>

This was an entertaining discussion. I love the thinking outside the box attitude that this forum spurs. Having a window into so many minds that think in a similar fashion as myself is a very interesting opportunity that larger communities typically do not support. The gloves off, no holds barred, openness and honesty in these discussions is inspiring, and in actuality I believe it is also these same traits that spur innovation. I've never been "broken" as most would define it here, and have been moving exponentially closer to optimal over the past 2-3 years. It is incredible to me that no matter how much I do and how awesome it makes me feel, a week later I will have surpassed that and achieved even better. I know you are all on the same path no matter how far along your individual journey you have come thus far. If not sooner, I hope to re-group with all of you at the next ice age.

I witness what broken is first hand with my girlfriend.. I don't think she could fathom actually doing this, but if anyone is in the Bay area I'm always down for experimenting if someone wants to borrow some poo (...errr, you can keep it... also, no return policy please and thanks)!

# Thread: luxury CT opportunity in Carmel.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/luxury-ct-opportunity-in-carmel.3317/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis

### janagram — 2012-08-28T20:24:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/luxury-ct-opportunity-in-carmel.3317/post-47829>

At my recent retreat at Asilomar, a friend invited me to bop over to "The Refuge" in Carmel Valley for an afternoon of tub hopping! There among the /boulders and palms were a multitude of smallish pools of varying temps. The cold was verry cold and I could only walk thru it up to my bra-line...I stayed in it for a couple minutes at most. But it was kind of awesome...when you get out you feel warm...(cuz anything's warmer than that)

I confess I am a superwimp but I thought of you guys and how fun it would be for you. Then there's a huge dry sauna, and all the rest.

[http://www.refuge.com/the\_refuge\_experience\_two.html](http://http://www.refuge.com/the_refuge_experience_two.html)

# Thread: Weaned toddler needs + seasonality of macros
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weaned-toddler-needs-seasonality-of-macros.3318/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JoeBranca — 2012-08-28T21:44:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weaned-toddler-needs-seasonality-of-macros.3318/post-47880>

We have a 19 month old toddler (mama stopped producing enough milk/toddler lost interest around 15-16 months) and for solid foods she's doing pretty good but I'm still not clear how much we should be watching what she gets as far as protein/fats/carbs. According to this recent post, it should mimic healthy breastmilk ratios:

[http://www.thepaleomom.com/2012/08/carbohydrate-recommendations-for-kids.html](http://http://www.thepaleomom.com/2012/08/carbohydrate-recommendations-for-kids.html)

And I wonder if that's sound advice.

As far as carbs, she gets raw pastured goat milk daily, but also lots and lots of fruit in addition to sweet potato at most meals. My confusion is if this is a good thing or suboptimal for toddler development, as it replaces what could be more caloric intake of brain developing fats, and perhaps in the long run encourages a preference for the sweets over other things if presented as choices.

Should I not worry about fruit intake for the rest of summer and just have mom titrate down the fruit into the fall and winter months, so that the majority of toddler's carbs are from the goat milk and veggies? Are sweet potatoes a good year round thing or should that be seasonal as well?

Well, to make the question simpler, for a young toddler do you tweak the seasonality principle according to context of their particular developmental needs? Should I instead forget seasonal macro ratios altogether and be thinking purely in terms of micro nutritional intake?

Am I overthinking this?

### Shijin13 — 2012-08-28T22:15:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weaned-toddler-needs-seasonality-of-macros.3318/post-47893>

Here's what I do. I have a 2yo (still nursing 1x/day) & 4yo.

Breakfast always includes protein & FAT same for lunch & dinner

This summer we've done a lot of talking about seasonal fruits w/our 4yo. We no longer buy fruit out of season

Fruit is our desert and we only have it w/meals. Dd is almost able to handle 3 meals/day but not always so when she's hungry we offer protein/FAT combo

Ds who's 2 eats on instinct. Every am while making bkfst he asks for plane ghee before his meal. So I give it to him some days he'll eat 3-4 TBS of ghee. He's still eating more on a nursing schedule of an older toddler every 3-4 hrs, even though he's not nursing throughout the day

Dd in the winter naturally shuns veggies/carbs i no longer fight her on that. If all she wants is protein/fat that's what she eats. She knows this intuitively

With both kids we've really been pushing the epipaleo and tgey've embraced it. They love their shrimp, mussels, oysters and squid (too ds a couple of wks to get there but he now asks if he can have squid).

Give them unlimited access to the good stuff and that's what they'll ask for. Your dd will follow your lead what you eat she'll eat

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weaned-toddler-needs-seasonality-of-macros.3318/post-47897>

Playdate? Arranged marriage?

### Shijin13 — 2012-08-28T22:58:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weaned-toddler-needs-seasonality-of-macros.3318/post-47911>

> **Cavemam;48348 wrote:** Playdate? Arranged marriage?

LOL.... I think the epi-paleo kids will find their own kind.... w/o our interference...

### kathylu — 2012-08-29T06:39:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weaned-toddler-needs-seasonality-of-macros.3318/post-48031>

I still struggle with this. My 5 year old eats well, but not as cleanly as I would like. We often have to bribe him with fruit..."if you eat your shrimp and steak, then you can have grapes", etc. I have gotten very tough about introducing foods though. He automatically rejects anything he hasn't seen before. So, I just state "this is dinner. If you don't want to eat, fine but nothing else tonight. And dinner will make a great breakfast tomorrow! We'll save it for you." He is slowly coming around. A big part of the problem is that we sent him to pre-k where they had terrible junky snacks and parties almost every day. I can't believe it, but parents would bake full size cupcakes and adorn them with icing and candies and give it to the kids with juice boxes. Then wonder why they were bouncing off the walls a few minutes later. I caved to peer pressure though...I let him eat these things thinking a few times per week wouldn't really hurt. Boy, did it ever! He would beg and cry for cereal in the mornings, because "everyone else eats it!" Well, we are homeschooling now and have almost broken the bad habits. Still a work in progress.

[http://www.dailymail.co.uk/health/article-2194432/Touch-frost-banish-arthritis-pain.html?ito=feeds-newsxml](http://http://www.dailymail.co.uk/health/article-2194432/Touch-frost-banish-arthritis-pain.html?ito=feeds-newsxml)

# Thread: from newbie serotonin99--history and questions
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-newbie-serotonin99-history-and-questions.3322/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### serotonin99 — 2012-08-28T23:46:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-newbie-serotonin99-history-and-questions.3322/post-47923>

Hello everybody,

I found this site via serendipity. Here's my story:

Many years ago I was diagnosed with CFS and Fibromyalgia and because I could not sleep I was given paxil and klonopin. The paxil started acting up over three years ago so I stopped it cold turkey as I had no idea how addictive it is. It has left me with gut pain (nausea/cramping), fear, terror, weight loss (I have a hard time keeping my weight up), PTSD and OCD.

I am sensitive to sunlight, and I have osteoperosis and I have some tooth decay. It's hard to eat as my gut hurts most of the time, but I do eat--I am not anorexic but I look that way.

I have done some research and I think I have too much serotonin in my brain and gut. Clues: sunlight exacerbates my symptoms; gardening does too. Eating chocolate is problematic.

My post paxil world feels like things are moving way too quickly and I'm pretty anxious all the time. I never was this depressed, but I am now.

I also think that I have been poisoned by fluoride as that is in paxil.

Help....this is no way to live. I want to gain weight, not lose, feel much calmer than I am now, and live a semblance of a life. Thanks for reading.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-08-29T00:49:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-newbie-serotonin99-history-and-questions.3322/post-47942>

welcome.............

### nonchalant — 2012-08-29T01:23:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-newbie-serotonin99-history-and-questions.3322/post-47954>

Hi serotonin99. Do you take tyrosine to raise dopamine levels? You might consider the urine testing for neurotransmitter levels.

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-29T01:28:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-newbie-serotonin99-history-and-questions.3322/post-47959>

Hi There

If you were taking an SSRI I doubt you have too much serotonin. It is much more likely that you got depleted. That is why you feel like crap when you get off them. Read a bit more about how antidepressant medicines work and what they do to your brain.

[http://www.drkaslow.com/html/neurotransmitter\_depletion.html](http://http://www.drkaslow.com/html/neurotransmitter_depletion.html)

You can restore your neurotransmitter levels by eating epi-paleo rx and some of the other recommendations in the blog. Or you might need more help - look into Dr Kalish, he does organic cation transporter testing (neurotransmitter level testing) and will be able to help you get your NTs back in balance, most likely. I'm working with him

[http://www.kalishresearch.com/](http://http://www.kalishresearch.com/)

Sorry about the anorexic look. People can be a\*\*holes. I looked like that forever and I hated people staring at me. But I actually was anorexic

### caroline — 2012-08-29T03:10:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-newbie-serotonin99-history-and-questions.3322/post-48000>

Welcome -make sure you read all of Dr. K's blogs - If you are like me you will have to re read many times but there is so much there and when you have questions - so many really smart people on this forum to ask. Good luck! and you have come to the right place now you just have to dig in and learn

# Thread: Hernando Dâ€™Escalante Fontanedaâ€™s Memoir
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hernando-d%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2escalante-fontaneda%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-memoir.3325/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michael — 2012-08-29T09:31:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hernando-d%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2escalante-fontaneda%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-memoir.3325/post-48033>

Interesting as providing a snapshot of people living in historical time in a coastal zone and exploiting marine and land-based resources.

This would be what's now Florida. They sound pretty healthy:

> Indians are on these islands, who are of a large size: the women are well proportioned, and have good countenances. ... They go naked, except only some breech-cloths ... archers and men of strength.
>
>
> Thwir food includes:
>
>
>
> The common food is fish, turtle, and snails (all of which are alike fish), and tunny and whale ...

More interesting stuff:

[http://www.keyshistory.org/Fontenada.html](http://http://www.keyshistory.org/Fontenada.html)

### Zorica Vuletic — 2012-08-29T19:08:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hernando-d%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2escalante-fontaneda%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-memoir.3325/post-48109>

I'm coming to the conclusion that when looking at traditional peoples, it's more beneficial to look at the women...and if they are in good shape. See only looking at men can be dangerous, b/c the men sometimes might do special rights and training therefore they look strong...then you see the women fat as ever. No thanks.

So the Bajau people I noticed that the women are quite a nice shape with good composition, yet not looking TOO lean or 'ripped'. They are not fat either though...but seem to have JUST the right amount of subcutaneous fat for healthy fertility, yet their body composition is impeccable and they are also strong.

...and the skin quality is another thing to look at.

Too bad these Florida Indians got wiped out...:-(

### Michael — 2012-08-29T20:20:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hernando-d%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2escalante-fontaneda%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-memoir.3325/post-48136>

> **Gladina;48560 wrote:** ...it's more beneficial to look at the women...

Yeah, I'd prefer that.

Sorry, I couldn't resist the joke.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-08-29T20:28:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hernando-d%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2escalante-fontaneda%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-memoir.3325/post-48140>

Astute observation Gladina. I happen to agree totally.

### JoeBranca — 2012-08-29T21:51:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hernando-d%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2escalante-fontaneda%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-memoir.3325/post-48163>

This reminds me to go back and re-read some historical accounts again. Sven Hedin (Swedish explorer) has memoirs from his explorations around the turn of the 20th century, into Persia and up through the Himalayas into Mongolia. His accounts of locals he encoutered were somewhat detailed, iirc.

Also, Thomas Hariot (Elizabethan astronomer/mathematician) wrote an account of his New World travels including a wintering over with the Powhatans at Roanoke (prior to Jamestown disaster), who lived a stone age life without nary a stone in the region, and prided themselves on minimal clothing even in winter. Not sure what he wrote about diet, but he did end up bringing the potato back to Britain : /

### Michael — 2012-08-29T22:05:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hernando-d%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2escalante-fontaneda%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-memoir.3325/post-48169>

If he's the man I think he is, he had views on just about everything and certainly wrote about diet, although I don't think any of it would stand modern scrutiny. In fact, I think one of his health prescriptions was smoking. I had an idea that the man in question has been said to be perhaps the first European victim of tobacco. I just consulted wikipedia - what's the verb for that? I just wikkied him, perhaps? - and it said Hariot died of an ulcerous cancer on his lip. So maybe.

If you get anything good that's online would love a link.

# Thread: Hi from South Island, New Zealand
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-south-island-new-zealand.3326/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 23
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-08-29T10:18:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-south-island-new-zealand.3326/post-48035>

Hello everyone,

I am a 42 year old mother of 4 children aged between 16 and 8 years old. I've been very overweight for a very long time and have been on lots of different diets which work for a while and then I just can't seem to stick to them. I came across an interveiw with Dr Jack about 4 weeks ago by 'accident', and I have been reading and listening to as much as I can since then. I started the paleo diet on Monday 27th Aug (2 days ago), and started the face dunking as well. I have been working very hard gardening in the last two days and usually by the second night I am KNACKERED!!! But tonight I have good energy, my knees would normally be hurting like hell, but they are fine. I'm so excited about this - the cold thermogensis seems like the 'missing link' that I have been searching for all these years. Time will tell I guess, but it just makes complete sense to me.

We live in the foothills of a mountain and are 420 metres above sea level with amazing views over the valley on a 100 acre property where we are endeavouring to be as self sufficient as possible - grow our own veges, and raise our own meat, etc. The paleo way of eating is therefore perfect! I just found myself an old cast iron bath that I bought for $40 and I'm going to put it next to our water tank so that I can fill it straight from there (and it will double as a water trough when I need it for that). We are just heading into spring here, and the days are getting longer, so I will be in the cold bath looking at the view in the evenings! Sounds like bliss - not so?!

I feel like a shift has happened inside of me - that I am starting a new way of living, so that I can live the rest of my life with no inflammation and use my body to its fullest capacity. I'm very excited!! Thank you Dr Kruse!!

### freesia — 2012-08-29T10:45:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-south-island-new-zealand.3326/post-48036>

Hi Amber. I envy your cast iron bath with a view set-up!

I'm from the North island and I've been doing the Leptin Reset and Cold Thermogenesis since early April. Seafood heavy diet since July.

I don't have excess weight but I did actually lose a few pounds overall and gain some muscle. Inflammation seems to have improved, fitness definitely has...out of sight (!) and adrenal fatigue too.

I look forward to reading about your progress!

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-31T00:20:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-south-island-new-zealand.3326/post-48449>

> **KiwiLauren;48917 wrote:** Hurray! Finally another Kiwi! I have been on board with Dr K (epi paleo, CT, circadian cycles, etc etc etc) since the beginning of 2012 and my life has taken fantastic turns as a result. I am in Christchurch. Where on the South Island are you? (It is hard to get the seafood when you're rural?) I am spinning my wheels trying to find local docs who can support me as I get the testing, BHRT, etc that I need... NZ is bliss but it's also in the dark ages when it comes to what the Americans have available in this realm! So glad to have you on board!! Lauren

Hi Lauren! I am in the Waitaki Valley in North Otago. Yes this is all such new stuff - no one has heard about it around here, and I'm still so new to it that I don't even know what epi-paleo is! We have a fantastic doctor here who is of American decent, and she is amazing at researching new stuff. If you know exactly what tests you need to have done, then I could ask her if she is able to do them. We are about 3 hours drive south of Chch. I go to Oamaru once a week to get groceries, seafood is rather expensive - can you stick to meats and have seafood occasionally?

### KiwiLauren — 2012-08-31T04:33:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-south-island-new-zealand.3326/post-48505>

Thanks for the potential heads-up about working with your doc. I may come back to you on that. I need someone to support me with BHRT and this is proving hard to find. Dr K is so good that I currently know more than the doctors I'm talking to!

Oamaru is my fantasy CT place! Have you CT'd there? I will some day.

There is a lot to learn when you come on board. Dr K began discussing the epi-paleo diet a while back... basically to be optimal we must eat a seafood-heavy diet. There is an entire webinar about it. Seafood at least 5 times per week, with shellfish being number one and fish number two. And raw better than cooked. I have been totally capitalising on roe season... $6.95/kg and I eat it raw every day! There is a lot of info out here. The other big thing, if you haven't got there yet is the light cycles. Light is more important than diet. So no artificial light and definitely none after dark. We just made it through an entire winter with nothing but candlelight and no screens of any kind after sunset. It's been fantastic actually.

Keep in touch! (And feel free to PM me if that's easier.)

Lauren

### KiwiLauren — 2012-08-31T04:34:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-south-island-new-zealand.3326/post-48506>

PS I should add that some of us are eating seafood 7-14x week and others are doing much less. The 5 times a week was just a general number, not something Dr K has prescribed.

### FreeRangeKiwi — 2012-09-01T03:25:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-south-island-new-zealand.3326/post-48680>

> **KiwiLauren;48917 wrote:** Hurray! Finally another Kiwi! I have been on board with Dr K (epi paleo, CT, circadian cycles, etc etc etc) since the beginning of 2012 and my life has taken fantastic turns as a result. I am in Christchurch. Where on the South Island are you? (It is hard to get the seafood when you're rural?) I am spinning my wheels trying to find local docs who can support me as I get the testing, BHRT, etc that I need... NZ is bliss but it's also in the dark ages when it comes to what the Americans have available in this realm! So glad to have you on board!! Lauren

Hey guys, thought I'd let you know you're not the only Kiwis! I've been addicted to Jack's blog since June 2011. If you want to join Primal/Paleo Dunedin or the Dunedin Polar Bears Club on fb here are the links [https://www.facebook.com/groups/188990581234244/](http://https://www.facebook.com/groups/188990581234244/) and [https://www.facebook.com/DunedinPolarBears](http://https://www.facebook.com/DunedinPolarBears). The water in Dunedin harbour has been down to 5.2 degrees this year and I'm trying to get down there daily although sometimes I have to resort to the bath at home. I have gone from 124kg to 88kg and am always warm. Last time I was this weight I was 16 and could run 100m in around 15seconds. I swam competitively too. I am now 33, can run 100m in around 12seconds and can swim faster than when I trained and only need to breathe every 14 strokes (once per short-course length). I don't not do any training other than lifting weights once or twice a week or whenever I can be bothered.

I have got a friend doing CT and he's an avid reader too. We are both in the Physical Education department at Otago Uni. I'm doing postgrad training as an exercise physiologist (looking at hyponatraemia) - might be setting up a CT study - will keep you posted. My friend is one of the neurophysiology lab techs in the Physed Dept. There is heaps of research going on in the department on extreme environments, but it is all to do with performance. I hope to change that as I'm into clinical exercise and metabolism.

Good to hear there are others... I heard from someone in Queenstown when they commented on Jack's FB page. Sing out if you're a silent observer!

### freesia — 2012-09-01T04:43:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-south-island-new-zealand.3326/post-48681>

Hi FRK

I LOVE that you might be setting up a CT study. (Otago does great work!)

I have ME/CFS...have had it all my adult life so my response to CT likely isn't exactly typical, but in my own way I have made fantastic gains.

Thanks for introducing yourself here....great to know others are discovering how well we can do when we embrace Winter conditions as we're designed to!

Hi FreeRangeKiwi, wow so good to know there is someone close to us here. You have done so well to lose 40kgs! How long did that take you? I'm very excited about this whole paleo/ct regime - its so different and yet makes so much sense. I got an old cast iron bath today and have put it by our water tank, will be cleaning it out tomorrow and giving it a try. I am only 6 days into my face dunking, but I can't wait. I've lost 3 kilos in 5 days, so feeling good about that. Just hope I can keep it up. Do you follow the circadian rythyms like KiwiLauren does? I'm interested to learn more about it.

Great to know you're out there! Funny that we all seem to be situated on the south island. I did some ocean CT over winter... i wish I had a club (you should have seen the looks of those walking along the beach all rugged up and me in the water all by myself in my togs (with my hat on of course). I'm guessing you get those looks regularly. I no longer complain about the typical Kiwi uninsulated house in winter. It's built in daily CT for me. Like Amber, I'd be keen to know if you're following circadian cycles and epi-paleo diet, too. If your peers think you're weird swimming in the ocean, just wait til you have to stay home past 5pm with the lights off! How many do you have in your Polar Bear club?? (Btw, I tried to reply yes to your friend request on this forum but am not sure I did it correctly).

### [email redacted] — 2012-09-03T13:47:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-south-island-new-zealand.3326/post-49161>

I spoke to KiwiLauren on the phone last night - thank you for answering so many questions for me Lauren... I had lots and still have many more. I have been doing the paleo diet for one week today, plus face dunking and I have lost 3.8 kg! (8.3 pounds) I'm stoked about that!

I wanted to share a little story that I told Lauren last night about ct. I grew up on a farm here in the south island of New Zealand, and as a child we did A LOT of swimming in the rivers, and even now in the summer I always take my kids to the river to swim rather than the local swimming pool, the rivers are colder but much more fun and we love being in nature. About 20 years ago I backpacked around the world by myself starting my journey in the States and traveling up the west coast and into Canada. I hitched a lift with a couple of Australians and we were camping in the Rockies. One morning after not having showered for 3 days I decided I was going to wash my hair in a river (somewhere in the Rockies in Alberta) and this river was glacial fed, so it was FREEZING. I must have been in the water for 10 minutes or so and it really was so cold it took my breath away. But after I got out and dried myself by skin was tingling all over and I felt so warm and ALIVE!! I often tell people that it was the best I have ever felt in my life! On another occasion (about 2 years ago), my husband and I slept the night out in the hills behind where we live, and in the morning I jumped into a stream that was also freezing, but only up to my waist as it was just too cold. I then got out and drip dried in the sun, and my skin started tingling from the waist down, and that was where I was warm - I was cold from the waist up! So I jumped back into the stream and went under this time as I knew I would be warmer all over and feeling alive afterwards.

So now that I am learning about cold thermogenesis for the first time, I can call on my own experiences to back up the benefits that ct does for us!

### caroline — 2012-09-03T23:45:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-south-island-new-zealand.3326/post-49245>

What a great story - thanks for sharing

# Thread: Breast lump
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breast-lump.3330/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mery — 2012-08-29T15:20:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breast-lump.3330/post-48069>

Iâ€™d like to share my experience with a lump I developed on my right breast.

Iâ€™ve been putting ice on my breast and tummy as it was the only way to get some CT. I did this on and off, as it was not easy to begin with and then in the middle of July I started applying the ice daily. At the start, the longest I could manage was 15 minutes but I slowly increased the time, now I can last 35 to 40 minutes.

On July 27, I noticed a lump on my right breast on the lower part on the left side. The diameter was 2 inch by 1 inch, I asked my husband to check for me. This is when I started having pain on the lymphatic area under my armpit every time I lifted my arm and after a few days the pain went.

At this time I was having problem with my ears as I could not hear and I was feeling a bit down so I went to the doctor to have my ear checked. I mentioned to the doctor that my sister has a lump and the doctor said that she should have it checked. I said that she didnâ€™t like mammograms and the doctor said they donâ€™t do that anymore that instead they do a scan. I didnâ€™t say it was me because I knew they would send me letters.

After my ears were cleaned, I felt more positive, throughout all this stressful time I was putting ice on my breast and tummy and I noticed that the lump was getting smaller and on Friday I checked it and it was gone.

Iâ€™m so happy and being calm, not telling the GP has paid off. I still got my 2 beautiful ladies and now they are firm and can pass the pencil test.

Like Dr Jack said no toxin will survive CT, thanks Dr Jack

I started with lumpy breasts in my 30's. Many years later, after many painful cysts and cyst aspirations, I started supplementing with Vitex and progesterone cream. All of a sudden, my fibrocystic breasts were a thing of the past, like they never existed.

### Mackay — 2012-08-30T14:18:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breast-lump.3330/post-48331>

Iodine has also been shown to help a lot with breast and uterine cysts.

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-31T00:41:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breast-lump.3330/post-48455>

> **Destiny;48782 wrote:** I started with lumpy breasts in my 30's. Many years later, after many painful cysts and cyst aspirations, I started supplementing with Vitex and progesterone cream. All of a sudden, my fibrocystic breasts were a thing of the past, like they never existed.

I'm starting to think that FBD isn't \*just\* caused by iodine deficiency but also by messed up hormones.

### colleencoble — 2012-08-31T01:08:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breast-lump.3330/post-48459>

It's messed up hormones. I had it too, and even had a lump removed at 18. since I'm on progesterone, those lumps are a thing of the past.

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-31T01:16:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breast-lump.3330/post-48463>

My FBD got tons worse on high-dose iodine. This month it was much better (though still pretty bad) and I started on P last month. And dropped the iodine.

Just another thought to add to this. If it were me, I'd want an hsCRP and a Pg:E ratio if I had a breast lump. Basically, I'd want to know my inflammation levels and how my hormones were stacking up (and maybe a vitamin D level too since it's clearly correlated with breast cancer). I'm not saying that CT didn't work, only that there are other ways to test your overall health and risk of something nasty developing.

# Thread: How does Hashi's destroy your hormones?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-hashis-destroy-your-hormones.3332/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bigknitwit — 2012-08-29T17:34:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-hashis-destroy-your-hormones.3332/post-48088>

This was mentioned to me in my consult. Hashimoto's destroys thyroid hormones, then comes insulin resistance, food intolerances, poor Pg/E2 ratios.

First goes the thyroid hormones, and oral replacement is needed. What is the mechanism that makes the other hormones fall, and how do we lose hormone control at the brain level? Is this all thanks to high levels of inflammation? How does the insulin resistance come about? I am trying to figure out the full cascade of events to get it all clear in my head (going to the doctor tomorrow).

### MartiD — 2012-08-29T17:38:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-hashis-destroy-your-hormones.3332/post-48089>

I'd be interested in this as well as I just was diagnosed with hashi's and have an appt. in two weeks. I am hoping this new Dr. will be proactive instead of just wanting to wait around for the antibodies to destroy the thyroid and then treat with replacement.

### nonchalant — 2012-08-29T17:55:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-hashis-destroy-your-hormones.3332/post-48093>

Hi knit. I bet Dr. K mentioned that you should read the Hormone 101 blog. Your body converts the cholesterol in your LDL, using T3 from the thyroid and vitamin A, into the precursor hormone, pregnenolone. Then pregnenolone becomes cortisol, or it becomes DHEA, the various sex hormones, and vitamin D.

Hashi's attacks the thyroid, which lowers the T3 needed early in this process. So all those hormones suffer.

Inflammation comes into the picture by increasing stress. If the body is stressed, cortisol rises. So what little pregnenolone the body makes, is all used up by the more important function of keeping cortisol high to deal with stress. And the other hormones crash.

That was all from memory, Dr. K. How did I do?
